name: Liquid Courage's Championship Belt
desc:
This belt grants Advantage on all Performance (Charisma) checks related to sicknasty combat tricks.

A championship belt from the Liquid Courage's Opening Night at the Molotov. Says World's Greatest Dad on it. Did she steal this from somewhere or what?

name: Absorbing Paint
desc:
This paint can be applied to a shield's facing side and takes 10 minutes to dry fully, after which it is permanently bonded with the shield. In addition to any existing effects, the shield now holds 4 charges. You can expend one charge to cast Absorb Elements at first level on yourself.

 Spending more than one charge will increase the spell's level by one for each additional charge. The shield regains 1d4 charges at dawn.

name: Medic Armor
desc:
While wearing this armour, when your HP is reduced to 10% or less of your maximum HP, you regain hit points up to half your maximum health. In addition, when you drop to 0 hit points but are not killed outright, you instead have 1 hit point. Both properties reactivate at dawn. 

This suit of full plate was an attempt to design armour that would heal its wearer and keep them up in battle.

name: Book of the Theurge
meta:
*Requires attunement, very rare.*

This item functions as a wizard's spellbook except you may copy cleric spells of first level or higher from scrolls into the book for double the price. These spells function as wizard spells for you and you may only prepare one cleric spell for every four levels in wizard that you have (rounded down). These spells count toward your total number of prepared spells.

name: Mirror of the Past
desc: The holder of this platinum hand mirror can learn something about the history of a specific object or creature by taking an action to gaze into the mirror and think of the target. Instead of the holder's reflection, the mirror presents scenes from the target's past. Information Information conveyed is accurate, but it is random and cryptic, and presented in no particular order. Once it is activated the mirror gives its information for one minute or less, then returns to normal. it can't be used again until the next dawn.

name: Dark Shard Amulet
desc:
This amulet is fashioned from a single shard of resilient extraplanar material originating from the realm of your warlock patron. While you are wearing it, you gain the follow benefits:

You can use the amulet as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells.
You can try to cast a cantrip that you don't know. The cantrip must be on the warlock spell list, and you must make a DC 10 Intelligence (Arcana) check. If this check succeeds, you can cast the spell. If the check fails, so does the spell, and the action used to cast the spell is wasted. In either case, you can't use this property again until you finish a long rest.

name: Mask of the Beast
desc: This wooden mask is shaped in the likeness of a beast’s visage and has 3 charges. While wearing the mask, you can expend 1 charge and use the mask to cast the *animal friendship* spell as an action. The mask regains all expended charges at dawn.

name: Armor of the Mark
meta:
*Heavy Armour, Legendary, requires attunement.*
AC: 17
Stealth: Disadvantage
Weight: 60 lbs
desc:
This oft-reforged and patched suit of armour bears marks, inscriptions and doodles from its many wearers, as well as a raven painted across the front plates.

You gain a +1 bonus to AC while you wear this armor.

As a Bonus Action, you can call out for aid from your fellow guild members. A spectral image of a guildie you choose appears within 15ft of you and acts on its own initiative, using the guild member's stats and features at the wearer's level (or lower, if the guildie has not reached the same level yet).

The guildie will help the wearer and their allies to their best efforts and can use any action the actual guild member could, including casting spells. They disappear after one minute or after reaching 0HP.

Once this property is used, it cannot be used until 7 in-game days pass.

name: Quiver of Ehlonna's Champion
desc:
Each of the quiver's three compartments connects to an extradimensional space that allows the quiver to hold numerous items while never weighing more than 2 pounds. The shortest compartment can hold up to sixty arrows, bolts, or similar objects. The midsize compartment holds up to eighteen javelins or similar objects. The longest compartment holds up to six long objects, such as bows, quarterstaffs, or spears.

You can draw any item the quiver contains as if doing so from a regular quiver or scabbard.

All arrows stored within the quiver become +1 arrows for 1 minute after they are removed from the quiver.

name: Chromatic Amulet
meta: *Wondrous item, uncommon (requires attunement by a spellcaster that uses the Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard spell lists)*
desc:
While attuned to this amulet, you can cast the *Chromatic Burst* cantrip.

Chromatic Burst
Evocation Cantrip
Casting Time: 1 action
Range: 90 feet
Components: V,S
Duration: Instant
You launch a burst of chromatic energy at an enemy you can
see within range. Choose cold, fire, lightning, or poison and
make a ranged spell attack against the target. If the attack
hits, the creature takes 1d10 damage of the chosen type.
(2d10 at 5th level, 3d10 at 11th, 4d10 at 17th).

name: Weather Orb - Blizzard
desc:
Smashing this orb on the ground causes a powerful blizzard
to form over 10 minutes in a 1 mile radius area. This storm
will persist for 2d6 hours, creates 3 inches of snow per hour,
and drops the temperature to dangerously freezing levels. For
each hour a creature is in this area and not wearing proper
clothing, it must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving
throw or gain a level of exhaustion. While the storm persists,
vision in this area is heavily obscured. Once eight inches of
snow has fallen, the area becomes difficult terrain. Once the
storm has ended, the weather returns to normal over 10
minutes (though the snow persists until it melts naturally).

name: Weather Orb - Snow
desc:
Smashing this orb on the ground causes a snowstorm to
form over the next 10 minutes in a 1 mile radius area. This
storm will persist for 2d6 hours, creates 1 inches of snow per
hour, and drops the temperature considerably. While the
storm persists, vision in this area is lightly obscured. Once
eight inches of snow has fallen, the area becomes difficult
terrain. Using this item while a natural snow storm is already
in effect instead doubles the duration of the snow storm as
well as the amount of snow that falls per hour. Once the
storm has ended, the weather returns to normal over 10
minutes (though the snow persists until it melts naturally).

name: Gem of Pure Potential
desc: These gems are sometimes to store spells or energy. They can be traded to a very experienced crafter to be transferred into a spell scroll. Your DM will determine the rarity and spell level of the gem quality you were able to find.

name: The Scepter of Pure Ice
desc:
While attuned to this item, when you are dealt cold damage, reduce the total damage taken by 5. Additionally, you cannot gain exhaustion as the result of bitter cold weather. 

Once per day you can use an action to create a bridge of ice. This bridge is up to 30 feet long and 5 feet wide and must be between and two points you can see within 120 feet. This bridge turns to snow after 10 minutes.

The scepter has 7 charges. It regains 1d6 + 3 expended charges daily at dawn. 

You can use an action to expend 1 or more of the scepter’s charges to cast one of the following spells from it: ice knife (1 charge), Snilloc’s snowball swarm
(2 charges), sleet storm (5 charges). If the scepter is reduced to 0 charges, roll a d20. On a 1, the scepter shatters into ice shards and is destroyed.

Spells cast using the scepter have a +7 bonus to hit and a DC 15 saving throw. In areas within 1 mile of natural snow or ice, this increases to a +9 bonus to hit and a DC 17 saving throw.

The scepter is currently bound with the Essence of Pure Ice, granting the user passage into the Plane of Pure Ice with a Plane Shift spell. Avis is the protector of the Essence and she can be summoned and dismissed to be talked to, though she will only protect the Essence and cannot be used in battle otherwise.

If the scepter is destroyed, the Essence of Pure Ice is threatened and Avis will become hostile to all creatures to protect the Essence and transport it safely to the Plane of Pure Ice.

name: Staff of Plant Growth
desc:
The Staff of Plant Growth is a 6-foot pole of mossy wood with vines wrapped all around it, ending in a small sapphire. 

This staff can cast the Plant Growth spell on command. The staff has 3 charges, and recharges one every day. If the last charge is used, roll a d20. If a 1 is rolled, the staff loses one of its spell slots permanently. A staff that has lost all its spell slots loses all magic and becomes a regular staff.

name: Pipes of the Swamp
desc:
Wondrous Item, uncommon
**Attunement**
Requires Attunement
**Description**
While you are attuned to the pipes, ordinary frogs and giant frogs are indifferent toward you and will not attack you unless you threaten or harm them.

The pipes have 3 charges. If you play the pipes as an action, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 to 3 charges, calling forth one swarm of frogs (reskinned Swarm of Rats) with each expended charge, provided that enough rats are within half a mile of you to be called in this fashion (as determined by the GM). If there aren't enough rats to form a swarm, the charge is wasted. Called swarms move toward the music by the shortest available route but aren't under your control otherwise. The pipes regain 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

Whenever a swarm of frogs that isn't under another creature's control comes within 30 feet of you while you are playing the pipes, you can make a Charisma check contested by the swarm's Wisdom check. If you lose the contest, the swarm behaves as it normally would and can't be swayed by the pipes' music for the next 24 hours. If you win the contest, the swarm is swayed by the pipes' music and becomes friendly to you and your companions for as long as you continue to play the pipes each round as an action. A friendly swarm obeys your commands. If you issue no commands to a friendly swarm, it defends itself but otherwise takes no actions. If a friendly swarm starts its turn and can't hear the pipes' music, your control over that swarm ends, and the swarm behaves as it normally would and can't be swayed by the pipes' music for the next 24 hours.

name: Inferno Gauntlets
desc: While wearing these beautiful scaled gauntlets, your unarmed attacks deal 1 bonus fire damage and you gain a +1 to your unarmed melee attack rolls. You may use your bonus action to ignite these gauntlets for one minute, causing them to deal an additional 1d4 bonus fire damage on every attack instead. Once you have used this feature, you can’t use it again until the next dawn. You may also cast the burning hands spell at first level with a save DC of 13 once per day.

name: Bottle of Whispers
desc:
When the cork of this bottle is removed, it will capture whispers of any conversation within 30 feet. As it captures these conversations, colorful smoke begins to swirl through the bottle. The colors are vibrant and diverse, each unique to an individual conversation. Once 5 minutes of conversation have been captured, the bottle is completely filled and can hold no more whispers until its contents are emptied.

When the bottle contains smoke, it can be tipped on its side to pour out conversations one at a time. Each of these conversations are written on a small piece of parchment wrapped up and bound by a colorful string that matches the
color of smoke that was poured out of the bottle. There is no indication of who spoke the words written on the page and the participants are simply listed as Speaker 1, Speaker 2, etc…

Once the contents of the bottle have been poured out, it requires twenty-four hours to recharge before it can absorb additional conversations.

name: Scorch
desc:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. You also gain the following benefits:

* Once per day, you can cast *fireball* with a save DC of 15. 
* You can use your bonus action to speak the name of this weapon and cause lava to flow through rifts in the blade. For the next minute, attacks with this weapon deal an extra 1d6 fire damage. Once you have used this feature, you can’t use it again until the next dawn.


**Paired Set.** If you are also attuned to Scorch, the following additional benefits are granted:

Once per day as a bonus action, you can transform Scorch and Shock into a +2 double-bladed polearm for one minute. This weapon has the two-handed, reach, and finesse properties and deals 1d10 slashing damage. If you are proficient with short swords, you are also proficient with this weapon. Each time you attack with this weapon, you must choose if you would like to make the attack with the Scorch or Shock end of the polearm. If you choose Scorch, your attack deal an extra 2d6 fire damage and ignore fire resistance. If you choose Shock, your attack deal an extra 2d6 lightning damage and any creature hit can’t take reactions until the end of its next turn.

name: Amulet of Pure Flame
desc:
While wearing this beautiful ruby amulet you gain the following benefits:

* When you are dealt fire damage, reduce the total damage taken by 5.
* You gain the *control flames* and *produce flame* cantrips.

This amulet has the following additional properties. When one of these properties is used, it can’t be used again until the next dawn.

***Burning Passion.*** You touch a creature and fill it with a fiery passion for battle. For the next minute, that creature gains a +1 bonus to all saving throws. While this effect is active, the inspired creature has ethereal flames cast off their body and produces bright light in a 10-foot radius area and dim light for an additional 10 feet.

***Illuminate.*** You cast the *daylight* spell. The light created by this spell casts everything in a shade of red.

***Incinerate.*** You pull a bead of pure flame from the amulet and launch it at a target within 60 feet. If the target is a creature, make a ranged spell attack against it with a +7 bonus to the attack roll. On a hit, the target takes 8d6 fire damage as the bead swells to a three foot orb and bathes the target in lava. A creature killed by this effect is turned to ash and can only be resurrected via a Wish. 

The Amulet is currently bound with the Essence of Pure Fire, granting the user passage into the Plane of Fire with a Plane Shift spell. Ashmaw is the protector of the Essence and it can be summoned and dismissed to be talked to, though it will only protect the Essence and cannot be used in battle otherwise.

If the Amulet is destroyed, the Essence of Pure Fire is threatened and Ashmaw will become hostile to all creatures to protect the Essence and transport it safely to the Plane of Fire.

name: Coin of Luck
desc: When you make a skill check, attack roll, or saving throw, you may instead roll a 1d2. If the result is a 2, the result is equivalent of a natural 20. If the result is 1,the result is equivalent to natural 1. The coin has limited uses and does not recharge.

name: Mug of Comfort
desc:
This mug has two coloured halves with a small circular sign on each half. One red with a flame and one blue with a snowflake.

When the appropriate sign is touched, liquid within the mug is kept at a comfortable cool or warm temperature.

name: Crown of Storm Herald
desc:
While wearing this crown, you have resistance to lightning damage. As a bonus action you can summon a 3 foot wide nimbus cloud that you can ride for up to 8 hours a day. This cloud has a flight speed of 30 feet and can only be ridden by you. If you summon this cloud while there is a storm active, this cloud grows to 15 feet wide, has a flight speed of 60 feet, and can be ridden by up to 8 medium creatures. While riding this cloud, you will never be struck by lightning.

This crown has 7 charges. It regains 1d6 + 3 expended charges daily at dawn. You can use an action to expend 1 or more of the crown’s charges to use the following abilities:

**One with Lightning (1 charge).** You teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. If you are outdoors, upon arrival, a bolt of lightning streaks out of the sky and strikes you. Each creature within 5 feet must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d10 lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

**Jolting Blast (3 charges).** You send a powerful burst of lighting at an enemy within 60 feet. Make an attack roll against that creature with a +7 bonus to hit. On a hit, that creature takes 5d10 lightning damage and must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be stunned until the end of its next turn. If the creature fails this saving throw by 10 or more, it is paralyzed instead.

**Barrage of Lightning (7 charges).** Lightning strikes the ground at four locations you can see within 120 feet that are at least 20 feet apart from one another. Each creature within a 10 feet of any of these points must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take 5d10 lightning damage. This damages objects in the area and ignites flammable objects that aren’t being worn or carried. This ability can only be used while outdoors. If there are stormy conditions when you use this ability, the damage of each bolt is increased to 8d10.

name: Weather Orb - Lightning Storm
desc: Smashing this orb on the ground causes a vicious lightning storm to form over 10 minutes in a 1 mile radius area. This storm will persist for 2d6 hours, produces half an inch of water per hour, and causes the area to become heavily obscured. Creatures outdoors during this storm are constantly at threat of being struck by lightning. For every ten minutes a creature is outdoors during the storm, roll a d10. On a result of 1, a bolt of lightning strikes at that creature’s location. That creature must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take 10d10 lightning damage. Once the storm has ended, the weather returns to normal over 10 minutes.

name: Amulet of the Storm Caller
desc:
While attuned to this amulet, you deal an extra 1d6 lightning damage with all weapon and spell attacks and your attacks ignore lightning resistance. Once per day you can use your action to cause a lightning storm to form over the next 10 minutes in a 1 mile radius area. This storm will persist for 1 hour and blocks all natural sunlight. Using this item while a natural storm is already in effect instead doubles the duration of the storm. While this storm persists, your bonus lightning damage is increased to 1d10. Once the storm has ended, the weather returns to normal over 10 minutes.

**Paired Set.** ?

name: Boots of the Electromancer
desc: These boots hold 5 charges. Whenever you expend all of your movement in a single turn, they regain an expended charge. If you used the Dash action this turn, you can expend any number of these charges as a bonus action to unleash a bolt of lightning at a creature within 30 feet. That creature must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 lightning damage per charge expended.

name: Shock
desc:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. You also gain the following benefits:
* Once per day, you can cast lightning bolt with a save DC of 15.
* You can use your bonus action to speak the name of this weapon and cause lightning to spark from the end of the blade on each attack. For the next minute, attacks with this weapon deal an extra 1d6 lightning damage. Once you have used this feature, you can’t use it again until the next dawn.

**Paired Set.** If you are also attuned to Scorch, the following additional benefits are granted:

Once per day as a bonus action, you can transform Scorch and Shock into a +2 double-bladed polearm for one minute. This weapon has the two-handed, reach, and finesse properties and deals 1d10 slashing damage. If you are proficient with short swords, you are also proficient with this weapon. Each time you attack with this weapon, you must choose if you would like to make the attack with the Scorch or Shock end of the polearm. If you choose Scorch, your attack deal an extra 2d6 fire damage and ignore fire resistance. If you choose Shock, your attack deal an extra 2d6 lightning damage and any creature hit can’t take reactions until the end of its next turn.

name: Ring of the Hydromancer
desc:
While wearing this ring, you gain a swimming speed of 30 feet and can breathe underwater. Once per day you can use your action to bind two targets - creature or object - together with a stream of ice cold water. If either target tries to move more than 20 feet away from its bound partner, it must expend 2 feet of movement for every 1 foot traveled and it both targets take 1d8 cold damage for every 5 feet it travels. This effect persists for 1 minute or until the targets move at least 50 feet apart.

This ring has 9 charges. It regains 1d6 + 3 expended charges daily at dawn. You can use an action to expend 1 or more of the ring’s charges to cast one of the following spells
from it: shape water (1 charge), create or destroy water (2 charges), water breathing (3 charges), tidal wave (4 charges).

Spells cast using the ring have a DC 15 saving throw. While it is actively raining or you are completely submerged in water, the saving throw increases to 17.

This item is bound to Lysidia the Hydromancer, Guardian of the Essence of Pure Water. While submerged, you may speak with Lysidia if she is on the same plane as you or the Plane of Water.

name: Cloak of Everflowing Water
desc:
While wearing this cloak that is indistinguishable from water, you can use an action to create a simple melee weapon out of water that lasts for one hour. You are considered proficient with this weapon and attacks with this weapon deal 1 bonus cold damage on hit. Additionally, once per day, you may use your reaction to gain immunity to fire damage until the end of your next turn.

You also gain access the Water Blast cantrip while wearing this cloak.
> **Water Blast**
> *Evocation Cantrip*
> Casting Time: 1 action
> Range: 20 feet
> Components: V,S
> Duration: Instant
> You launch a blast of freezing cold water at an enemy you can
> see within range. Make a ranged spell attack against that
> creature. On a hit, the creature takes 1d12 cold damage.
> (2d12 at 5th level, 3d12 at 11th, 4d12 at 17th).

name: Aquan Quiver
desc: At dawn each day, ten of the arrows in this quiver become enchanted with aquatic power. These arrows deal cold damage rather than piercing and deal 1 additional damage on hit. Once per day when you fire an aquatic arrow, you can use your bonus action to cause the arrow to explode in a powerful torrent of water as it hits its target. Each enemy within 5 feet of the target must make a DC 12 Dexterity saving throw, taking 2d6 cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

name: Trident of Deep Terrors
desc:
This seemingly coral weapon's prongs seem to be made of the hardened beaks of things unknown. 

You may use a bonus action to speak this weapon's command word - causing eerie green wisps to shimmer along the weapon's length and in your hand. While this weapon is alight, it deals an additional 2d6 psychic damage and you may use a bonus action to call this weapon to your hand.

name: Band of Familiars
desc:
When you wear this band around your arm, the bond with your familiar deepens. Any familiar that is summoned using 'Find Familiar' will have x4 its HP. 

If your familiar takes damage, and if you can see the familiar, you may use your reaction to get them out of harm's way. The familiar has immunity to all damage until the beginning of your next turn.

You can use your action to turn your familiar into a Large version of itself, making you able to ride it. The familiar gains the physical stats (STR/DEX/CON), HP, AC, and movement speed(s) of one of these animals of your choice:

 - Griffon
 - Riding Horse 
 - Giant Goat
 - Giant Owl
 - Mastiff

Once you use this ability, the familiar can change shapes between the chosen form and its original form for 8 hours. If you cast 'Find Familiar' (using a spell slot or cast ritually) during these 8 hours, you can choose a new animal. It replaces your old choice. After these 8 hours are up, this ability cannot be used for 5 days.

name: Frostvein Hammer
desc:
The head of this rugged hammer glows a faint blue when drawn, illuminated by veins of crushed elemental crystal running in intricate lines across the metal.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and it deals an extra 1d4 cold damage on a hit.

**Rimestrike.** The hammer has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a Large or smaller creature with the hammer, you can expend a charge to force it to make a DC 15 Strength saving throw. On a failure, it takes 3d4 cold damage and is restrained by ice until the end of its next turn. On a success, it takes half damage and is not restrained.

name: Shield of Resolution
desc:
This star-shaped mithril buckler is very lightweight, though incredibly unyielding. A golden, glassy lens is set in its center, which shimmers in the sunlight.

While holding this shield, you have a +2 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.

You can use a bonus action while holding the shield to cause it to emit bright light from the lens in a 15 foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet. The light lasts until you use a bonus action to extinguish it or until you fall to 0 hit points.

**Radiant Aura.** As an action, you can doff the shield and speak its command word, throwing it to a friendly creature (if it has a free hand) or unoccupied space within 30 feet of you that you can see. For 1 minute, the shield casts bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Any friendly creature within the bright light can add your Charisma modifier to any saving throws it makes, and it has advantage on saving throws against being frightened. Completely covering the affected shield with an opaque object blocks the light. Once the light fades, this property can't be used again for 12 hours.

name: Ring of Guidance
desc: This ring has 3 charges, and it regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing the ring, you can use an action to expend 1 of its charges to cast the Guidance Spell.

name: Olga's Magical Tea Spoon
desc: You choose an area of liquid that fits within a 5-foot cube. Any liquid in the area that is stirred with this magical teaspoon can be flavored as either Chamomile, Jasmine, Earl Gray, or Green Tea.

name: Pouch of Magic Spices.
desc:
These magical spices can be sprinkled onto any edible substance. They look like regular spices, but can be used to change the subtance's taste to anything, including for it to be tasteless. 

Once they have been sprinkled on an edible substance, it is enchanted by its magic for one hour. A creature who consumes these spices must succeed on a 13 DC Wisdom saving throw or be under its effects.

Creatures under the effects of these spices create a magical psychic link for 1 hour. The linked creatures can share any physical sensation they feel to all others under the effect so long as the creatures share the same plane of existence. The effect ends after one hour or is dispelled by dispel magic

Each serving can affect up to ten creatures. The pouch holds three servings.

name: Shroud of the Twelfth Lord
desc:
A striking red cape made of an endless waterfall of blood. 

When a creature you can see moves into a space within 5 feet of you or starts its turn there, strands of blood whip at the intrusion, dealing 1d6 slashing damage. The user can choose not to inflict this damage.

If the user is wearing the cape when they are sleeping, the cape surrounds the user in a protective cocoon at night and alerts them to any danger within 10ft.

Your Intelligence score is 19 while you wear this shroud. It has no effect on you if your Intelligence is 19 or higher without it

name: Elara's Sword
desc:
This magic longsword has the finesse property. You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While grasping the hilt of this long-sword, you can use a bonus action to cause the blade to be bathed in pure radiance.  

While activated, the weapon deals radiant damage instead of slashing damage. When you hit an aberration with it, that target takes an extra 1d8 radiant damage.

The sword's luminous blade emits bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet. The light is sunlight. While the blade persists, you can use an action to expand or reduce its radius of bright and dim light to a maximum of 30 feet each or a minimum of 5 feet each.

name: Staff of the White Hand
desc:
This gnarled, jet-black staff has a curled white handprint painted underneath its head.

You can use a bonus action to speak this staff's command word, causing the painted handprint to manifest as a spectral hand. The hand lasts for 1 minute or until you use a bonus action to speak the command word again.

Whenever you hit a creature with the hand-end of the staff, it must succeed a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or take 3d6 necrotic damage.

The hand can be attacked while it is manifested. It has an Armor Class of 15 and 20 hit points. If the hand drops to 0 hit points, the staff loses its magical properties, becoming a mundane quarterstaff. As long as it's not destroyed, the staff regains all lost hit points when it reverts to its inanimate form.

name: Lightning Whip (Exalted)
desc:
This magical whip is eternally bathed in lighting energy. It deals 1d6 slashing damage and an extra 2d4 lightning damage to any target it hits. 

While holding this whip, you have resistance to lightning damage.

**Overcharge:** The whip has three charges and regains all of them each day at dawn. When you hit a creature that is not immune to lightning damage with this weapon, you can expend 1 of its charges to overcharge it and force the creature to make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes an extra 3d6 lightning damage and its speed becomes 0 until the start of your next turn, as they lock into place. On a success, it takes half damage and its speed is halved instead.

**Lightning Bolt:** Once a day, you can use an action to strike the whip to cause a bolt of lightning to leap from it in a line that is 5 feet wide and 120 feet long. Each creature in that line must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw, taking 12d6 lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

**Lighting Wave:** Once a day, you can use an action to cause the whip to issue an electrifying wave. Each creature within 30 or 60 feet of you (your choice of range) must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 6d8 lightning damage and becomes sheathed in lighting energy until the start of your next turn. Targets sheathed in lighting have their speed halved. Additionally, If the target willingly moves 5 feet or more before your next turn, the target takes an extra 4d6 lightning damage. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't sheathed in lighting.

**Lighting Strike:**  Once a day, when you hit with a melee attack using the whip, you can cause the target to take an extra 3d6 lightning damage. You may not use this feature at the same time as *Overcharge.*

**Lighting Shock:** Once a day, when you hit a creature that is not immune to lighting with a melee attack using the whip, you can cause the staff to emit a targeted shock to the creature. The target you hit must succeed on a DC 17 Constitution saving throw or become stunned until the end of your next turn.

**Shocking Strike:** Once a day,  you can use an action to use the *Lightning Strike* and *Lighting Shock* properties at the same time. Doing so doesn't expend the daily use of those properties, only the use of this one.

**One with the Whip:** Once a day, while holding this weapon, you might use your action to call upon the strength of the whip. When you do, you become sheathed in lighting for 1 minute, gaining the following benefits for the duration:

*  You have immunity to lightning damage.
* Any creature that starts its turn five feet from you receives 1d6 lightning damage.
* Any unarmed attack or an attack made with a natural weapon deals an extra 2d4 lightning damage
* As a bonus action, you might hurl lighting to one creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The target must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw, taking 2d8 lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Once you attune to this weapon, its normally shocking handle feels fine to the touch.

name: Last Breath of Ysilda
meta: Martial weapon (longbow), ranged weapon, very rare (requires attunement by a cleric or paladin)
desc:
This icy longbow constantly emits an aura of frost and rime, and there is a lantern attached to the bottom of it that glows with a pale blue light. 

The bow counts as a holy symbol for you.
 
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit with an attack using this magic longbow, the target takes an extra 1d8 radiant damage.

As a bonus action, you can speak the command word and open the lantern attached to the bottom of the bow to cause the lantern to emanate a thin cloud of frost and wind out to 10 feet for 1 minute. At the start of each of your turns, you and any other creatures in the cloud each regain 1d8 hit points. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

name: Dancer’s Bangle
desc:
This triangular bracelet is composed of three polished rods, each made of a different semiprecious stone.

You can use a bonus action to speak this bracelet’s command word, causing it to splinter apart into three floating shards. The shards orbit around you until you use a bonus action to speak the command word again, transforming them back to their bracelet form.

While the bracelet is activated, the shards are magical darts with no long range penalties. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with the shards, and they return back to you immediately after being thrown.

Additionally, each shard deals an extra 2d4 damage on a hit. The damage type of the shard depends on which stone it is composed of.

If you attempt to attune to a second Dancer’s Bangle, you can change the composition of each Bangle as part of the attunement process. As normal, however, you cannot attune to two Bangles simultaneously.

**Shard List**
Carnelian (Fire); Lapis (Cold); Turquoise (Lightning); Jade (Poison); Malachite (Acid); Quartz (Thunder); Iolite (Psychic); Moonstone (Radiant); Onyx (Necrotic); Opal (Force)

name: Orb of Fun
desc:
A shimmering orb of dubious magical energy.

You can use an action to point this orb at a person or object. When you do so and touch the surface of the orb, roll a d10000 and ping Moox.

NOTE: This item will not work when on a job, or when inside the Raven's Mark.

name: Mechanism of Fun
desc:
An oblong object. It has a handle, with some sort of trigger mechanism and a barrel. It is brightly-colored and has a small note with it. '10.000 Wacky Ways to have Fun!'.

You can use an action to point this mechanism at a person or object. When the trigger is pulled, roll a d10000 and ping Moox.

NOTE: This item will not work when on a job, or when inside the Raven's Mark.

name: Helm of Enhanced Telepathy
desc:
While wearing this helm, you can use an action to cast the **detect thoughts** spell (save DC 15) from it. The range of the spell effect is expanded to 120 ft of you. As long as you maintain concentration on the spell, you can use a Bonus Action to send a telepathic message to a creature you are focused on. It can reply — using a Bonus Action to do so — while your focus on it continues.

While focusing on a creature with **detect thoughts**, you can use an action to cast the **suggestion** spell (save DC 15) from the Helm on that creature if they are within 30 ft. Once used, the **suggestion** property can't be used again until the next dawn.

name: Ring of Alacrity
desc: This black metal ring has a dodecahedral opal set into it. Whilst wearing this ring, you can use it to cast the spell 'Gift of Alacrity'. Once the ring has been used in this way, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

name: Dunestrider Boots
desc:
This set of footwear is made of light, supple leather and is always quite cool to the touch. While you wear them, you gain the following benefits:
- You have resistance to fire damage.
- You ignore difficult terrain created by sand or loose earth.
- You can tolerate temperatures as high as 150 degrees Fahrenheit and as low as 0 degrees Fahrenheit without any additional protection.

name: Rubies of Location
desc:
These ruby stones are magically paired. As long as both pieces remain in the same plane of existence, you can use an action while holding one to activate them and locate the exact distance and direction of the other one. This effect lasts until you use an action to turn it off. 

As part of the action, you can set the ruby stones so that only one of them can be activated.

name: Midas Hand
desc:
While holding this gold hand, you can use an action to turn any object or creature it touches gold-colored. The effect remains for twenty-four hours.

Using this feature on the same creature or object once every day for a year makes the effect permanent.

name: Noose of Rope Trick
desc: Pulling on the noose of this magical rope, allows the user to cast the spell **Rope Trick.** Once this feature has been used, it cannot be used until next dawn.

name: Ring of Force
desc:
A silver ring with golden ram's horns curling around the edge of its own. 

After making an unarmed attack or an attack with a natural weapon, you can choose to activate the ring's power to infuse your strike with its magic.  A wave of force explodes from the impact, doing an extra 2d10 force damage. Once this feature has been used, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

name: Ring of Concentration
desc: Once a day, you may call upon the ring's power. When you fail a concentration check as a result of taking damage, you can reroll the result. You must use the new roll.

name: Canister of Soothing Tea
desc:
This small ornate metal canister contains 6 bags of magical tea. You can steep a bag of tea for 1 minute in a cup of boiling water to transform the water into a rich, flavorful tea that calms the nerves and revitalizes the spirit. When you drink a cup of the hot tea, you regain 1d6 + 2 hit points and gain the same amount as temporary hit points. In addition, any effect causing you to be frightened is suppressed for 1 hour, after which any suppressed effect resumes, provided that its duration hasn't expired in the meantime.

If the tea has been left to cool before you drink it, you regain only half the amount of hit points and temporary hit points, and no other effects are suppressed. The canister regains 1d4+2 bags at dawn.

name: Bracers of The Weapon Master
desc:
These bracers are studded with 12 solid mithril studs spaced equally along each bracer.

The wearer of these bracers can store a weapon in each stud, at which point an engraving of the weapon appears on the surface of the stud.

The wearer can summon forth any weapon that has been stored in the bracer at will, including during an attack roll, allowing the wearer to switch quickly between different potential weapons.

name: Arrow of Vitality
desc:
This magic arrow is blessed with divine energy. An attack made with this ammunition deals no damage to any target hit by it. When you hit a creature other than yourself with this ammunition, that creature regains hit points equal to 2d4 + your Dexterity and Wisdom modifiers (minimum 1).

 If the target is a friendly creature, you have an advantage on the attack. Once it hits a creature, the arrow loses its magic until the next dawn. This arrow has no effect on undead or constructs.

name: Ring of the Necromancer
meta:
*Ring, very rare*

**Attunement**
*requires attunement by a spellcaster*
desc:
This Black ring with white gray accents was worn by one of the tomb rulers

While wearing this ring, when casting a spell that allows you to create undead from corpses, it allows you to target an extra corpse. Additionally, they are risen with extra hit points equal to your character level.

name: Ring of Harm Protection
desc:
This black ring with white stripes was worn by one of the tree tomb rulers.

You have advantage on saving throws against spells.

The ring has 2 charges. If you fail a saving throw against a necromancy spell or a harmful effect originating from an undead creature, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and turn the failed save into a successful one. The ring regains one charge each day at dawn.

name: Orb of Answers
desc:
This black orb has 3 charges. As an action, you can rattle the orb and ask it a question that can be answered with a yes or no. 

Roll a d10, on a 1 an inscription appears on the orb that says "Try Again Later" and the charge is consumed. 

On any other roll of the die, you receive a correct answer for the question.

In a case where a yes or no answer might be misleading or the answer might be unclear, an inscription with the words "Reply Hazy" will appear.

The Orb might alternate between "It assuredly so,"  "Signs point to yes"" , "Yes," and  "Definitely so"  to respond affirmatively and "Definitely not," My sources say no," "No," and "My reply is no" to respond negatively.

Once a charge has been used, it takes a week from the moment of asking for the Orb to regain that charge.

name: Ring of Pure Darkness
desc:
This black ring with red stripes was worn by one of the tomb rulers.

Any non-evil person takes 1d8 fire damage the first time it is worn. 

While wearing this ring, you gain a +1 bonus to AC and saving Throws. 

Once a day, you can use the ring to cast the **Finger of Death** or **Circle of Death** spells.

name: Threads of the Restless
desc:
These salvaged wrappings from the arms of a mummy lord hang loosely from your arms but never seem to slide off. While wearing the wraps, you have resistance to necrotic damage.

In addition, while wearing the wraps, you can use a bonus action when you take the Attack action to cause a piece of the wrapping to lash out at a target within 10 feet of you. Make a melee spell attack for the wrapping, using an attack bonus of +9. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 necrotic damage.

The wraps have 3 charges and regain 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. As an action, you can hold out your hand and expend 1 or more of the wraps' charges to release a blast of sand from it in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in the area must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature is blinded until the start of your next turn, and for each charge you spend, it takes 1d6 bludgeoning damage plus 2d8 necrotic damage. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't blinded

name: Evanova's Frozen Crown
desc:
While wearing this frozen crown, you can command the TRACE system.

While wearing the crown, you can cast the **Ray of Frost** and **Frostbite** spells.  Additionally, once a day, you can cast the **Snilloc's Snowball Swarm** spell. These spells use a spell save 15 DC and spell bonus attack of +7.

name: Felipiano's Flaming Baton
desc:
While holding this baton, you can command the tower's security system.

While holding this baton you can cast the **Fire Bolt** and **Produce Flame** spells.  Additionally, once a day, you can cast the **Aganazzar's Scorcher** spell. These spells use a spell save 15 DC and spell bonus attack of +7.

name: Magic Flipbook
desc:
While holding this flipbook, you can use an action to magically print a sequence into the book from your mind. The flipbook can create a sequence that lasts up to a minute.

Once the sequence is created, it plays out for anyone until someone uses an action to create a new sequence.

name: Felipiano's Signet Ring
desc: *This ring works as a key to unlock specific arcane locks across Felipiano's Tower.*

name: Dunestrider Greataxe
desc:
A small crystal orb is set in the center of this runic axe-head, filled with a fine, golden sand.

You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

**Dune Stride.** While you are attuned to this axe, you have resistance to fire damage, and you ignore difficult terrain created by sand or loose earth. Additionally, you can tolerate temperatures as high as 150 degrees Fahrenheit and as low as 0 degrees Fahrenheit without any additional protection.

**Call the Sands.** The axe has 10 charges and regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. As a bonus action, you can expend 1 or more charges to summon a stream of sand from the axe. The sand spreads in a 5 foot radius, plus an additional 5 feet for each charge expended.

When you summon the sand, you can choose to have it travel with you or remain where you summoned it. If the sand travels with you, its radius is halved.

The sand lasts for 1 minute before dissipating in the wind. When you summon it, you choose which form it takes:
- **Dunes.** The sand pours onto the ground, transforming the area into difficult terrain.
- **Sandstorm.** The sand swirls around in a sphere. A creature that starts its turn in the sphere or enters for the first time takes 1d8 magical slashing damage. You are immune to this damage.

name: Dancing Asaran Saber
desc:
The hilt of this long, curved blade is bejeweled with brilliant rubies. While you are attuned to it, you can use it as a longsword with the finesse property.

You can use a bonus action to toss this magic sword into the air and speak its command word. When you do so, the sword begins to hover, flies up to 30 feet, and attacks one creature of your choice within 5 feet of it. The sword uses your attack roll and ability score modifier to damage rolls, and it is considered to be wielded in two hands for the purposes of the Versatile trait.

While the sword hovers, you can use a bonus action to cause it to fly up to 30 feet to another spot within 30 feet of you. As part of the same bonus action, you can cause the sword to attack one creature within 5 feet of it.

After the hovering sword attacks for the fourth time, it flies up to 30 feet and tries to return to your hand. If you have no hand free, it falls to the ground at your feet. If the sword has no unobstructed path to you, it moves as close to you as it can and then falls to the ground. It also ceases to hover if you grasp it or move more than 30 feet away from it.

name: Sunhome Mint Tea
desc:
This small glass vial contains enough loose leaf tea to make 1d4+1 cups of magical tea. You can submerge a pinch in a cup of boiling water for 1 minute to transform the water into a rich, flavorful tea. After you drink a cup of the hot tea, you are under the effects of the bless spell for 1 hour.

You can drink the tea using an action, although it's most commonly finished over the course of at least 1 minute. If the tea has been left to cool before you drink it, its effects only last for 10 minutes.

name: Bow of Light (Uncommon)
desc: The ammunition fired from this weapon glows with a warm, white light until the start of your next turn. This weapon deals an additional 1d8 damage to Undead and Fiends.

name: Bow of Light (Rare)
desc: The ammunition fired from this weapon glows with a warm, white light until the start of your next turn. The rare variant deals an additional 1d8 radiant damage on a hit. Additionally, this weapon deals an extra 1d8 damage to Undead and Fiends.

name: Bow of Light (Very Rare)
desc: The ammunition fired from this weapon glows with a warm, white light until the start of your next turn. The very rare variant is a +1 weapon that deals an additional 1d8 radiant damage on a hit. Additionally, this weapon deals an extra 1d8 damage to Undead and Fiends.

name: Bow of the Huntmaster
desc: Once per day, as a bonus action while wielding this weapon, you can cast the spell Hunter's Mark. Once the spell ends, this property cannot be used again until the next dawn.

name: Renegade
desc: This +1 weapon will occasionally take matters into its own hands. If you roll a 1 on your attack roll when using this weapon, roll a die corresponding to the number of targets within your weapon's range. You may then make an attack against the rolled target.

name: Old Ironsides
desc: +1 weapon. This crossbow looks old and worn, but well kept and functional. You can cast the Shield spell once while wielding this weapon. You regain the use of this property after a short or long rest.

name: Silent Strike
desc: This +1 weapon is magically silenced while operated. If you take the attack action with this weapon while you are Hidden, you remain hidden after the first shot, and after every subsequent shot done as part as that Attack Action. Afterwards, you are revealed as normal. You must make all of the attacks without interruption.

name: Blackrock Defender
desc: This +2 crossbow has Vershnilan proof marks on its stock, and a crest of Castle Blackrock emblazoned on the bow. When you make an attack with this weapon and another enemy is within 10 ft of the original target, you may make another attack at the second target as part of the same action. You must finish a Long or Short Rest to use this ability again.

name: Ring of Minor Illusion
desc: This ring has 3 charges. While wearing it, you can use an action and expend 1 charge to cast minor illusion (spell save DC 13) from it. The ring regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

name: Boots of Running
desc: These boots increase the wearer's speed by 10 ft.

name: Plate of Minor Image
desc: A small, rectangular metal plate about the size of a Tarot card. When activated using an Action, the face of the plate shows a minor illusion of anything you point at. This effect lasts up to 1 minute, requires concentration, and can be ended using a bonus action. When the effect ends, the image is instantly etched into the plate until the next time it is used in this way. The effect cannot be used again until the next dawn.

name: Instant Treehouse
desc:
You can use an action to throw this 6-inch wooden twig at a Large tree and speak its command word. The twig attaches to the tree and grows a treehouse over the course of one minute. If the branches of the tree are not strong enough, the twig adds extra supports on the bottom, but it takes up to ten minutes to do so. The treehouse remains until you use an action to speak the command word that dismisses it, which only works if the treehouse is empty.

The treehouse is a 15x15x15 cube which can (snugly) accommodate up to four people. It has a roof and is a suitable shelter in most non-extreme conditions. The treehouse will push creatures out of the way as it grows to fit the space, but will not damage them.

name: Everwarm Pebble
desc: A small, magically warm stone. Enough to keep hands warm.

name: Clothes of Comfort
desc: On command, this item of clothing will cool or heat to a temperature comfortable to the wearer. This effect is not strong enough to protect from extreme temperatures.

name: Cloak of Majestic Presence
desc: Upon arriving at a specified location, or upon command (somatic), this cloak announces your arrival with loud music and/or illusory visual effects. You need to attune to the cloak and have the musical piece performed at a DC15 check in order for the cloak to reproduce it. The cloak can hold up to 10 minutes of music in increments of 1 minute or more. The visual component of the presentation works as with an Instrument of Illusions, and must similarly be performed before the cloak can reproduce it. The visual effects can be up to a minute long, in 10 second increments.

name: Weapon Charm - Lantern
desc: This tiny lantern can be attached to the grip or pommel of a weapon. On command, it casts light as per the Light cantrip.

name: Bonded Rings
desc: These silver rings often come in pairs and indicate the well-being of its wearer. If you and another friendly creature are wearing these rings within 120 feet of each other, you are magically aware of the other creature's current number of hit points and condition.

name: Death Turner
desc:
This tiny, spinning hourglass has six seconds' worth of sand inside it and can be easily held or worn as a pendant. When a creature that you can see within 120 feet of you dies, you can use your reaction to speak the hourglass's command word and spin it, allowing you to immediately take an extra turn, interrupting the current turn.

If at the end of that turn, the dead creature remains dead, it returns to life with a number of hit points equal to your current hit point total (up to its hit point maximum), and you die. If the dead creature was prone, it can immediately stand up (no action required). Once this property has been used, it can't be used again until 2 months has passed.

When you die as a result of using the hourglass, your body is enchanted by protective magic for 24 hours. During this time, if a spell, such as raise dead, has the sole effect of restoring you to life (but not undeath), the caster doesn’t need material components to cast the spell on you. In addition, while you're protected by this magic, you are also under the effect of the gentle repose spell.

name: Manual of Lost Fighting Styles
desc:
This book's magically charged pages are filled with detailed descriptions and illustrations that teach unique styles of combat. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying the book's contents and practicing its guidelines, you gain one of the following Advanced Fighting Styles:

**Advanced Simplicity.** Your attacks made with simple weapons score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

**Mortal Blows**. When you score a critical hit using a melee weapon that has the heavy property, the target takes 10 extra damage.

**Net Mastery.** When you throw a net, its normal and long range is doubled. In addition, the first time that a creature attempts to escape from a net that you threw, the escape DC is equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice), unless it's already higher.
 
**Quick Shot.** You gain a special reach with ranged weapons called a firing reach that's a number of feet equal to 5 + your normal reach. While you are wielding a ranged weapon, other creatures provoke an opportunity attack from you when they move out of your firing reach.

**Readied Bulwark.** You gain a +2 bonus to AC against opportunity attacks while holding a shield.

**Versatile Expert.** When you hit with an attack using a weapon that has the versatile property, you can use the two-handed damage die even if you're only wielding the weapon with one hand.

 Once the book has been used to grant a lost Fighting Style, it loses its magic, but regains it in a century.

name: Earrings of  Whispers
desc: While wearing these earrings, you can cast the **message** spell.

name: Bracelet of Armor Disguise
desc:
While wearing this bracelet, you can use an action to create an illusion on any armor you are wearing. You may change the illusion on the armor to appear as different armor, clothes, body accessories, or none at all.

Physical interaction with the armor reveals it to be an illusion. A creature that uses its action to examine the image can determine that it is an illusion with a successful 15 DC Intelligence (Investigation) check.

name: Pixie Lamp
desc:
As an action, you can speak the command word to cause this lamp to shed bright light in a 10- feet radius. Any creature under the light is affected by the pixie magic imbued within. A prone creature under the light only needs 5 ft of movement to stand up as faint golden dust helps them raise from the ground more easily.

The lamp sheds light for one hour or until the user uses an action to turn its light off. Once the lamp has been used in this way it cannot be used again until next dawn.

name: Asetri's Heart
desc: A magical gem in the realistic shape of Asetri's heart. While touching this gem, you can telepathically communicate with Asetris. This communication transcends extra-planar distance.

name: Living Eye
desc:
A magical living eye that imprints on you and follows you around. It is impervious to all damage. As an action, the eye can grow insect-like legs out of its body to move around.  With these legs, the eye can walk on walls as if replicating the spider walk spell.

The creature does not follow your command and its sole purpose seems to just follow you around wherever you go.

name: Ring of the Mind Eaters
desc:
This Silver band resembles several interwoven tentacles and it is always cold to the touch.

Once a day, you can release a blast of psionic power from the ring. Every creature in a 20 ft cone originating from you must make a 15 (DC) Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes 3d8 psychic damage and can be pushed back up to ten feet. On a success, the creature takes half-damage and it is not pushed back.

name: Stormstruck
desc:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and it deals an extra 1d4 lightning damage on a hit.

**Thunder Strike.** The hammer has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a huge or smaller target with the warhammer you can spend a charge and cause a thunderous force to be released on impact.  The creature takes an additional 2d6 thunder damage and is pushed up to ten feet.

name: Watchful Eye
desc:
A flask with a black liquid-like substance with a floating living eye inside. It is instructed to keep it away from sunlight at all times and in an open space.

**Learned Traits:**

- The substance will try to replace one of your eyes if the flask is opened. 

- The substance creates 1 extra eye on the person's body after some time of residing within the subject's eye socket.

While one of your eyes is replaced with the one inside the flask, you can use an action to see through your original's eye location.

You can also use an action to activate one of the following benefits:

**Darkvision.** You gain dark vision out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have darkvision, its range is increased by the same amount

**See Invisibility.** You gain the ability to see invisible objects, writing, and creatures.

**Ethereal Sight.**  You can see into the ethereal plane out to a range of 60 feet. 

These benefits last for one hour. Only one of these can be activated at one time. Once one of these options has been activated, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

name: Sash of the Alchemist
desc:
This leather sash has two glass vials strapped to the front, each filled halfway with water. Though the vials are stoppered, it’s impossible to open either of them up.

You can use an action to pull off one of the vials and throw it up to 20 feet. Make a ranged attack roll for the vial, treating yourself as proficient. On a hit, you can mentally command the liquid to transform into acid, oil, or alchemist’s fire. If you issue no command, the liquid transforms randomly on its own.

The vial behaves as a standard vial of the transformed substance. Any missing vials magically regenerate daily at dawn.

name: Bahamut's Shield
desc:
This magical Platinum shield glows with a majestic blue sheen and is said to have been forged out of one of Bahamut's Scales.

While holding this shield, you gain a +3 bonus to AC and resistance to cold damage. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.

Once a day, as a reaction, you might activate the shield's defenses to halve the damage taken from one attack. This energy is then stored in the shield for 1 minute. As a bonus action, you might cause a burst of force within 20 ft of you. Every creature in that range must make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw or take radiant damage equal to the halved portion of the damage.

While holding this shield, you may activate one of its presences at any time.

**Frightening Presence:** Any creature hostile to you that starts its turn within 20 feet of you must make a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is frightened until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, the creature is immune to this power of the shield for the next 24 hours. Once you use this power, you can’t use it again until the next dawn.

**Heroic Presence:** Any willing creature of your choice that starts its turn within 20 feet of you is affected by the presence as if under the **Bless** spell. Once you use this power, you can’t use it again until the next dawn.

**Majestic Presence:**  Any willing creature of your choice that starts its turn within 20 feet of you gains advantage on Charisma based checks. Once you use this power, you can’t use it again until the next dawn.

Each one of these presences lasts for 1 minute once activated and only one may be active at once.

name: Bahamut's Greatsword
desc:
This immense sword is made of bright platinum and refracts light in the metallic colors of all good dragons. It is said to have been created out of one of Bahamut's claws.

You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

When you attack a creature with this magic weapon and roll a 20 on the attack roll, that target takes an extra 1d6 fire, lighting, cold, acid, radiant, and force damage. Once a day, you may force a creature to take this extra damage no matter the attack roll.

This sword has five properties.When you active one of its properties, the sword radiates a specific color:

**Brass Strike:** When you hit a creature with this sword, you can shower them with sleeping magic. The target must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it falls unconscious until the end of your next turn. This effect ends for a creature if the creature takes damage or someone uses an action to wake it.

 **Bronze Strike:** When you hit a creature with this sword, you can create a repulsive force on impact. The creature must make a DC 17 Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is pushed up to 60 feet away from you.

**Copper Strike:** When you hit a creature with this sword, you can envelop them in slowing magic. The target must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature can't use reactions, its speed is halved, and it can't make more than one attack on its turn. In addition, the creature can use either an action or a bonus action on its turn, but not both. This effect lasts until the end of your next turn.

**Gold Strike:** When you hit a creature with this sword, you can strike with weakening force. The creature must make a DC 17 Strength saving throw. On a failure, they gain disadvantage on Strength-based attack rolls, Strength checks, and Strength saving throws until the end of your next turn. 

**Silver Strike:**  When you hit a creature with this sword, you can strike with paralyzing force. The creature must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they are paralyzed until the end of your next turn.

Once one of these properties has been used, it cannot be used again until the next dawn. A creature may only be under one of these effects at one time.

name: Bahamut's Mask
desc:
This mask of platinum radiance reshapes and molds to the wearer's face. It is said to have been created and forged out of Bahamut's spines and frills.

While wearing the mask you are granted draconic aspects and gain the following benefits: 

**Draconic Cunning:**  You have advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against magic.

**Draconic Breathing:** You can breathe underwater.

**Draconic Fury:** While your current hit points are equal to or less than half your hit point maximum, you deal an extra 1d4 cold damage with your melee attacks.

**Draconic Resistance:** You gain resistance to cold and radiant damage.  

**Draconic Sight:** You gain darkvision out to 60 feet, or to an additional 60 feet if you already have that sense. 

**Draconic Tongue:**  You can speak and understand Draconic. You also have advantage on any Charisma check you make against  Metallic Dragons.

**Draconic Wings:** You can manifest platinum wings from your back, gaining a set flying speed of 120 feet.

name: Bahamut's Blessing
desc:
You have been honored with a blessing from Bahamut, The Platinum King, God of Good Dragons.

* You gain +1 to saving throws.
* You gain proficiency on one skill of your choice.
* You gain proficiency with one weapon, instrument, or tool of your choice.
* You gain the ability to speak, write, and understand Celestial, Draconic, and Auran. 
* You gain the ability to learn one of the following cantrips:  **Acid Splash, Firebolt, Ray of Frost, Sacred Flame, Shocking Grasp.** You use your spellcasting modifers or Wisdom score if you do not have one.

name: Bahamut's  Bowstring
desc:
This bowstring radiates an intense platinum light, releasing a celestial hum while it is strung back. It is said to have been made from one of Bahamut's whiskers.

This bowstring provides several benefits to those who use it. These can be activated at any time during your turn and can be used until the end of your turn:  

* Encase your ammunition in ice, gaining a 2d6 Cold bonus to damage rolls.

* Encase your ammunition in lighting, gaining a 1d6 lighting bonus to damage rolls and disrupting the reactions of targets you hit until the start of your next turn.

* Encase your ammunition in force, gaining a 1d6 force bonus to damage rolls and allowing you to push a target you hit up to ten feet

* Encase your ammunition in celestial energy, turning its damage into healing.

*  Turn your ammunition ethereal, ignoring cover, including total cover.

* Create light, allowing you to recreate the **Daylight** spell once on a piece of ammunition.

*  Empower your shots, allowing them to ignore all resistances.

* Quicken your movements, allowing you to fire one extra ammunition.

* Stabilize your aim, gaining a +10 bonus to attack rolls.

Once one of these abilities has been used, it cannot be used again until the next dawn. Only one of these can be active at once.

name: Bahamut's Tear
desc:
A frozen tear once shed by the King of Dragons. A tear can replace any spellcasting component worth up to 10000 gold. 

If the spell component was consumed by the spell, the tear is consumed as part of the process.

name: Dragon King Bag
desc: This bag works exactly as a **Bag of Holding** but is branded with the mark and symbol of the Dragon King himself.

name: Coins of Bahamut
desc: These coins of Bahamut signify the goodwill and honor of the owner and can gain you favors across the Platinum Palace of Bahamut.

name: Whistles of Friendship
desc: These Whistles signify your growing friendship with the Friendly Hollyphant Turu during your stay at the Monastery in Mount Celestia.

name: Bracers of the Weapon Master
desc:
These bracers are studded with 12 solid mithril studs spaced equally along each bracer.

The wearer of these bracers can store a weapon in each stud, at which point an engraving of the weapon appears on the surface of the stud.

The wearer can summon forth any weapon that has been stored in the bracer at will, including during an attack roll, allowing the wearer to switch quickly between different potential weapons.

name: Mask of the Rat
desc: While wearing this mask, you have advantage on any ability check that relies on smell. In addition, you can use an action to track a particular scent. When you do, you perceive that smell visually as a cloud of vapor for up to 1 hour. Whenever you make an ability check made to track the scent, you treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10. Once this property of the amulet has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

name: Moodmark Ring
desc: This ring changes to reflect your mental state. A creature that can see the ring and makes a successful DC 10 Wisdom (Insight) check can discern whether you are happy, sad, angry, disgusted, surprised, or afraid, as well as the main source of that emotion. For example, you might communicate fear caused by a monster you just saw around the corner, grief at the loss of a friend, or happiness derived from pride in your performance in combat. A dark elf has advantage on this check.

name: Ring of the Marid
desc:
This ring was crafted by Zinuf the Damp in the Plane of Water. While this ring is on the body of a Marid, it has no effects.

When you are attuned to the ring:

- You gain resistance to Cold and Acid damage.
- You gain vulnerability to Lightning damage.
- You are immune to the effects of creatures and items from the Plane of Water, that would change your shape or body. Previous effects you have incurred are reverted.

name: Stein of the Drunkard
desc: This detailed stein has a laughing face carved into one side. Any nonmagical alcoholic beverage drunk from this stein bypasses immunity or resistance to the 'poisoned' condition. The stein has no effect on poisons or magical substances.

name: Silver Lion Cane
desc:
The head of this jet-black cane is adorned with the silver statuette of a roaring lion. It can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff in one hand, though it lacks the Versatile trait, and it has a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

The cane has 5 charges, and it regains 1d4+1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the lion statuette tarnishes and never regains another charge.

While holding the cane, you can use an action to expend some of its charges to cause one of the following effects:

**Presence (1 charge).** You infuse your voice with magic, gaining advantage on the first Charisma (Intimidation) check you make within the next minute.

**Command (1 charge).** You compel another creature to grovel or flee, as with the Command spell (save DC 15).

**Fear (2 charges).** You cause the cane to emit a loud roar. Each creature within 20 ft. of you must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or become frightened of you for 1 minute. While it is frightened in this way, a creature must spend its turns trying to move as far away from you as it can, and it can't willingly move to a space within 20 feet of you. It also can't take reactions. For its action, it can use only the Dash action or try to escape from an effect that prevents it from moving. If it has nowhere it can move, the creature can use the Dodge action. At the end of each of its turns, a creature can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.

name: Robe of Bats
desc:
A robe of bats is a stylish silk garment that is popular among wealthy nobles and retired assassins. The robe is emblazoned with 9 stylized bats, all brightly colored.

As a bonus action on your turn, you can transform one of the robe’s bats into a giant bat. The bat instantly flies from the robe, hovering into an unoccupied space next to you, and acts on your initiative count. The bat can tell friendly creatures from hostile ones and attacks the latter. The bat disappears in a harmless puff of smoke after 1 hour, when it drops to 0 hit points, or when you dismiss it (no action required). Once detached, a bat can’t return to the robe. When all of the rob’s bats have detached, the robe becomes a nonmagical garment.

name: Whip Sword
desc:
This magic longsword has the finesse property. You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.  Due to its advanced craftsmanship, this weapon deals 1d10 slashing damage while held with one or both hands.

While grasping the hilt of this long-sword, you can use a bonus action to cause the interlocking mechanisms of the blade to expand, turning it into a serrated whip. During whip form, this weapon does 1d6 slashing damage instead of the original 1d4.

While holding this weapon, you can decide to take double your **Hemocraft Die** damage to use your **Crimson Rite Feature** as a free action. Additionally, you may choose for your Rite damage to be necrotic damage.

Proficiency with a longsword allows you to add your proficiency bonus to the attack roll for any attack you make with it on both forms.

This weapon's metal seems to absorb the blood from the wounds done with it.

name: Blood Crystal
desc:
As an action you can slice your hand and bleed onto the crystal, taking 2d4 + 2 necrotic damage as it hungrily sucks at your blood. Your hit point maximum is reduced by an amount equal to the necrotic damage taken until your next finish a long rest. Once the crystal has been used this way, it will stop receiving more blood.

As a bonus action, you can hold the crystal to a wound and squeeze against it. The crystal expends its charge and you recover a number of hit points equal to the number of necrotic damage you took.

Once the crystal has been used to heal, it cannot be used in this form again until the next dawn.

name: Magic Rock
desc:
This small rock looks exactly the same as any other normal rock and it fits on the palm of your hand. As an action, you can speak its command word and cause it to instantly grow into a 10 ft. radius stone. 

As an action, you can throw the rock and activate it mid-air to a range of 60 ft. Any creature on its landing zone must make a DC 15 Dexterity Saving Throw, taking 6d6 bludgeoning damage on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful . All creatures smaller than the rock are pushed to the nearest unoccupied space beside it.

You can speak its command word once again to turn it back into a small rock.

Once the rock has been magically enlarged, it cannot be enlarged again until the next dawn.

name: Cloak of Perpetual  Suffering
desc:
This white and golden cloak has been imbued with magic.  Once a day, you can use your reaction to cause it to come between you and incoming danger, halving the amount of damage you take. 

While you wear it, the cloak randomly screams with the voice of a woman who is in deep pain.

name: Ring of Life Stealing
desc:
This diamond ring has been imbued with magic. Whenever you deal necrotic damage, the gem of this ring steals a piece of their life-force, dealing 1 extra necrotic damage.

The clear gem of the ring turns darker with each time that you deal necrotic damage to an enemy. Once the ring has obtained 10 charges, it becomes entirely black. You can use your bonus action to release that energy around your weapon. For the next minute, your melee attacks do an extra 1d6 necrotic damage. The ring cannot obtain more charges during that time, after the time has passed, it turns entirely clear once again.

name: Bloodmist Cloak
desc:
This tattered crimson cloak is silky-smooth to the touch. In darkness, it almost seems to move on its own, as though propelled by an invisible wind.

If you are reduced to zero hit points while wearing the cloak but not killed outright, you can cause the cloak to flutter to life and envelop your body. If you do so, you regain 1d6 hit points and transform into a cloud of red mist, as per the Gaseous Form spell. This transformation lasts for 1 minute, but it ends instantly if you enter sunlight or running water, or are reduced to zero hit points. Sunlight or running water also instantly drop you to zero hit points. It cannot otherwise be ended early.

While in mist form, you cannot take any actions aside from Dash or Disengage. Once the transformation ends, you revert back to your original form.

Once you use this cloak's property, you cannot do so again until the next dusk.

name: Spiderweb Bracers
desc:
These jet-black metal bracers are embossed with an eight-spoked pattern of spiderwebs.

The bracers have 2 charges and regain 1d2 expended charges daily at dusk. When you are hit with a melee attack, you may expend one charge to use your reaction to spray a tangle of sticky webbing at your attacker. The creature must succeed a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or become entangled by the webbing. While entangled, the creature's speed is reduced by 10 ft., and it has disadvantage on the first weapon attack it makes.

The webbing lasts for 1 minute, or until a creature uses its action to remove the webbing. If the webbed creature takes any amount of slashing or fire damage, the webs are also removed.

name: Collapsible Shield
desc: A small, round disk, a few inches across. You can use an action to activate and don the item, which grows to the size of a normal round shield, and acts like one. Using an action, you can doff the shield, which shrinks back to its inert state.

name: The Avowed's Magnificent Magnifier
desc: This magical magnifying glass helps deduce the arcane nature of items. While looking through the glass at an object within 1 foot of you, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any Intelligence (Arcana) or Intelligence (Investigation) check you make regarding that object's magical properties (you gain proficiency for the purpose of this check only). In addition, you can speak the glass's command word as an action to cast the identify spell from it, targeting an object with the spell that you can see through the glass within 1 foot of you. Each time you do, there is a cumulative 5 percent chance that the glass shatters and is destroyed after casting the spell.

name: Hagsbane's Transformation
desc:
This weapon has been transformed by the influence in the Star's Tunnel. A starry swirling glow comes and goes along its surface, making the weapon's weight change slightly under your hold. As a result, it gains the following properties:

This weapon does an extra 1d4 Force damage on Hit.

Additionally, if you attune to the weapon, you gain these additional properties: 

- This weapon has two charges. As part of your attack, you can activate a charge and attempt to change a Huge or smaller creature's gravity. The creature must make a DC 15 strength save throw, on a failure they either: 

     Fall prone to the ground, unable to move until the start of their next turn. 

  Float five feet up in the air, unable to move until the end of your next turn.

- Once a day, you can affect your own gravity as if you were under the effects of the ***levitate*** spell. The effects last ten minutes or until you use a bonus action to end it. You can use this as a reaction when falling.


This weapon recharges one charge daily at dawn.

name: Whipsword's Transformation
desc:
This weapon has been transformed by the influence in the Star's Tunnel. Stange translucent black vines with a mysterious red liquid pulse across its hilt and surface. As a result, if you attune to the weapon, it gains the following properties:

- When you hit a huge or smaller creature while in sword mode, you can force it to make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, it takes 2d4 psychic damage and is restrained by black living vines that wrap around them on impact. A creature restrained by the vines can use its action to make a DC 15 Strength check to free itself.

- While in whip form, you might activate the vines to extend the weapon's reach. You might decide between extending its reach by five feet for one minute, or ten feet for one turn.

Once one of these features has been used, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

name: Hot and Loud's Transformation
desc:
This weapon has been transformed by the influence in the Star's Tunnel. As a result, any ammunition placed in it seems to shift in and out of reality and time.
