Additionally, if you attune to the weapon, you gain these additional properties: 

- When you miss an attack, you can repeat the same attack one more time, as the bolt moves back in time onto your weapon again.

- When you hit with an attack, you can cause the weapon's elemental damage to happen once again, causing an extra 1d4 fire and 1d4 thunder damage. 

- You can target a spot in the air with the weapon. There the bolt's physicality changes into a rain of bolts that cover a 15-ft-radius sphere on the ground. A creature in this space must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take 8d8 piercing damage.  On a success, the creatures take half the damage.

Once one of these features has been used, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

name: Stormstrike's Transformation
desc:
This weapon has been transformed by the influence in the Star's Tunnel. A swirling burple magical energy sheathes the weapon at sporadic times. As a result, if you attune to the weapon, you gain these additional properties: 

- You can store spells of 5th level or lower in the magic energy that surrounds the weapon by casting it into it. The surrounding magical energy can store up to seven spell levels. The spells remain for one week before its magic disappears from it.

     While holding the weapon, you can cast the spells stored in it. The spell uses the slot level, your spell save DC, spell attack bonus, and spellcasting ability at the time of casting.

- Once a day, you can harness the magical energy around the sword by sacrificing some vitality. You can cast a spell you have prepared of fifth level or lower without using a slot by using this energy, but as a result, you take 5+(Slotlevelx1d10) psychic damage.

name: Black Goop
desc:
This edible black goop grants random magical effects to anyone who consumes it. Roll a 1d100 to determine which of its effects come into effect. 


**1:** Death
**2-10:** You lose your sight for 1d6 hours as your eyes disappear temporarily and instead gain blindsight at a range of ten feet.
**11-19:** You grow a temporary extra hand in a random part of your body. This hand cannot perform attacks or be used to cast spells. Otherwise, it functions like a normal hand. The hand falls off after 2d6 hours.
**20-29:** You are wrapped in star-like matter until the next dawn. As a result, you shed bright light 15 ft around you and dim-light an extra 15 ft.
**30-39:** You lose all ability to speak any language and instead gain telepathy at a range of 50 ft for 1d3 days. 
**40-49:**  You grow two extra eyes in the back of your head for 1d2 days, granting you advantage on all perceptions checks based on sight and the inability to be surprised as long as the eyes are not covered.
**50-59:** You get covered by an acidic slimy substance for 1d12 hours, granting you resistance to acid damage. Any creature that attacks you with a melee attack takes 1d4 acid damage as a result.
**60-69:** Sticky tentacles sprout out of your arms and gills form on your throat, granting you climbing and swimming speed equal to your walking speed, as well the ability to breath underwater. This changes last for 1d4 days.
**70-79:** You gain a luck point that can be used at any time over the next 4x1d10 days.
**80-89:** A hole opens up on your palm and a hundred and twenty-five gold pieces fall off from it at a rate of one per minute. After the last piece falls, the hole closes.
**90-99:** A piece of black candy grows out of your hand. It lasts for one day and you gain the benefits of a long rest once you eat it.
**100:**  You gain the benefits of the spell ***foresight*** for 1d4 days.

Only one of these can be active at any one time. Consuming more than one serving of Black Goop will lead to severe indigestion and no new effects. One vial of black goop allows for ten servings.

name: Aberrant Cleansing Stone
desc: This stone removes any undesired aberrational influence on any weapon of your choice. Once used, it becomes a normal stone.

name: Paint of Graphical Imaging
desc:
The paint in this bucket is multicolored, and the gradient seems to shift around almost on its own.

The bucket contains enough paint to cover a 5'x5' area. When you complete the painting, you can cause it to become animated, creating a short animation or other visual effects that last for 6 seconds before looping. Anyone who views the image can discern that it is paint without making a check.

The illusory effects last until they are dispelled, turning the image into an ordinary painting.

name: Arctic Coat
desc:
This thick, over-sized woolen coat boasts a fur collar and wraps snugly around the shoulders. While wearing this coat, you can tolerate temperatures as low as −100 degrees Fahrenheit without any additional protection, and have advantage on any Constitution saving throws made against inclement arctic weather.

The coat has 6 charges and regains 1d4+2 expended charges daily at dawn. You can use an action to expend up to 4 charges to cast **Armor of Agathys** at a level equal to the number of charges expended.

Additionally, if you take cold damage, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge to cast **Absorb Elements** without any components.

name: Icicle Blade
desc:
This item appears to be a hollow rapier hilt made of metal and glass, with bluish water swirling within. While grasping the hilt, you can use a bonus action to speak the weapon's command word, causing the water to surge forth into an icicle point or make the blade melt back into its hilt. 

You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon, which deals your choice of cold or piercing damage.

When you roll a 20 on an attack roll with this weapon, the ice surges outward and begins to crystallize on the target's body. You deal an extra 1d8 cold damage, and if it is a creature of Huge size or smaller, it has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

Additionally, while the blade persists, you can use an action to extinguish all non-magical flames within 30 ft. of you. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until an hour has passed.

name: Charm of the Hearth
desc:
This orange charm is always pleasantly warm to the touch. Whenever you finish a rest while wearing the charm around your neck, you lose one level of exhaustion and gain temporary hit points equal to two of your hit die plus your Constitution modifier (minimum 1). These temporary hit points last until your next rest.

You may place the charm on the ground and speak its command word as an action, transforming it into a crackling campfire. The fire lasts for one hour or until you revert it to its original form by speaking the command word again. Once the fire reverts to a charm, it cannot be used in this way again until the next dawn.

A creature that completes a short rest within 20 ft. of the fire may treat any hit die roll underneath that die's average as if it were the average value instead. If you yourself complete a short rest in this way, you also lose exhaustion and gain temporary hit points as if you were wearing the charm.

name: Sliver of Esoteric Essence
desc:
This shard of grey-purple crystal is ice-cold to the touch. While holding it, you can feel a faint pressure at the back of your head that dissipates as soon as you release the shard.

You can crush the shard in your hands and release its energy into one non-magical weapon you have in your possession. Doing so permanently destroys the shard and transforms the weapon into a **Lore-stalker's Weapon**. This process is irreversible.

name: Lore-stalker's Weapon
desc:
The core of this weapon pulsates with a faint purple glow and is ice-cold to the touch. Anyone who is not attuned to the weapon feels an uncomfortable pressure at the back of their head while they hold it.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and it deals an additional 1d4 psychic damage on a hit.

The weapon has 2 charges and regains 1d2 expended charges daily at dawn. While you hold the weapon in your hand, you can expend one charge to cast **Detect Thoughts** or **Mind Spike** without any components. The DC for these spells is 15.

name: Serpentine Scale
desc:
This grey scale is mottled with red flecks, and ice-cold to the touch. You can wear the scale as an amulet.

If you know another creature wearing a **serpentine scale** and that creature is within 1 mile of you, you can communicate telepathically with each other. As a bonus action, you can allow that creature to hear everything you hear for 1 hour. You can end this effect as a bonus action, and it ends if you're incapacitated.

While wearing the scale, you are immune to magic that allows other creatures to read your thoughts, determine whether you are lying, know your alignment, or know your creature type. Creatures can telepathically communicate with you only if you allow it, though communication through the scale itself will always go through.

name: Fanged Loop
desc:
This loop of three, hardened snake fangs is ice-cold to the touch. They can be worn as a bracelet, as long as care is taken not to pierce yourself with the points.

While wearing this loop, you can use an action to fire three rays of dark, psychic energy, targeting one creature or several. Make a ranged spell attack roll for each ray, with a bonus of +5. On a hit, a creature takes 2d6 psychic damage.

Once you use the loop in this way, you cannot do so again until the next dawn.

name: Bat Ring
desc:
This black ring has a hollow green gem that works as a lid. Once the lid is moved a bright light flashes from it. The light works much like a lantern, but has the symbol of a drawn bat on it, which is projected out when used.

The light is strong enough to reach up to 500 ft in a 10 ft radius cylinder. If pointed up, the light can be projected on the sky despite the distance to it.

The ring lasts a total of ten minutes before it needs to be charged again. The ring is charged manually If you spend two continuous hours rubbing the ring's gem, it recharges again.

name: Snail Sword
desc:
While in possession of this scimitar, you can yell the word "SNAIL" and transform into a giant Snail for up to eight hours. 

You retain all your known languages and can yell "SNAIL" again to return to your normal form.

Once this feature has been used, it cannot be used until the next dawn.

name: All or Nothing Coin
desc:
This gold coin has the symbol of a skull on one side or a diamond on the other one.

Before rolling in a situation where you would use a d20 die. You may flip this coin instead of rolling. If it falls on the skull's side your result will be a critical fail ( Natural 1), if you roll on the diamond's side your result will be a critical success (Natural 20).

Once this has been used twice, you cannot use it again until the next dawn.

name: Bag of Vernim
desc:
When you open this grimy bag, 3d20 rats come out from inside of it. If the bag were to be destroyed, 10d100 rats will appear from within it. The rats are not under the control of the person who opens the bag.

Once this bag has been opened three times in a day, it cannot summon rats until the next dawn.

name: Parchment of Desire
desc: A piece of old parchment that when held writes whatever the person holding it desires the most at that instance, given that such fits on a piece of paper.

name: Bone Knuckles
desc: Once a day, you may throw these knuckles and ask a question. A spirit of an old man will appear and answer that question to the best of his ability given his knowledge.

name: Olga's Magical Fork
desc: This magical Fork has the ability to change the taste and texture of anything that is eaten with it into delicious, recently baked pastries of all kinds.

name: Wand of Wands
desc: While holding this wand, you can use a bonus action to gain the benefits of another wand that you are carrying. Anytime the wand is used again to call for another wand, the new wand's effects replace the current one.

name: Fruit Matchbox
desc: Whenever a match is sparkled against this matchbox, it gives out the smell of a random fruit.

name: Bonded Bracelets
desc:
These pairs of bracelets are bonded to each other. When one breaks, the other one breaks as well. When one mends, the other mends as well.

You can use a command word to break or mend the rings.

name: Soap of Stealth
desc:
This bar of soap eliminates any odor that emanates from you for twenty-four hours.  You gain a +1 bonus to stealth as a result.

The bar is large enough to be used seven times before it disappears.

name: Seven
desc: A number seven made of wood, it rings any time a person within 30 ft says the word seven.

name: Boo
desc: Boo is not an item. He is a good boi that needs to be protected.

name: Maw's Petal
desc:
This petal grew on a flower within the mouth of an ancient and humongous creature at the bottom of the ocean. It was imbued with magical powers by Zunif the Damp.

When attuned to this petal, it transforms into a magical band made of flowers. The wearer gains resistance to cold damage and fire damage.

When the wearer of this petal gains cold or fire damage, roll a d20. On a 1, they become vulnerable to the triggering type of damage for one minute.

name: Vial of Holy Oil
desc:
You can spend an action to apply this oil to a melee weapon or ten pieces of ammunition. When hit by the weapon or ammunition, an undead or fiend creature must make a DC13 Wisdom saving throw. On a fail, the target takes 1d6 radiant damage.

The oil loses potency one minute after applying.

name: Heavy Whip
desc: This heavy variant of the whip sacrifices reach and weight for raw power.

name: Bracer of Flying Daggers, +1
desc:
This armband appears to have thin daggers strapped to it. As an action, you can pull up to two magic daggers from the bracer and immediately hurl them, making a ranged attack with each dagger. A dagger vanishes if you don't hurl it right away, and the daggers disappear right after they hit or miss. The bracer never runs out of daggers.

This variant of the standard *Bracer of Flying Daggers* grants a +1 bonus to hit and damage rolls made with the magic daggers.

name: Ruby of the War Mage, +1
desc:
Etched with eldritch runes, this 1-inch-diameter ruby allows you to use a simple or martial weapon as a spellcasting focus for your spells. For this property to work, you must attach the ruby to the weapon by pressing the ruby against it for at least 10 minutes. Thereafter, the ruby can't be removed unless you detach it as an action or the weapon is destroyed. Not even an antimagic field causes it to fall off. The ruby does fall off the weapon if your attunement to the ruby ends.


Additionally, you gain a +1 bonus to your spell attack rolls.

name: Ruby of the War Mage, +2
desc:
Etched with eldritch runes, this 1-inch-diameter ruby allows you to use a simple or martial weapon as a spellcasting focus for your spells. For this property to work, you must attach the ruby to the weapon by pressing the ruby against it for at least 10 minutes. Thereafter, the ruby can't be removed unless you detach it as an action or the weapon is destroyed. Not even an antimagic field causes it to fall off. The ruby does fall off the weapon if your attunement to the ruby ends.


Additionally, you gain a +2 bonus to your spell attack rolls.

name: Golden Orb
desc: A golden metallic orb with moving pieces. It some times do things when you move it in specific ways.

name: Staff of Dreams
desc:
This magical silver staff contains powers of unknown origin.

Once a week you can use the staff to cast the *dream*  spell. 

Once a day, you can hit a person with the staff in the head. As part of the attack, they must make a DC 17 Wisdom save or forget the last minute that has passed, including the hit with the staff.

name: Splintering Bolt
desc:
This magical bolt splinters into pieces once shot.

Choose up to five creatures you can see within 60 ft. Make a ranged attack against each target. On a hit, a target takes piercing damage equal to 3d12+proficiency bonus+Dexterity modifier.

Once it has been used, the bolt loses its magic.

name: Javelin of Misty Step
desc:
This magical javelin surges with magical winds around it. You do an extra 2d4 piercing damage on hit.

Once a day when you hit a creature with a ranged attack with this weapon, you can use your bonus action to teleport to the enemy and instantly make two melee attacks against the target.

name: Gloves of Perception
desc:
These magical gloves have the depiction of a closed eye on their palms.

As an action, you can speak its command word to have its eyes open. For the next two hours, you have advantage in perception (wisdom) checks, initiative rolls, and cannot be surprised.

Once this ability has been used, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

name: Transforming Sickle
desc:
This silvered sickle has a spot that can be pressed on its handle.

As a bonus action, you can transform the sickle into a scythe. The scythe uses the same properties as a glaive.

name: Vial of Gold
desc:
Any part of a weapon that is coated in this vial magically gains a gold-like look to it.

One Vial is enough to coat an entire weapon regardless of size.

name: The Sire's Chosen
desc:
This ornate rapier is entirely black, clouded with dark purple cracks. When pressed to one's ear or stabbed with it, you can hear mumbled whispers.

You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Additionally, you do an extra 1d4 psychic damage on hit.

When you attune to this weapon, you gain the following properties:
 
*  Once a day, you can cast the ***darkness*** spell.
*  Twice a day, on a hit, you can addle a creature's mind with whispers, giving it disadvantage on their attacks until the end of its next turn.
 * Once a day,  on a hit, you can force a creature with an intelligence greater than 2 to make a wisdom save DC 16 or become frightened of you for one minute. While frightened the creature's speed becomes 0. At the end of its turn, the creature can repeat its saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

name: The Champion's Blade
desc:
This magical longsword is engulfed with dancing electricity.

You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Additionally, you deal an extra 1d4 lightning damage on hit.

When you attune to this weapon, you can use the following properties:

* You can cast the ***shocking grasp*** cantrip.
* Once a day, you can cast the ***lighting bolt (5th-level version)*** and ***misty step*** spells.
* As a bonus action, you gain 10 ft flying speed and can hover for one hour. Once used, it recharges the next dawn.

The spells use a set +7 to attack rolls and 15 spell save DC.

name: Cloak of Insanity
desc:
Strange iridescent swirling patterns flow across this magical cloak. 

Once a day, as an action, you can target one creature to be affected by its magic for one minute. Whenever they start a turn 30ft or closer from you, they have disadvantage on any Wisdom or Charisma checks or saving throws as the cloak erodes their mind.

After their third turn under this magic, a creature can make a DC 20 Wisdom saving throw at the end of their following turns. On a success, it becomes immune to the effect for the next twenty-four hours.

name: The Assasin's Flying Dagger
desc:
This magical dagger floats in circles around your palm, crackling thunderous energy surging through it.  By concentrating on the weapon, you can cause it to shoot from around your hand.

This magical dagger is a ranged weapon (60/120), lacks the finesse property, and does piercing damage. You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. Additionally, you deal an additional 1d4 thunder damage on hit.

Once an attack has been made, you can use a free action to make the dagger magically reappear around your palm again.

Once a day, as a bonus action, you can speak a command word to draw the blade's magic, gaining the following properties for the next minute: 

* The blade magically sharpens, as a result, the dagger's damage die becomes a 1d6 instead of its normal 1d4.

* The blade magically erupts in flames, as a result, you gain an extra 1d4 fire damage.

Once a day, you can use its magical abilities to track a creature you have successfully hurt with it in the last twenty-four hours. For 10 minutes, you know the location of such creature as long as they are within 5 miles of you.

name: The Cultist's Desire
desc:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

You can cast the  ***suggestion*** spell, DC 15,  from this weapon. 

Once you have done so, you cannot do so again until the next dawn.

name: The Tormentor's Spear
desc:
You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

You can cast the spell ***hex*** from this weapon. 

Once you have done so, you cannot do so again until the next dawn.

name: Feyheart Ivy
meta: *Wondrous item, rare (requires attunement by a fey creature)*
desc:
A mysterious influence has caused this sprouting ivy seed to grow into a mass of leaves and vines. As an action, you may mentally command the vines to reshape and wrap around parts of your body or form into a wearable loop, such as a bracer, necklace, or crown.

The ivy also counts as a spellcasting focus for your spells, though you must have a free hand to manipulate it as normal.

The ivy has 3 charges, and regains 1d3 charges daily at dawn. While it is on your person, you can use a bonus action to animate the vines in your defense.

- **Shell (1+ charges):** The vines form a protective, thorny ward around you. You gain 10 temporary hit points for each charge expended, which last for 1 hour. A creature that hits you with a melee attack while you have these hit points takes 1d6 piercing damage for each charge expended.

- **Tendrils (1-2 charges):** You cause the vines to lash out at one creature that you can see within 10 feet of you. The creature can be no larger than Medium (1 charge) or Large (2 charges). It must succeed a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or be grappled for 1 minute. While grappled, a creature is also restrained and can use its action on each of its turns to escape the grapple (DC 15), freeing itself on a success. The effect ends early if you move more than 10 feet away from it.

name: Pocket Dress
desc:
A magical dress that can be stored in any pocket .You can decide the color and minor style changes of the dress before you bring it out.

The dress also has two pockets that lead to a 1 x1 ft extra-dimensional space that can hold up to 50 pounds.

If anyone compliments your dress, you must respond "Thanks, it has pockets"  Or all of its contents will be ejected out on the ground.

name: Majestic Beard
desc:
A majestic beard that reaches your navel and magically adapts to your face.

Any person that can see it and is 30 ft from you must succeed a DC 20 Wisdom save or feel compelled to compliment it.

name: Bear Rings
desc:
If you wear both bear rings in a finger of each hand, you awaken to its magic.

Once a day, as an action, you can magically try to influence a bear that you can see. The bear must succeed a DC15 Wisdom save or be influenced as if it were under the effect of the spell *Dominate Beast*.

This influence lasts one hour and the bear will turn hostile to you when it ends.

name: Force Gaunlets
desc:
These magical gloves turn invisible when worn, and instead, two magical spectral gauntlets appear hovering on your hands.

You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls to your unarmed strikes. Whenever you hit an enemy with an unarmed strike, the creature takes an additional 1d4 force damage.

Once a day, you can use a bonus action to activate the gauntlets' full potential for one minute. During this time, the spectral gauntlets grow in size and you gain the following benefits.

- Your unarmed attacks base die is replaced with a 1d10 die that does bludgeoning damage.

-  You can create an explosion of force by pointing your hands towards the ground. As a result, you gain the ability to jump up to 50 ft in any direction as a bonus action.

- Once per turn, you can create a wave of force on impact. When you hit a huge or smaller creature with an unarmed attack, you can push them five feet away.

- Once per activation, as an action, you can strike your gauntlets together and create a blast of force in a twenty feet radius. Every creature in that radius must make a strength save (DC 16)  or take  9d6 force damage. If the creature is huge or smaller, it is also pushed 15 feet away.  On a save, a creature takes half damage and it is not pushed away.

name: Trident of Whales
desc:
Once a day, as an action, you can use this trident by raising it to the skies. When you do you will be struck by lighting and take 2d10 lightning damage.

Afterward, roll a 1d100. On a 1-99 roll, a Killer whale appears ten feet in front of you. On a 100 roll, a Sperm Whale appears in front of you.

The Whale is charmed by you for the next 24 hours and follows your commands. Afterward, it turns hostile towards you.

name: Singing Clam Of The Seas
desc: When opened, this clam plays sea-shanties.

name: Third Time is The Charm, They Say
desc:
Whenever you miss an attack twice in a row, your third attack is rolled at advantage as the ring charming colors temporarily distract your enemy.

Once this occurs, the ring needs to recharge for one hour.

name: Sword In a Bottle
desc:
Magical seawater swirls around this hilt-like crystal bottle.

As a bonus action, you can activate the water rush out of the bottle and shape it into any type of sword. You have a 2d4 cold bonus damage to attacks made with any of its forms.

name: Salt  In The Wound
desc:
This magical dagger's edge is always coated in coarse sea salt. You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

Whenever you make an attack with this dagger, the salt left on the wound stings and hurts the enemy further as it rubs on their flesh. For each strike on the creature, it takes an extra two slashing damage at the start of its following turns.

A creature can use its action to brush away the salt from its wound, ending all effects

Any once creature can have a maximum of ten simultaneous salted wounds. 

 You can also use the salt as a spice to cook.

name: Mark of the Seas
desc:
This little conch can be used to call an enemy's attention. As a bonus action, choose a creature you can see within 30 ft of you. That creature must succeed on a DC 18 Wisdom Saving Throw or be unable to attack or cause harm to anyone but you on their following turn.

Once used, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

name: Glass of Wasps
desc:
After you take an attack, you can use a bonus action to throw or shatter this glass brimming with magical wasps at or on someone. You are proficient at this finesse attack and it has a range of 30/90. 

If the attack hits, hundreds of wasps start swarming the creature. If the attack misses, the wasps decide to instead swarm a random creature within 90 ft from them.

If their target dies, the wasps will move to a random creature within 90 ft of them. 

The Wasps use their dex+your proficiency modifier to attack, doing 8d4 damage on hit. Otherwise, they use the stats of a *swarm of wasps* with maximum hit points.

The wasps remain until every visible creature is dead around them or they are killed.

name: Star Certificate
desc: This pristine piece of paper declares you, the holder of such paper, the owner of a star named Icar

name: Source Crystal
desc:
While you wear this crystal, you can use an action to adapt your body to an aquatic environment, sprouting gills and growing webbing between your fingers. You can breathe underwater and gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed. This change lasts for one hour and cannot be used again until the next dawn.

In addition, whenever you roll a Hit Die to regain hit points, double the number of hit points it restores.

More features may be unlocked through further communion with the presence within the Crystal.

name: Sliver of Chaos
desc:
This condensed sliver of energy can be safely held in your hand, or worn as a necklace or amulet. It is constantly changing colors and temperatures, though even the extremes of its fluctuations don't seem to hurt to touch.

The sliver carries 11 charges, and regains 1d10+1 charges daily at dawn. You can spend 1-3 charges to activate one of the sliver's abilities. Each ability carries a 66% chance of incurring side effects when used, but this chance is reduced by 33% for each charge expended beyond the first (down to 0%).

- **Unstable Displacement:** As a bonus action, you teleport to a location you can see within 30 ft. of you. As a side effect, you become blinded upon arrival until the start of your next turn.
- **Chaos Strike:** When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expel a fragment of chaos. The target takes 1d20 force damage. As a side effect, you also take the same amount of force damage.
- **Matter Absorption:** When you are hit by elemental damage (any type besides bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing), you can use your reaction to gain resistance to the triggering type until the start of your next turn. As a side effect, you become poisoned until the end of your next turn, as the energy seeps into your body.

name: Molten Cragstone
desc:
This chunk of rock is warm, almost painfully hot to the touch. Veins of red and orange run across it like a web.

While you are attuned to the stone, you have resistance to cold damage, and you can withstand the effects of extreme cold weather with no ill effects.

You can use an action to slam the stone onto the ground, sending out a wave of molten magma in front of you. Each creature in a 40-ft cone or 15-ft radius circle centered on yourself (your choice) must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking 8d6 fire damage on a failure or half as much on a success. The area also becomes difficult terrain, and a creature that starts its turn in it or moves there for the first time on its turn takes 2d6 fire damage. You are immune to this effect.

Once you use this ability, you cannot do so again until the next dawn.

name: Branding Crystal
desc:
This pink tourmaline crystal is ice-cold to the touch and hums with subtle vibrations as you hold it. If you press the crystal against a melee weapon for 1 minute, the crystal sticks to the weapon and starts to coat its surface.

Whenever you roll a 20 on an attack roll against a creature with the crystallized weapon, you deal an extra 2d10 psychic damage and the creature must succeed a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be become poisoned, as crystal begins to invade its nervous system. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. If a creature remains poisoned by this effect for 1 minute, it immediately dies and transforms into a crystalline statue.

Once per day, you can activate this ability when you hit a creature with the weapon, regardless of the attack roll.

If you break your attunement to this crystal while it is attached to a weapon, it automatically detaches, and any extra crystal growth shatters into dust.

name: Sandstorm in a Bottle
desc:
This vial contains a pinch of sand from a desert in Limbo. The sand constantly swirls within the vial like a small, concentrated storm.

If you fall while attuned to this bottle, you can use your reaction to invoke the sandstorm. You can immediately fly up to 60 feet without provoking opportunity attacks. If you are still falling after this movement, you take only half damage from the fall.

Additionally, you can use an action to unleash the sandstorm in a 10-foot radius centered on you. The sandstorm follows you and lasts for 1 minute, or until you end it early as a bonus action. A creature that starts its turn in the sandstorm or moves there for the first time on its turn takes 1d4 slashing damage and must succeed a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become blinded until the start of its next turn. You are immune to its effects.

Once you use this second ability, you cannot do so again until the next dawn.

name: Slaadi Claw
desc:
This shattered, petrified shard of claw can be worn as an amulet or held in one hand.

While you wear or hold the claw in this way, you gain advantage on saving throws against the Confusion spell. Additionally, slaads automatically miss you on attack rolls of 1-4.

name: Vial of Empty Space
desc:
This glass vial is seemingly empty... but it actually contains emptiness from Limbo!

Once per day, you may cast *Levitate* from this bottle without consuming spell slots, targeting only yourself.

name: Raider's Weapon
desc:
This wicked-looking seems to be entirely alien in its design.

You gain a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls made with this weapon, and it deals an additional 1d6 psychic damage on a hit.

Additionally, you have advantage on attack rolls made against githzerai creatures.

name: Modron Translation Device
desc:
This strange device is mechanical and incomprehensible in design.

If you hold the device up to your ear, you can understand any spoken Modron within 30 ft. of you, as the device translates the words into Common that only you can hear.

If you hold the device up to your mouth, any words that you speak will be translated into Modron.

name: Bottle of Skywater
desc:
This bottle is half full of a clear seawater from the plane of Limbo. The water always flows to the top of the container, no matter how you orient it.

If you open the bottle, the magical chaos escapes and the water loses this property forever.

name: Sprig of Perfect Grass
desc:
This blade of perfectly green grass was taken from a meadow in Mechanus. It never requires watering, never wilts, and never grows. If it is damaged, it regrows and reheals within 24 hours.

If you pluck the blade of grass out of its soil, it loses all its magic and becomes a normal plant.

name: Xorith's Emblem
desc: This brass coin is embossed with an intricate inscription matching the amulet worn by Watchkeeper Xorith. Apparently, it can be used to contact him while you are on the plane of Limbo.

name: Star Crossed
desc: Once a day, you can swap consciousness with another willing star-crossed creature. This allows you to exist through their senses for an entire minute.

name: Star Goop
desc:
Drinking this star goop grants you a flying and hovering speed of 30 ft. 

Additionally, as a bonus action, you can attempt to telekinetically shove one creature you can see within 30 feet of you. When you do so, the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC 16) or be moved 5 feet toward you or away from you. A creature can willingly fail this save.

A minor side effect of its consumption is mild indigestion and terrifying nightmares involving strange abominations for one week after.

The effects of this potion last for one hour.

name: Ring of Arcane Infusement
desc:
While wearing this magical ring, you can draw more energy from the weave. 

Whenever you cast a spell, you can use a charge to empower it, casting it at one slot higher than you initially did. The spell consumes your original spell slot.

This ring has two charges and regains any used charge the next dawn.

name: Star-Infused Staff
desc:
This staff has been infused with the energy of V'allur, Devourer of Worlds.

While using this staff you have the ability to cast the spell  *telekenis*  (DC 18) once per day. 

As an action, you can send a flashing white light in a 30 ft cone. Every creature in that cone must succeed a Wisdom (18 DC) save or be stopped in time.

While a creature is stopped in time, they are incapacitated and unaffected by gravity. At the end of each of its turns, the target can make another Wisdom saving throw. On a success, the effect ends on the target.

The creature otherwise remains in this state for one minute or until you or your companions do anything harmful to it.

Once this feature has been used twice, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

name: Shield of Friendly Defense
desc:
This magical shield provides you with resistance to force and psychic damage.

Twice a day, As a reaction to an attack, you can cause a blast of force to explode from the shield. The creature must make a strength-saving throw (DC 16) or be pushed up to fifteen feet.
 
As a bonus action, you can cause the shield to go flying off your arm and hover around a creature of your choice you can see within 90 ft. Until the start of your next turn, such creature receives the benefits of the shield instead.

name: Dagger of Etherealness
desc: Any attack made with this dagger can affect creatures and objects on the ethereal place as long as the plane you are currently on borders with or is the Ethereal plane.

name: Ring of Water Transformation
desc:
You can use an action to activate this ring and transform yourself into a puddle of water for one minute.

Once it has been used, it becomes a mundane ring.

name: Vial of  Mutability
desc:
When a weapon is coated with the liquid in this vial, its metals start to change and its properties become mutable at will. 

A  person can decide for the weapon to gain or lose the two-handed and heavy properties at will, as the weapon changes its form. 

Losing the heavy or two-handed property reduces the damage die of the weapon by one die.

The vial loses its properties after one day of its creation and must be used before it.

name: Blanket of Warding
desc: A person who is resting under this blanket is under the effects of the *sanctuary* spell (DC 15).

name: Vial of Finesse
desc:
This potion contains a coating of dubious elements. Coating a non-natural weapon with it changes its weight and handling.

When you coat a weapon with this vial it gains the finesse property. It also loses the heavy property if it had it in the first place.

The property of this potion is lost after one month of being created if not used.

name: Coin Shooter
desc:
This strange contraption can be loaded with coins to be shot at people.  It has the same properties as that of a hand crossbow, except its range is 50/100.

The magical weapon's damage is determined by the coin's currency.

* A copper coin does 1d4 piercing damage.
* A Silver coin does 1d6 piercing damage.
* A Gold coin does 1d8 piercing damage.
* A Platinum coin does 1d12 piercing damage.

Any coin used will deform after being shot, therefore being unusable as either currency or ammunition thereafter.

name: Necklace of Fireflies
desc:
This delicately crafted necklace has eight firefly pendants on it.

As an action you can pull any number of them and throw them in the air. Each one of the forms into an actual firefly that you can command.

The firefly sheds light in a 5 ft radius and can be moved anywhere within sixty feet of you. A firefly cannot be further than 120 ft away from you and will continue to move towards you if so. If unable, it will reappear on your necklace as a pendant again.

After a minute, the firefly flights back to the necklace and becomes a pendant again. A used firefly cannot be used again until the next dawn.

name: Gloves of Mockery
meta:
*Wondrous item, rare*

**Attunement**
*Requires Attunement by a Bard*
desc:
*Sometimes words are the strongest of weapons.*

While wearing these gloves, you gain the following abilities:

**Burning Mockery:** When you use the *vicious mockery* spell, you can decide for it to do fire damage instead of psychic. 

**Venemous Mockery:** When you use the *vicious mockery* spell, you can decide for it to do poison damage instead of psychic. 

**Critical Mockery:** Twice a day, you can make your words cut deeper than normal. A creature who fails a saving throw against your *vicious mockery*  spell suffers an extra die of damage.

**Wounding Mockery:**  Twice a day, you can lace your words with lasting emotional pain. A creature who fails a save against your *vicious mockery* spell must also repeat the save at the start of their turn or take the same amount of damage again.

name: Best Friend's Protection
desc:
Once per day you can call for Best Friend's Aid. As a bonus action,  you can summon Best Friend's visage to protect you. It stands around you shielding you from damage. As a result, you receive temporary hit points equal to your blood-hunter level.

These temporary hit-points last until depleted or after one hour has passed. After which the visage will return to the sword.

name: Star-Infused Elephant Figurine
desc: This figure works exactly like the *marble elephant figurine of wondrous power* except the elephant is made out of star substance and posseses a 20 ft flying and hovering speed.

name: Necklace of Gravity Beads
desc:
This necklace has six pitch-black orbs of swirling energy.

As a bonus action, you can throw one of these beads to a place within 60 ft of you, at which point the bead turns into a gravitational hole. Every creature in a 30 ft radius of this point must make a DC 16 Str Save Check of be pulled 20 ft into it to the closes unoccupied space.

name: Dagger of Possibility
desc:
This magical dagger has a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

Once a day, when you throw this dagger towards an enemy, roll a 2d4. The dagger is affected by temporal magic, converging several timelines into one. Roll as many attacks as you rolled on the 2d4 against the target instead of the original one. 

Once used, this ability cannot be used again until the next dawn.

name: Bolt of Light
desc: Striking a creature with one of these bolts bathe them in light until the end of their next turn.

name: Once Removed
desc:
This magical dagger does an extra 1d4 force damage on hit.

Once a day, on hit, you can decide to leave the dagger's blade inside the target. On any turn after, you may decide to replace an attack to instead remove it from the creature. 

For every extra turn the creature had the blade lodged inside, it takes 1d6 force damage, to a maximum of 10d6 force damage.

name: Boots of  Indigestion
desc: If you are swallowed by a creature, the creature must make a DC 18 constitution save at the start of its turn or regurgitate you.

name: Pale Rose
desc:
This sword's hilt is made out of pristine marble. Its blade is white with swirling patterns of pale rose color. 

This longsword has the finesse property.

The weapon has currently developed the following magical feature:

- Once per turn, if you stab someone in the back with this weapon, you do an additional 1d6 radiant damage.

- Whenever the wielder stop holding or wielding the sword, while conscious, they immediately take one radiant damage.

name: Flute of Lullabies
desc: Once a day, you can cast the sleep spell by playing this flute.

name: Instrument of Inspiration
meta:
*Wondrous item, rare*

**Attunement**
*Requires Attunement by a Bard*
desc:
*Sometimes a couple of extra notes go a long way*

While attuned to this instrument, you gain the following benefits.

- **Opening Act:**  You have an additional Inspiration die. It is the first die used on a day and it resets at a new dawn.

- **From The Heart:** Once a day, your encouragement reaches more people than normal, allowing you to give two people a bout of inspiration when using your bardic inspiration feature.

* **Honeyed Notes:** Once a day, you might even use your encouragement to win over your enemies. You can spend on use of your bardic inspiration to target a creature you can see. The creature must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom Saving Throw or be charmed by you until the end of your next turn or until you do anything harmful to it.

* **Inspiring Solo:** On desperate times, a great solo might be just the thing in need. Once a day, as a bonus action, you may use this feature to use up to a number of inspiration die equal to your proficiency bonus. These cannot be given to the same creature.

name: Over There! No, Over Here!
desc:
While attuned to this magical shortbow you have advantage to hide when you take the hide action.

Additionally, any attack against a target you have successfully hidden from does an extra 1d6 piercing damage on hit.

name: Expanding Gum
desc:
This magical gum has magical properties. At some point after starting to chew on this gum, you can use an action to take it out and throw it at an enemy that is huger or smaller.

You are proficient with this finesse attack, and on a hit, the creature is restrained, escape DC (15). Once used, the gum loses its properties.

We recommend this piece of gum is ***NEVER*** swallowed.

name: Doorkiller
desc: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls you make with this magic weapon. If it hits an object made of wood, the hit is automatically a critical hit, and it can deal bludgeoning or slashing damage to the object (your choice).

name: Amber Spider Sweet
desc: Eating this sweet-tasting treat makes you immune to being frightened for one hour.

name: Lorette's Heart
desc:
The still-beating heart of the Witch Lorette, it drips with goo at almost all times.

While in the same plane as Lorette, you two can communicate feelings to one another.

Once a week, you can telepathically communicate with each other for one hour despite any distance.

name: Snowpeak Bulwark
desc:
This frosty tower shield is carved from a single slab of rock. A layer of rime coats its face, and the three-triangular symbol of a mountain peak is chiseled in its center.

While attuned to this shield, you gain resistance to fire and cold damage. You also are unaffected by difficult terrain caused by ice and snow, and you can withstand freezing weather conditions without issue. 

Additionally, this shield can carry 5 charges. It gains one charge whenever you take damage from a hostile source (only once per turn). For every 10 minutes you spend out of combat, the shield loses one charge.

**Rime Shards.** A creature that hits you with a melee attack takes cold damage equal to the number of charges accumulated, as the ice splinters and ricochets magically back at your attacker.

**Frost Shielding.** Whenever you take fire, cold, or piercing damage, reduce the total damage you take by the number of charges accumulated.

**Avalanche (3+ charges).** When you take damage, you can use your reaction to expend some or all of the shield's charges. A torrent of snow erupts out in a 60-ft cone in front of you, turning the area into difficult terrain for 1 minute. Each creature in the area must make a DC 17 Strength saving throw, taking 2d6 cold damage for each charge expended, or half as much on a success. On a failure, a creature is also restrained by the snow until it uses its action to free itself.

name: Boots of the Slipstream
desc:
A pair of decorative wings adorns the sides of these boots.

While wearing these boots, your speed increases by 10 ft. Additionally, you can cast *Etherealness* once as a free action without expending a spell slot, but it ends at the end of your turn. Once you cast the spell, you cannot do so again until the next dawn.

The boots have 7 charges and regain 1d6+1 charges daily at dawn. Whenever you take the Dash action, you can expend a charge to gain one the following benefits:
- You increase your speed by 20 ft. until the end of your current turn.
- You have a flying or swimming speed equal to your walking speed until the end of your current turn.
- As part of the Dash action, you can make one weapon attack or cast a cantrip.
- As part of the Dash action, you may attempt to escape from a grapple currently affecting you.

You can expend multiple charges in a single turn, but you can only benefit from each option once per turn.

name: Ring of the Infused Musician
desc:
This faceted amethyst ring has the image of a musical note inscribed on one of its faces.

While wearing the ring, you can use your action to cast *Animate Objects* without expending a spell slot. Once you cast the spell in this way, you cannot do so again until the next dawn.

If you targeted a medium or larger musical instrument with this spell, you may command the instrument(s) to play a song using the bonus action command from *Animate Objects*. When the song first starts, the instruments collectively cast one spell of your choice, depending on the size of the instruments:

- Medium or larger instruments can cast *Heroism*.
- Large or larger instruments can cast *Hypnotic Pattern*.
- Huge instruments can cast *Otto's Irresistible Dance*.

The song lasts for the duration of the spell, until you command them to start another song, or until you command them to stop. If the chosen spell requires concentration, the instruments are considered to be collectively concentrating on the spell but use your own concentration modifier.

name: Ring of Minor Puppetry
desc:
This faceted wooden ring has a vertical groove etched perpendicularly across its body.

While wearing this ring, you can target up to six nonmagical objects and cast *Animate Objects* on them. The spell functions as normal, except for the following changes:

- You target only six objects, not ten.
- The spell lasts for up to 10 minutes.
- Your concentration on this spell cannot be broken as a result of taking damage.

Once the spell ends, you cannot cast it again until the next dawn.

name: Elspeth's Staff
desc:
This staff can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff that grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. While holding it, you have a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls.

The staff has 10 charges, and it regains 1d6+4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the staff loses its properties and becomes a non-magical quarterstaff.

**Spells:** You can use an action to expend 1 or more of the staff's charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell save DC: 
- Charm Person (1 charge)
- Suggestion (2 charges)
- Modify Memory (5 charges)
- Dominate Person (5 charges)
- Mass Suggestion (6 charges)

While you are attuned to this staff, you are also considered to have these spells known and/or prepared.

name: Sanguine Insignia
desc:
This insignia bears a symbol of your Order. It can be acquired as either a magical tattoo or some form of accessory, such as an amulet or earring.

While you have this insignia on your person, you gain a +1 bonus to the saving throw DCs of your Blood Hunter features. If you are in the Order of the Profane Soul, you also gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your spells.

While you have this insignia on your person, you can use an action to regain all expended uses of your Blood Maledict feature. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

name: Bugle of Stealth
desc:
This looks to be a completely ordinary looking bugle, polished to a mirror sheen.

While you play the bugle at full volume, you become invisible along with everything you are wearing or carrying. The music magically carries up to 200 feet, audible to any creature not behind total cover. You can choose to have the bugle remain visible.

name: Ring of Infusion
desc:
This metal band has three sockets lined up in its center, with grooves carved to hold something in each one. Currently, each socket is empty.

As an object interaction, you can target one large or smaller non-magical object you can see within 30 ft. of you, imbuing it with transmutive magic. The object becomes infused by you for 10 minutes, or until you move more than 60 ft. away from it.

As a bonus action, you can command one infused object to take an action appropriate to its form, such as commanding rope to tie itself off, a vial to pour its contents, or a ladder to set itself up. The objects have a speed of 0 and cannot make attacks, but are capable of any animation required to complete the action. You can also control simple mechanisms such as levers, but not complex mechanism such as locks.

Once you infuse five objects, you cannot infuse any more until the next dawn.

name: Shipwright's Infusion Bead
desc:
This worn wooden bead has the small symbol of a ship's wheel carved into its center.

You may insert this bead into a *Ring of Infusion*, which allows you to command infused objects in new ways based on what material they are made of. If an object is made of multiple materials, pick which effect is created. An object is destroyed after it takes one of these commands.

- **Constrict (Rope):** You command an infused rope to lash out at a creature within 10 ft. of it. That creature must make a DC 15 Strength saving throw or become grappled and restrained for 1 minute. The creature can use its action to free itself.
- **Splintersalvo (Wood):** You command an infused wooden object to shatter itself. Creatures in a 15-ft. cone must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking 4d8 piercing damage on a failure, or half as much on a success.

name: Apprentice Windrider's Amulet
desc:
This shimmering opal amulet is strung on a golden chain.

While attuned to this amulet, you can cast the *Gust* cantrip at will (save DC 14).

This amulet has 3 charges and regains 1d3 charges daily at dawn. As an action, you can expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 14):
- Fog Cloud (1 charge)
- Gust of Wind (2 charges)
- Wind Wall (3 charges)

name: Vial of Condensed Red Ooze
desc:
This is a vial of viscous red slime.

The vial contains 7 portions of ooze. You can use 1 portion to coat one slashing or piercing weapon or up to three pieces of ammunition. Applying the ooze takes an action. A creature hit by the coated weapon or ammunition takes an additional 1d4 psychic damage. Once applied, the ooze retains potency for 1 minute before drying.

You can choose to apply 2 portions of the ooze to your weapon or ammunition. If you do, the psychic damage increases to 1d6, and a creature that is hit must succeed a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or have their mind muddled for 1 minute. During this time, it must subtract 1d4 from all attack rolls and saving throws it makes, as if affected by the *Bane* spell. Constructs, oozes, plants, and undead succeed on the save automatically.

name: Gnarled Hook
meta: *Weapon (any melee piercing weapon that lacks the two-handed property), uncommon (requires attunement)*
desc:
This shattered segment of wood seems almost alive in your grasp.

You gain a +1 bonus to hit and damage rolls made with this weapon.

When you make an attack with this weapon, you can cause it to elongate and lash out. The attack has a reach of 30 ft. and deals an additional 1d6 piercing damage on a hit. If the target is large or smaller, you can choose to pull it 10 ft. closer to you.

Alternatively, you can choose to target a wood or dirt surface within 30 ft. of you. Make an attack roll against AC 12. On a hit, you can pull yourself up to 30 ft. in that direction.

You can use this feature twice, and the weapon regains both uses each day at dawn.

name: Gold Star Sticker
desc: This Gold Star is a luck point to be used at any time.

name: Potion of Fire
desc: After drinking this potion, the next time you do fire damage, the damage die is maximized.

name: Sword of Shadows
desc:
This magical longsword has the finesse property. 

Once a day, as a free action, you can activate the sword to gain the properties of the *shadow blade* spell for one minute.

name: Ring of Quick and Permanent Death
desc:
This ring deals 1 necrotic damage per second to any person who wears it. It cannot be removed unless the person casts a *remove curse* spell on it, or the finger is severed.

Once it reduces a creature to 0 hp, their body turns to ash, and the ring loses its magic for the next two years.

name: Oak Tree Staff
desc:
This magical staff grants a +1 bonus to your spell attacks and saving throw DCs of your druid spells.

Once a day,  as an action, you can turn into a *Grandfather Oak*, with a  5 ft walking speed. You remain as a *Grandfather Oak* until you reach 0 hp, at which point you transform to humanoid form at 60ft high in the air. You can also turn back into humanoid form at any moment.

Any spell you cast that involves plant or plant life in any way has its range doubled.  It also has its area of effect tripled, and it acts as if it had been upcasted two levels over the used slot.

You can also change the damage type of any of your spell attacks to cold damage.

name: Teal Bag of Tricks
desc:
This ordinary bag, made from teal cloth, appears empty. Reaching inside the bag, however, reveals the presence of a small, smooth object. The bag weighs 1/2 pound.

You can use an action to pull the smooth object from the bag and throw it up to 20 feet. When the object lands, it transforms into a creature you determine by rolling a d8 and consulting the table that corresponds to the bag's color. See the Monsters Listing for the creature's statistics. The creature vanishes at the next dawn or when it is reduced to 0 hit points.

The creature is friendly to you and your companions, and it acts on your turn. You can use a bonus action to command how the creature moves and what action it takes on its next turn, or to give it general orders, such as to attack your enemies. In the absence of such orders, the creature acts in a fashion appropriate to its nature.
 
Once three smooth objects have been pulled from the bag, the bag can't be used again until the next dawn.

**Teal Bag of Tricks**

```|   d8 | Creature     |
|------|--------------|
|    1 | Chimeric Weasel      |
|    2 | Flying Snake         |
|    3 | Chimeric Cat         |
|    4 | Amphisbaena          |
|    5 | Giant Frog           |
|    6 | Hippocamp            |
|    7 | Two-Headed Crocodile |
|    8 | Category 1 Krasis    |```

name: Fart Sword
desc:
This magical rapier has been tarnished by a corpse flower essence. While holding it, you can use a bonus action to activate to enter one of two forms: Poisoner or Life Stealer.

While on its Poisoner Form, you gain the following benefits: 

* The rapier does an extra 1d6 poison damage on hit.
* You can cast the spell *Stinking Cloud* once per day.
* Once per day, you can release a blast of noxious smells and force a creature hit by tour attack to make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or be stunned until the end of their next turn.

While on  its Life-Stealer for, you gain the following benefits:

* The rapier does an extra 1d4 necrotic damage on hit.
* You can cast the *hex* spell once per day.
* Once per day, you can steal a person's vitality on hit, gaining hit points equal to half of the damage done to them.

Once a form has been activated, it will remain on it for the day, only allowing to change once a new dawn has come.

name: Amulet of Temporary Death
desc:
An amulet in the form of a heart, speaking one of its command words will cause you to die for four, eight, twelve, twenty-four, or forty-eight hours depending on the word you use.

Once a command word has been said, a small crack appears on the amulet, and the command word will not work again. Once all options have been utilized, the amulet will break in two and all magic will be lost.

Once the time has passed, you will resurrect where your dead body currently is, given that you have not lost any major organ, and with the same conditions you had before death.

name: Poisoner's  Teacup
desc: Any liquid poured into this teacup becomes poison. It does not change the taste or smell of the original content. The poison does 2d6 poison damage once ingested.

name: Bone Shards
desc: Crushing one of these shards lets you speak with the dead, as if you had cast the *speak with dead* spell.

name: Ring of Unyielding Spirit
meta:
*Ring, very rare*

**Attunement**
*Requires Attunement by a Barbarian*
desc:
*Focus tempered by pain.*


While wearing this ring, you can speak its command word to activate its inner blades. At the start of each round for the next minute, or until you speak the command word again, tiny blades will prick your finger.

The inner blades of this ring do 1 piercing damage, which cannot be negated in any way.

While wearing this magical ring you also gain the following benefits:

**Warrior Strenght:** You gain one extra rage. It is the first rage used in the day and it resets at a new dawn.

**Warrior Spirit:** Whenever you reach half-health or lower for the first time on a day during combat, or start combat under half health, and you are not raging or incapacitated, you automatically enter rage without spending one of your rages.

**Warrior Strikes:**  Once a day, you can focus your rage into your strikes. At the start of your turn, you can deice to double your base weapon damage die for that turn. This ability is not spent if all attacks on that turn miss.

**Warrior Aura:** Once a day, you tap deeper into the energy of your rage. A 5 ft wide aura surrounds you, with effects depending on your Primal Path.

The following effects occur for the Zealot Warrior, you choose to enter one of the following when you use this feature.
              
         -  *Protection:* One creature of your choice within your aura gains the same resistances given to you by your rage until the start of your next turn.
         -  *Retribution:*  Your Divine Fury damage is taken by any creature that ends its turn within your aura.

name: Brooch of  Marking
meta:
*Wondrous Item, very rare*

**Attunement**
*Requires Attunement by a Ranger*
desc:
*For a hunter is always ready to catch its prey.*

While attuned to this brooch, you gain the following benefits:

**Avid Mark:** You gain an additional use of your Favored Foe feature. It is the first one used in a day, and it resets at a new dawn.

**Expert Hunter:** Your Favored Foe feature does not require concentration.

**Fierce Mark:** Once a day you can strengthen your mark on a target, causing your Favored Foe damage to trigger on all of the attacks you make on that target.

**Swift Marks**  :  Once a day, you can apply your Favored Foe Feature on two creatures with only one use of it.

**True Strike:**  Once a day you can force advantage to hit one creature that has been marked with your Favored Foe Feature.

name: Duck, Duck, ..... Goose!
desc:
A wind-up duck that moves in place wherever you throw it once activated. A wind-up duck will walk in place for a number of turns equal to the number rolled in a 1d6 die, before honking and going off in a magical explosion of force.

Any creature in a 30 ft radius of the duck must make a Constitution saving throw DC (15). A creature takes 5d10 force damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Non-magical objects that are not worn or carried are destroyed.

name: Majestic Swimwear
desc: Anyone who wears this outfit gains advantage on persuasion checks made while swimming or underwater.

name: Candle of Appreciation
desc:
This candle burns for six hours. It sheds bright light for twenty feet and dim light for an additional twenty.
 
The light given by this candle is only seen by you and creatures of your choice. 

Every minute it passes, the candle mutters words of affirmation and appreciation to the holder that only they can hear.

name: Ring of Ghostly Visage
desc:
Once a day, you can activate this ring to give you an illusory ghastly visage of an undead ghost-like creature. This visage lasts for an hour and can be activated and deactivated at will during it. 

While activated, you have advantage on intimidation checks to all creatures that are not undead.

name: Instrument of Invisiblity
desc:
While playing this instrument, you are invisible. If at any point you stop playing, you stop being invisible, and its magic is lost until the next dawn.

Any person who attempts to play it past four continuous hours must be successful on a constitution save (DC 12). Each hour after, the save must be repeated and the DC is increased +5. On a failed save, your fingers or mouth cramp, and you stop playing.

The invisibility breaks temporarily if you attack a target or cast a spell, taking a minute of continuous playing afterward for the effect to resume.

name: Nature's Armor
desc:
This magical armor covers the wearer in nature's protection. It is lighter than metal and does not provide disadvantages to stealth. 

While worn by a druid, the armor provides a +2 AC bonus to wildshaped beast forms, as it magically morphs to protect the creature's form.

name: Weave Prism
meta: *Wondrous item (amulet), rare (requires attunement by a spellcaster)*
desc:
This small crystaline prism is set in a golden ring and worn on a thin chain. An endless sparkling light flashes within, in all the colors of the rainbow.

While attuned to this amulet, you gain one additional spell slot. This slot is a copy of one of your spell slots of your choice, though its level is capped at 3rd regardless of the original's level. If you have no spell slots at all, this slot is 1st level.

Additionally, this special slot refreshes on a short or long rest. If the slot would normally refresh this way (such as a warlock's pact slot), you can instead spend one minute in meditation to regain the slot. Once you do so, you cannot do so again until you have finished a short or long rest.

name: Standard of Valor
desc:
This small patch of fabric is embroidered with two golden chevrons.

You can take one minute to press the patch against a two-handed weapon you are holding, fusing the two together. Thereafter, whenever you draw this weapon, you can choose to create the spectral image of a war standard. The standard flutters from your weapon and can be of any color and design of your choosing.

While the standard is visible, it emanates a 10-ft aura of bravery. Allied creatures within this aura have advantage on saving throws made to resist being frightened. Additionally, if they start their turn within the aura, they gain 1d4+1 temporary hit points.

You can plant the weapon into the ground as a bonus action. When you do so, the standard doubles in size, increasing the radius of its aura to 20-ft. and the number of temporary hit points to 2d4+2. You can use an action to retrieve the standard, reverting it back to its normal size.

If you unattune to this item, it immediately detaches from its host weapon.

name: Shield Charm
desc:
This golden band has the image of a shield etched into its center signet.

While wearing the ring, you can use a bonus action to activate the magic within. When you do, a shimmering, golden aura surrounds you for one minute, providing a +2 bonus to AC.

Once you use this property, you cannot do so again until the next dawn.

name: Weapon of Warmth
desc: When this weapon is exposed to cold temperatures, it glows slightly orange and gives off a faint warmth. Dim light extends out in a 5-ft radius, and the holder is protected from weather as cold as 0 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Marks of Druidic Channeling
meta:
*Wondrous item, very rare*

**Atunnement**
*Requires attunement by a Druid*
desc:
While attuned to these marks, your druidic energy is channeled in a more focused and powerful manner, granting you several benefits.

Whenever you use wildshape, you can transform into plants and plant creatures instead, following the rules of wildshape of your given subclass.

You also gain the following benefits: 

 - **Sporic Overcharge:** You gain an extra wildshape. This wildshape can only be used to awaken your symbiotic form. It is the first wildshape spent whenever you enter your symbiotic form, and it cannot be regained until the next dawn.

- **Symbiotic Union:** Your Symbiotic Form grants you temporary hit points equal to five times your druid level instead of four. Your melee attacks during your Symbiotic Form do 1d8 damage instead of 1d6. Once per day, you might activate it as a bonus action instead of your action.

- **Deadly Spores:** Your Halo of Spores feature expands an extra five feet and does not use your reaction to be triggered. Furthermore, its damage goes up one die given your respective druid level and does half damage on a successful save. It can occur a maximum of once per round.

- **Necrotic Infestation:** Whenever you use fungal infestation, your zombies are created with additional hit points equal to your druid level and last for a number of hours equal to your proficiency level. Their attacks do an extra 1d6 necrotic damage. Its reaction range is also expanded to 30 ft.

- **Fungal Bombs:** Whenever you use your level 10 ability to spread spores, its sizes doubles, and you can use your reaction to cause the spread spores to burst into flames, dealing 2d6 damage to those inside them.

- **Fungal Combustion:** Once a day, you can cause your halo of spores to ignite and burn those in it, causing them to take 4d6 fire damage.

name: Ring of the Trickster's Boon
meta: *Wondrous item, rare (requires attunement by a spellcaster)*
desc:
This seemingly diamond ring is forged from fool's gold and set with a large gem of zirconium.

While attuned to this ring, you can concentrate on two spells at the same time, provided both spells are from the illusion school of magic.

Whilst using this ability, if you fail a Constitution saving throw to maintain concentration when you take damage, you lose concentration on both spells.

name: Amulet of Ample Fury
meta: *Wondrous item, uncommon (requires attunement by a barbarian)*
desc:
A small glass vial hangs from this amulet, filled with perpetually roiling blood.

While attuned to this amulet, you gain 1 extra use of your Rage feature.

name: Draught of Force
desc:
This round bottle is filled with a swirling storm of purple energy.

You can use an action to throw this draught at a point within 30 feet, upon which it shatters and releases the energy inside. Each creature within 10 feet of the exploding draught must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking 4d6 force damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

name: Eyeglasses of Chill
desc: While wearing these dark-lensed glasses, you have resistance to fire damage. In addition, you and everything you wear and carry are unharmed by temperatures as high as 175 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Hat of Sorcery
desc:
While you are wearing this magical hat, you gain the following benefits:

   *  You can use the hat as a spellcasting focus for your sorcerer spells.

  * You can try to cast a cantrip that you don’t know. The cantrip must be on the sorcerer spell list, and you must make a DC 10 charisma (Arcana) check. If the check succeeds, you cast the spell. If the check fails, so does the spell, and the action used to cast the spell is wasted. You can attempt to use this feature up to three times per day.

name: Hag's Bag
desc: This brown and grey tote bag is adorned with pink lace and smells of fish and mushrooms. The bag seems to breathe shallowly and appears to be alive to most creatures. This bag can hold up to 5 nonmagical objects. Small objects count as 2 towards this total. Medium objects count as 4 towards this total. It cannot hold any objects larger than medium size. Any creature that reaches into this bag with their hand to retrieve something inside is dealt 1 piercing damage as something bites them. This bag has 3 charges and regains all charges after finishing a long rest. By spending a charge, you cause all objects within the bag to animate as if by the animate objects spell. The bag itself maintains concentration on the spell, which ends if the bag is directly targeted by an attack and is hit (AC 11), or if the bag takes damage from an area of effect spell.

name: Blessing of the Lucky Ones
desc: Once a day, when you roll a natural one, you can decide to reroll it. You must take the new roll.

name: Gloves of Strength
desc: While wearing these gloves, your carry and drag capacity is doubled. It is tripled during full moons.

name: Potion of Crocodile Form
desc:
When you drink this potion, roll a 1d4, you take on the aspects of the crocodile for that number of hours.

During that time, your head reshapes to that of a crocodile and you grow its tails as well. You gain the *giant crocodile* bite and tail abilities, using your own stats or the crocodile's, whichever is higher.

You also gain +1 to AC as your body is sparsely covered by hard scales.

name: Blood Drinker
desc:
This magical sword has a +3 bonus to attack and bonus rolls. Whenever its magic is active, blood coats and swirls around the weapon. It seems to drink the blood from the wounds it causes.

To activate its magical properties, you need to cut yourself and coat it with your fresh blood to bind it to you, taking 2d10 slashing damage.

Whenever you strike a creature with this sword, you regain 3 hit points.

**Sanguine Blades:**  Once a day, once this weapon has done at least 50 damage, you can activate two blades of blood to swirl around you. They remain for one minute.As a bonus action, you can make an attack with each blade. The blades have the thrown property and do 2d10 slashing damage

**Sanguine Binding:** Once a day, once this weapon has done at least 100 dmg, you can activate its blood binding technique. As a bonus action, once each turn, you can cause threads of blood to wrap around a creature huge or smaller. The creature must succeed a DC 15 Strenght or Dexterety save or be restrained. 

**Flaming Blood:** Once a day, once this weapon has done at least 200 dmg, you can active its Flaming Blood trait with a bonus action, setting the blood on fire. While active, every strike you make with it or its sanguine blades do an extra 1d6 fire damage.  Similarly, any creature restrained by Sanguine Binding takes 1d10 fire damage at the start of its turns.

The weapon loses any blood charge at the start of a new dawn.

name: Freezing Rune
desc:
This rune can be applied to a nonmagical weapon. Once applied, the weapon gains the following properties:  

The weapon gains a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

The following benefits occur once you attune to the weapon:

**Cold Strikes:** Each time the weapon strikes an enemy, spikes of cold explode under them and around them, doing an extra 1d6 cold damage to it and all creatures around it.

**Freezing Blast:** Twice a day, you can use your action to cause a 20-ft radius explosion of cold around you. Every creature must make a Constitution saving throw DC 16 or take 5d6 cold damage.

**Cone of Cold::** Once a day you can cast the *cone of cold* spell at the sixth level.

 **Frozen Statue:** Twice a day, when you hit a creature that is huge or smaller, you can encase it on ice until the start of your next turn. The creature must make a Constitution saving throw DC 15 or be incapacitated. On a save, the creature is not incapacitated but its movement speed becomes 0.

 **Form of Ice:** Once a day, you can use your actions to enter a form of ice, being covered in a cold embrace. For the Next minute, you gain the following benefits:

*  You gain immunity to cold damage.
* Whenever a creature strikes you, it takes 1d6 cold damage.
* You can walk on any liquid substance without sinking or taking damage from if it washarmful.
* As a bonus action you can send blasts of cold to a creature you can see within 60 feet of you. The creature must make a Dexterity saving throw DC 17 or take 2d8 Cold Damage.


Once a day, you can choose to remove the rune from a weapon and apply it to a new one over the course of an hour. The rune's magic does not survive more than one day without being transferred into a new weapon after being removed.

name: Charm of Restoration
desc:
This charm has four charges and regains 1d4+1 per new dawn.  You can use one of its charges to cast any of the following spells: *lesser restoration*, *revivify*, *greater restoration*, *mass cure wounds*, *heal*.

Once one of those spells has been cast in a day, it cannot be cast again until the new day.

name: Cloak of Shadows
desc:
This cloak allows you to cast the *misty step* at will as long as you are moving from and into a shadowed area.

Once a day, you can cast the *dimension door* spell.

name: Pearl of Suns
desc:
This pearl necklace has four sun-colored pearls on them. Each pearl can replicate a spell you can cast, and it will be cast at sixth level, as long as it is an evocation spell. 

Once a pearl has been used, you cannot use it again until the next dawn.

name: The Never-Ending Flask
desc: This magical healing potion refills every new day. When drank, it heals you for 12d4+25 hit points.

name: Coating of Increased Range
desc: Applying this coating to a weapon with the range property increases its range by five extra feet, as it gains the ability to magically extend or compact depending where you wish to strike.

name: Grenade of Holy Water
desc: This contraption is filled with holy water. When thrown towards a spot, it explodes on contact, its mechanism bathing every creature in a twenty feet radius in holy water.

name: Holy Censer Bombs
desc:
As an action, you can throw one of these censers to a spot you can see within thirty feet. 

Once thrown, the censers activate, releasing a ten feet radius of holy water smoke. Any creature that starts its turn in the smoke area reacts as if struck by a holy water flask.

The censer releases smoke for one minute and is too hot to handle or be picked up once thrown.

name: Holy Blaster
desc:
This small conic weapon shoots crystallized shards of holy water in a fifteen feet cone. It has a range of 5 ft and it has the properties of a light crossbow.

To utilize, you must turn your holy water into pellets and load them into the weapon. One flask of holy water equals one ammunition and targets everyone in the fifteen feet cone.

name: Band of Zealotry
desc: Once per short rest, you can cause your enemy to take your Divine Fury damage a second time after striking it.

name: Barbed Cloak
desc: A creature that grapples or is grappled by the wearer of this magical cloak takes five piercing damage at the end of their turn each round as long as either grapple continues.

name: Ring of Flamesong
meta:
*Ring, very rare*

**Attunement**
*requires attunement by a wizard*
desc:
This magical scorched ring contains the secret style of dexterous infernal blade wielders. When activated, your weapons and yourself become sheathed with flames.

While attuned to the ring, you gain the following benefits: 

**Flame Blade:** You learn the *green flame blade*  cantrip.

**Song of Fire:** You gain an additional Bladesong per long rest. This can only be used to enter the Flamesong Style.

**Scorching Song:** Twice a day, you might cast the *Burning Hands* spell at third level.

**Igniting Strike:** Once a day, when you strike an enemy, you can ignite them in fire. At the start of each of their turns, they will take 1d10 fire damage until they use an action to douse the flames. 

**Fire Song:** Whenever you use your bladesong feature, you can instead enter the Flamesong style instead, which replaces your bladesong effects with the following: 

* You gain a 1d6 fire bonus to your melee attack damage rolls.

*  You gain resistance to fire.

* You have advantage on Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks.

*  Whenever you are hit with a melee attack while in Flamesong, the creature takes fire damage equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1).

name: Fiendish Chain
desc:
This magical chain can act as a magical whip that you are proficient with. It does an extra 1d6 fire damage.

As a bonus action, you can animate the chain instead. The Chain is a large object and has an AC of 17 and HP of 20 and it takes its turn after yours.  It has a +6 bonus to hit, it does 2d10 nonmagical slashing damage and can move for 20ft on its turn.

Once per combat, while animated, you can order the chain to try to restrain a larger or smaller creature instead of attacking. The creature must succeed a dexterity save DC 14 or be restrained. 

Once a minute has passed or the chain has reached 0 hp, it cannot be animated again until the new dawn.
