name: Bracelet of Bestial Spirit
desc:
While you are wearing this bracelet, you are more attuned to the world of beasts.

You gain advantage to wisdom (Animal Handling) checks.

name: Infernal Kazoo
desc: This Kazoo is used by Baphmnoth's spined devils to call reinforcements into an area.

name: Lance of Lighting
desc:
A lance covered in lighting, once carried by a black knight who perished in hell.

This magical lance has a  +1 bonus to attack rolls and a +1 lighting bonus to damage.

Once a day, you can cast the *lightning bolt* spell from its tip.

name: Feather Sword
desc: DO LATER

name: Shocking Dagger
desc:
This magical lighting does an extra 1d4 lightning damage. 

Once per day, when you hit a creature you have thrown the dagger at, you can choose to teleport to it. You create an explosion of lighting on contact that does 2d6 lighting damage to every creature within 20 feet of them.

name: Candle of Dreams
desc:
If this candle is lit when people are taking a rest, they are given wonderful dreams, joyous snippets of other people's wonderful lives.  Each creature wakes up with an inspiration point.

This candle has enough dreams in it to burn for three nights.

name: Bear Punch
desc:
Whenever you strike someone with these bear-engraved bar knuckles, you invoke the power of the bear.  Your unarmed strikes do 3 more damage. 

If you roll a critical hit with these, you summon a magical cave bear to strike your target. Once the target has been dealt with, the bear is not under your command.

Once the cave bear has been summoned, you cannot summon another bear until the new dawn.

name: Mask of Many Faces
desc:
This magical half mask covers a side of the face and can shape to show many expressions. Each of the expressions holds a particular magical essence

**Joyful Expression:** Any magical or nonmagical healing that occurs on you while wearing this expression heals for the maximum amount.

**Mournful Expression:** You and every ally within 60 ft of you have advantage on death-saving throws.

**Valiant Expression:** You and any ally ten feet from you has advantage against being frightened.

**Foolish Expression:**  You or a creature you choose that you can see gains advantage on the first attack they perform on a turn.

**Wrathful Expression:** You or a creature of your choice, which must remain ten feet from you, gain a bonus fire damage equal to your proficiency bonus. 

The mask has a mind of its own, and sometimes it changes to an expression without your input:

- If you fall below 30% of your hit points, the mask immediately changes to Joyful expression.

- If a friendly creature you can see falls unconscious or fails a death-saving throw, the mask immediately changes to Mournful Expression.

- If you or a friendly creature you can see become frightened, the mask immediately changes to Valiant Expression. 

- If a creature within thirty feet of you gains disadvantage or misses an attack, the mask automatically changes to Foolish Expression.

- If a creature within ten feet of you is attacked by a creature they attacked, the mask automatically changes to Wrathful Expression.

You can forcefully change the mask fo a expression of your choice at the start of your turn. This can only be done once per expression per long rest. 

 Additionally, once a day, you can force the mask to constantly change expressions for one round, granting all its benefits until the end of your next turns. Afterwards, the mask changes to a random expression.

name: Phoenix Feather
desc:
While this feather is on your person,  if you reach 0 hit points and do not die from the attack,  you can decide instead to use the feather's magic, rising from unconsciousness in flames. 

When you do, you regain 2d4+4 hit points. 

Once you have used the feather, it burns up into ashes.

name: Amulet of Aged Blood
desc: While wearing this amulet, you can regain one use of your blood maledict as a bonus action.

name: Feather Cape
desc:
This cape seems as if were the feather of a massive feathered bird. Its edges are permanently covered in frozen snow.


Once a day, you can activate the cape's magic to summon winds around you. For the next minute, you have advantage on dexterity saves and you gain ano

name: Golden Hummingbird Statuette
desc:
This magical hummingbird statuette can come to life once its command word is spoken. The hummingbird will remain animated for the next hour.

While animated you can give it the following commands as a free action:

**Hunt:** The hummingbird will fly to find the nearest object that is worth more than any specific amount of gold you specify. You can order it to disregard objects held by specific people.

**Message:** You can speak a message into it and designate a person you are familiar with. The hummingbird will fly to it as long as it is in the same plane of existence and deliver the message. Once the message is delivered, or its animation time has ended,  it will reappear on your hand.

**Dart:** The hummingbird will close its wings and fly to pierce a creature of your choice. It uses your modifiers and proficiencies.

name: Charm of Hearts
desc:
When pressing this heart-shaped charm against another person's chest, they must make a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw or be under the spell *calm emotions*.

Once this ability has been used, it cannot be used until the next dawn.

name: Piercing Arrow
desc:
This piercing arrow never stops moving forward up to the maximum range of your weapon.

Roll an attack for every creature as there are in a line, as the arrow moves through them. The attack and damage bonuses or conditions in effect for the first target also are in effect for every target thereafter.

Once used, its magical properties are lost until the next dawn.

name: Amulet of Radiance
desc:
Whenever you strike a critical hit, you can use your smite feature as if having used a second-level spell slot without using any resources.

Once used, you cannot use it again until a new dawn.

name: Hot N Cold
desc:
This magical greatsword has both fire and ice along each of its edges.

The sword does an additional 1d4 fire or 1d4 cold depending on which side struck a creature first.

Once a day, you can activate both sides, launching a 15 ft cone of swirling flames and ice from its tip. Every creature inside that cone must make a 16 DC dexterity save or take 2d6 fire and 2d6 cold damage. On a successful save, a creature takes half the damage.

name: Ruby's Ring of Duplicity
desc: While wearing this ring if you fail a concentration check to maintain an Illusion spell you can reroll the d20 and must use the new roll. Once successful, it cannot be used again until you finish a long rest.

name: Crimson Flame Brazier
desc:
This brazier produces a striking crimson flame when lit and is small enough to fit in your hand.

This brazier allows one who knows the Find Familiar spell to cast it as an action without using a spell slot. In addition, a familiar created with this Brazier gains temporary hit points equal to 3 times your proficiency bonus. Finally, the material component cost is reduced by half. This brazier can be used in this manner two times per day. It behaves as a standard brazier for any additional uses until the next dawn.

name: Ruby's Goading Grin
desc:
This small iron pendant has the curious appearance of a grinning feline's teeth. 

While wearing this necklace, you can give an enemy disadvantage on the next Wisdom saving throw they make against your Goading Attack. If they are still successful in making their saving throw your Superiority Die is refunded. Once used, it cannot be used again until you finish a short rest.

name: Ruby's Holy Symbol
desc:
A small coin bearing the face of Tymora surrounded by several shamrocks. It seems almost magnetic, sticking easily to any weapon or armor.

When you roll a 1 or a 2 on a healing die for a healing spell you cast, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll, even if the new roll is a 1 or a 2. The spell must incapable of targeting more than one creature to benefit from this item.

name: Crimson Bulwark
desc:
A kite shield with a solid crimson appearance.

While holding this shield you have advantage on saving throws against Enchantment spells or when resisting the Charmed condition.

name: Baneful Brew
desc:
A small bottle containing a sanguine liquid that smells and tastes of roses with a hint of honey. 

After imbibing this brew you may permanently target one additional enemy with the Bane spell, providing the target meets all other requirements of the spell.

name: Freezing Rune  Corruption
desc:
The *Freezing Rune* has been corrupted with V'allurs energy as a result, the following properties apply while attuned:

Once per long rest, you can use a bonus action to activate its Eldritch properties, gaining the following for one hour:

- All damage done with a weapon afflicted by the Freezing rune can be changed to psychic or force damage.

- The weapon gains the thrown property  (30/60). The weapon returns to your hand after every attack.

- **Blinding Blast:** As a bonus action, once per long rest, you can cause of a blast of light. Every creature of your choice in a 60ft radius must succed a Constitution Save DC 18 or be blinded for a minute. A creature can repeat this save at the end of its turn to stop the condition.

name: Hagsbane Corruption
desc:
The weapon has been corrupted with V'allurs energy, as a result, the following properties apply while attuned:

* You gain a +1d4 psychic damage to your damage rolls.

* You gain flight and hovering speed equal to your walking speed as long as you are holding the weapon.

* **Gravity Shockwave:**  Once per long rest, you can shift your gravity as an action. Choose a spot within 120ft that you can see. You can rise to the skies and then fall down like a comet onto that spot, creating a shockwave of force and eldritch energy as your weapon hits the ground. Every creature in a 30 ft radius has to make a Dexterity Saving Throw DC 18, taking 6d8 force and 6d8 psyichc damge or half damage on a success.

* Once per long rest, you can add a +10 bonus to one attack.

* As a bonus action, once per long rest, you can activate the weapon's eldritch energy, gaining the following properties for one minute: 

     - Your attacks can strike creatures on the Ethereal Plane and ignore half-cover
     - You gain 15 ft extra movement
     - Creatures of your choice that end their turn within ten feet of you take 2d6 psychic damage.

name: Hot and Loud's Corruption
desc:
The weapon has been corrupted with V'allurs energy, as a result, the following properties apply while attuned:

* You gain a +1 bonus to bonus rolls and 1 psychic damage to damage rolls.

 * Whenever you roll a natural twenty roll on an attack roll with this weapon, you do an extra 1d6 radiant, necrotic, force, psychic, and piercing damage. 

* Twice per long rest you can bathe a bolt in chaos energy. An enemy that is hit with chaos energy explodes in a 10 ft radius around it at the end of its turns, taking 4d6 damage of random elemental damage. The effects last for a minute.

* Once per long rest you can attempt to link two creatures within two hundred feet of you. Each creature must make a Constitution saving throw, DC 19, rolled at a disadvantage if they are within 30 ft of each other. If both fail, they are linked for one hour. Any effect you cause on one creature, it is caused on the other one as well.

*  As a bonus action, you can activate the weapons' eldritch energy, for one minute, it is affected with aberrant energy and strong chronomancy:

      - As part of your attack, you can fire a second bolt.
     -  You do an extra 1d6 force damage.
      -  Your critical roll range expands from 18-20. 
      -  Your attack rolls have advantage.

name: Blood-Drinker's Corruption
desc:
The weapon has been corrupted with V'allurs energy as a result, the following properties apply while attuned:

  * Blood-drinker can take the shape of any other basic weapon you decide

Once you have done 300 damage with Bloodrinker, you can activate its Eldritch Path as a free action. Black tendrils wrap around the sword. For a minute, you gain the following properties:

- Your strikes do an extra 1d4 psychic damage.
- As a reaction, when you are hit, you can cause tendrils to emerge and stop the attack, gaining an extra 3 ac.

name: Ring of Flowing Time
desc:
Black sand swirls inside the gem of this ring. As a bonus action, you can break the gem to release its magic, changing time itself.

Once used, you return to the state you were at the turn before. Your position, hitpoints, and status effects all reverse to those at the start of the previous turn.

Once used, the ring's magic disappears for a week, slowly reforming with time.

name: Order of the Chromata Lapel Pin
desc:
A magical gold, eye-shaped lapel pin.

As an action, you can see through the eye of the lapel pin as if it were your senses.

name: Order of the Chromata Clothes
desc:
While wearing these clothes you gain the following benefits:

- You gain 5 ft of extra movement.
- Once per long rest, you can use the clothes magic to impose a disadvantage on an attack as a reaction. It must be done before the attack is rolled.
- You can withstand extreme high and low temperatures ( -50/150 F).

name: Vial of Transmutation
desc:
This magical vial contains energy created during V'allur's death, and it transmutes objects it touches.

If you apply this coating to two magical items of the same kind, their properties mix as the items become one. The following rules apply:

 - The items must occupy the same slot (Ring+Ring, Armor+Armor, etc)
- It cannot be weapons or any item that can be used to make an attack roll.
-  If at least one of the items requires attunement, the new item will require attunement as well.
- If the items possess the same effects, the one that provides the higher one will be kept, they are not cumulative. (+1 AC + 2 AC = +2 AC)

There is enough in one vial to mix two magical items into one.

name: Curse of the Far Realms
desc:
Your experience with far realm entities has remained with you. As a result, you will often see things where there are none, hear whispers when no one is speaking, and smell strange odors when you close your eyes. Strange and abhorrent creatures watch from corners before disappearing, eyes watch you from inanimate objects, and strange visual phenomena occur in the skies.

Nightmares might come and go, some feel as real as life itself. The feeling that someone is following you, watching you, that something is about to happen, that it is wrong. Creatures of smoke, strange sensations on your skin, indescribable events, moments of chaos.

It manifests at random intervals, perhaps minutes, days, months, or even years apart. Perhaps small episodes, or longer ones of some that are more intense. However it rears its head, this experience has shifted something within you.

name: Dimensional Split
desc:
You have been temporarily imbued with far realm energy, time and reality shifts within you.

As a result, you can change any subclass ability that allows you to choose an option: Fighting Style, Spell Selection, Rites, and the like. You also gain the ability to change or replace any feat or ASI taken.

You cannot change your race, class, subclasses, level-split, size, or base ability scores.

name: Gravity Shard
desc:
As an action, you can throw this shard into an empty spot within two hundred feet of you and create a small black hole. The hole pulls all creatures within 60 ft who fail a strength saving throw DC 21 to the closest empty space of its epicenter. 

Once used, the shard is destroyed.

name: The Curse of The Chromatic Eyes: Crimson
desc:
The crimson eyes grant its carrier the gifts of magical perception, as a result, you gain the following benefits:
 
   - You learn the *detect magic, detect evil and good, detect poison and disease, and detect thoughts* spells and can cast them at will.
- You cannot be surprised and gain advantage to perception checks.
- Three times per long rest, you can cast the *true seeing* spell.
- Once a day you can cast the *Scrying* spell.
- Once per short rest, you can perceive creatures' and objects' weak points like crimson fractures across them for thirty minutes. As a result you can: 
  - Slice through any nonmagical object that is large or smaller. If the object is bigger, you create a large-sized gash on its surface.
   -  Create a Bleeding Wound on a target. At the start of each of their turns, the target takes an additional 1d10 damage per wound. You can only create one Bleeding Wound per turn per creature.

name: The Curse of The Chromatic Eyes: Golden
desc:
The golden eyes grant its carrier the gifts of magical traveling, as a result, you gain the following benefits:

- You learn the *misty step* spell and can cast it at will.
- You have advantage on dexterity saves against effects that you can see.
- Twice per long rest, you can cast the *teleport* spell.
- Twice per long rest, you can cast the *dimension door* spell.
- One per long rest, you can cast the *planeshift* spell.
- Once per long rest, you can cast *word of recall* spell,  randomly leading you to one of the Order doors scattered across the lands.
- Once per long rest, as a reaction to being hit, you can gain resistance to the attack and teleport to a spot you can see withing 60 ft of you.

name: The Curse of The Chromatic Eyes: Azure
desc:
The azure eyes grant its carrier the gifts of magical protection, as a result, you gain the following benefits:

- You learn the spell *armor of Agathys* and can cast it at will. The spell is cast at its 3rd level.
- Twice per short-rest, you can cast *absorb elements* spell.
- Every day you can choose a creature to be under the effect of the *deathward* spell.
- Once per long rest, you can cast the *circle of power* spell.
- Once per long rest, you can absorb any damage taken and store it within you. You suffer no damage and your next successful attack will do extra damage equal to half the absorbed amount.
- Once per long rest, you can use a bonus action to activate the eyes magic and gain resistance to all damage for one minute.

name: The Curse of The Chromatic Eyes: Onyx
desc:
The Onyx eyes grant its carrier the gifts of magical entrapment, as a result, you gain the following benefits: 

 -  You learn the *earthbound* spell, can target up to three creatures, and cast it at will.
- Once per long rest, you can cast the *flesh to stone* spell.
- Once per long rest, you can cast the *wall of force* spell.
- Once per long rest, you can cast the *forcecage* spell.
- Once per long rest, you can attempt to entrap a creature into an extraplanar dimension. As an action, you can designate a creature huge or smaller to be trapped in another dimension through a black hole, it must succeed a strength 18 DC saving throw or be sucked into it.

name: The Chromata Initiation Kit
desc:
Each Kit has the following items in them:

- Order of The Chromata Lapel Pin
- Order of The Chromata Clothes
- Lantern of Revealing 
- Lanter of Tracking: Aberrations
- Ring o Mind Shielding
- Helm of Comprehending Languages
- Googles of Night
- Periapt of Health
- Portable Hole

name: Fool's Choice
desc:
This little hourglass-shaped charm holds a starry substance inside. As a reaction, when you see a creature within 200 ft of you die, you can decide to die for them instead.

The creature you save instead regains all its hit points and is transported to the closest safe space.

name: Forceful ring
desc: Once per long rest, you can activate this ring to cast the magic missile spell, with the number of missiles being equal to your proficiency bonus

name: Band of Stardust
desc: Once per long rest, before you attack, you can shower a creature within ten feet of you in stardust. The attack is done with advantage, and if it hits they take an extra 4d6 radiant damage. If the attack misses, the creature instead takes 2d6 radiant damage.

name: Bottle of Chronal Respite
desc:
Drinking this potion makes it feel as if you had rested for hours in mere minutes. You gain the effects of a long rest if you rest for ten minutes.

This long rest does not restart Item charges.

name: Rose Stars
desc: These rose-shaped stars can be used to heal your wounds. Each rose consumed heals 20 hit points.

name: Deed: Bellbroke Castle
desc:
A medium sized-castle over-looking the beautiful Southern Avian coasts. It is magically protected and warded. What once was a minor base of operations for the Chromata Order.

As thank you for your role in defeating V'allur, the Order has gifted the castle to the group.

name: Frostguard Bulwark
desc:
This frosty tower shield is carved from a single slab of rock. A layer of rime coats its face, and the three-triangular symbol of a mountain peak is chiseled in its center. Avalanche privately refers to the shield as Tsinchal'guvauravekiola'quetol, "The Gentle Snowfall that Blankets Magic to a Dull Whisper.”

While attuned to this shield, you gain resistance to fire and cold damage. You also are unaffected by difficult terrain caused by ice and snow, and you can withstand freezing weather conditions without issue. 

While holding this shield, you have advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects, and spell attacks have disadvantage against you.

Additionally, this shield can carry 5 charges. It gains one charge whenever you take damage from a hostile source (only once per turn). For every 10 minutes you spend out of combat, the shield loses one charge.

**Rime Shards.** A creature that hits you with a melee attack takes cold damage equal to the number of charges accumulated, as the ice splinters and ricochets magically back at your attacker.

**Frost Shielding.**  Whenever you take fire, cold, or piercing damage, reduce the total damage you take by the number of charges accumulated.
 
**Avalanche (3+ charges).** When you take damage, you can use your reaction to expend some or all of the shield's charges. A torrent of snow erupts out in a 60-ft cone in front of you, turning the area into difficult terrain for 1 minute. Each creature in the area must make a DC 17 Strength saving throw, taking 2d6 cold damage for each charge expended, or half as much on a success. On a failure, a creature is also restrained by the snow until it uses its action to free itself.

name: Mask of Expression
desc: The Mask is shaped in the likeness of a face. While wearing it you can use a bonus action to alter the face’s expression.

name: Blowdart of Holding
desc:
This feathered dart is fashioned with a trinket in the shape of a pouch. The dart has a range of 60ft.

Whenever you hit a medium-sized or smaller (5x5) object that is not being worn or carried, the object will be stored inside its pouch, changing the feathering from red to green, and returning the dart to the person who fired it. When the dart is fired next, it will release the item in the closest uncopied space where it was fired.

The dart can only hold one object at a time.

name: Shards of Lighting
desc: These natural shards of lighting still hold the energy from a storm.As an action, you can shatter them in a spot within 10 ft of you. It creates a 20 ft long five-foot-wide line of lighting as it releases its energy from within. Each creature on that line must succeed a Dexterity Saving Throw DC 15 or take 4d6 lightning damage.

name: Red Guardian Frog Gem
desc: Once a day, as a bonus action you can target a creature within 60 ft of you to focus their anger on you. You can activate the frog gem's magic, and they must make a DC 16 Wisdom Saving throw. On a failure, the creature can only attack you on their next turn.

name: Green Healer Frog Gem
desc: Once per day, whenever a die is being rolled for healing, you can activate the frog gem's magic and add an additional healing die to the roll.

name: Yellow Savior Frog Gem
desc: Once a day, when you see a creature within 60 ft of you suffer damage, you can activate the frog gem magic and use your reaction to halve their damage and suffer half of that damage instead.

name: Blue Sage Frog Gem
desc: Once a day, whenever you roll an intelligence check to know information, you can activate the frog gem's magic and gain triple advantage to the roll.

name: Mosquito Pin
desc: This gold mosquito pin emits a sound inaudible to most creatures. While wearing it, you repel all small mosquitoes. Larger mosquito creatures have disadvantage to attack you.

name: Sack of Goodberry Raisins
desc: Four dried and cured, magic infused goodberry raisins rest in this pouch. A creature can use its action to eat one raisin. Eating a berry restores 1 hit point.

name: Shards of the Madness Gem
desc:
This sharp orange gemshard contains raw, untapped madness. As an action, it can be thrown at the ground, releasing its madness and allowing you to cast the following spells based on the roll of a 1d6:

1: Vicious Mockery
2: Mind Sliver
3: Dissonant Whispers
4: Tasha’s Hideous Laughter
5: Crown of Madness
6: Sleet Storm

The spells use a spell save DC 15. After the shard is shattered, its magic does not return.

name: Tear of the Shaed
meta:
*Woundrous item, very rare*

Attunement
*Requires attunement by a (former) Shaed*
desc:
This teardrop-shaped gem looks black on the surface, but when tilted reveals a myriad of colors underneath. It can be worn inside a ring, along the neck, or as a piercing.

While wearing the Tear, you gain the following benefits:
 - When casting a spell using ki points, you can choose to spend an additional ki point to cast the spell as a bonus action.
- The wearer learns the *Light* and *Message* cantrips.
- When hitting with an Unarmed Strike, you can choose to change the damage type of your strike to piercing, slashing, necrotic or radiant damage.
- When using a ki point to take the Dodge action, you can choose to move up to half your movement speed (rounded down) immediately.
- When you turn invisible using the Cloak of Shadows feature, you gain resistance to slashing, piercing and bludgeoning damage.
- When using the Diamond Soul feature, you have advantage on your reroll.

name: Shadow's Mark
meta:
*Wondrous item, Very Rare*

Attunement
*Requires attunement by someone who spent more than a century in the Shadowfell.*
desc:
Your long exposure to the Plane of Shadows set in motion certain bodily changes. This can manifest in an intracate mark on your arms, back or head.


While the Mark is on your body, you gain the following benefits:
- When summoning your Echo, it gains health equal to twice your level.
- The range of your echo extends to 45 ft.
- You gain one spell slot, which can be used for any spell you have in your spellbook. When using this spell slot, it is cast at the level which is necessary for the spell. You regain this spellslot at dusk. 
 - When you inflict a creature with your Hexblade's Curse, you regain health equal to 1d10+your Charisma modifier.
 - You gain resistance to radiant damage.

name: Potion of the Dark
desc:
This potion has a swirling black shadow inside its purple liquid. 

When you drink the potion, you gain resistance to radiant damage for 1 minute. Additionally, in that minute, when you are in bright light, you can cast the *invisibility* spell once, without components.

name: Threaded Bolts
desc:
This bolt has a knitted, shimmering bell on a string at the end of the bolt. 

If you hit a creature with this bolt, until the end of your next turn you are always aware of their position by the sight and sound of this bell. These bells do not turn invisible, even if the creature they are stuck in does.

name: Trancing Incense
desc:
You can use an action to lit this incense.  It lasts a minute after being lit,  and its smoke is lined with ancient herbs.

Whenever lit, an area of 10 ft around you becomes inundated with the incense's smoke and smell. A creature inside the smoke must make a Constitution saving throw DC 15 or become incapacitated. On a successful save, the creature is immune to the incense's effects for one minute.

The incapacitated creature remains incapacitated as long as they are inside the incense's smoke or they take damage. Once, woken from the trance, they are immune to the incense's effects for one minute.

Once lit the incense loses its medicinal properties.

name: Magic Sand Pebbles
desc:
Whenever you cast a spell, you can use one of these pebbles as part of the casting. By doing so, you double the spells' range.

Once the magic has been used, the pebble turns into a mundane rock.

name: Amulet of the Gravestriker
desc: Once per day as a reaction, when you kill an undead creature, you can gain 3d4+2+PB Hit Points.

name: Mourning Gold Coins
desc: As long as these gold coins rest upon the eyes of a dead creature, the creature becomes consecrated and cannot be raised as undead.

name: Quill of Secrets
desc:
This magical quill is often used by spies and secretive kinds. A message written by such begins to slowly fade almost immediately.

A creature must make an Intelligence check equal to the number of hours since it has been written to determine its contents.

After 24 hours the contents have disappeared entirely.

name: Heating Stones
desc: These polished stones keep the warmth from the sunlight and other heating sources in them, providing comfortable heated surfaces to rest.

name: Sibilant Black Lotus
desc:
This gem-like fragment has the shape of a black Lotus, forever hissing as if hundreds of snakes were kept inside.

Any creature that dies within 10 Miles of the Sibilant Black Lotus has its soul trapped inside the gem. A creature who has not been resurrected in the following twelve days after death and whose soul is trapped within the Lotus raises as an undead. 

A person attuned to the Sibilant Black Lotus gains the following benefits:

- Advantage to Death Saving Throws
- Ability to manipulate or release any soul locked within it for any purpose.
- Control of all undead raised by the Lotus.

name: Spiked Spiked Pick
desc:
This magical War Pick has a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

As a bonus action, you can activate its magic to create poison-spiked spikes all over its head, for one minute you gain the following benefits:

Your attacks gain a  +2 slashing damage.

Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack using this magic weapon, you can create a poisoned wound on the target. At the start of each of the wounded creature's turns, it takes 1d6 poison damage for each time you've wounded it, and it can then make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw, ending the effect of all such wounds on itself on a success.

Once activated, you cannot do so again until the new dawn.

name: Poisonshard Arrowheads
desc:
These arrowheads can be used with normal arrows. When you hit a creature or spot with one of these arrows, the shard breaks, releasing a cloud of poisonous gas in a 10 ft radius.

Every creature inside it must make a DC 15 Constitution Saving Throw or take 4d6 Poison Damage.

name: Magic-Bound Poison Orb
desc: Thrice a day, when you cast a spell that deals damage, you can use a charge of this orb of magically entuned poison to infuse your strikes with it.  You do an additional 1d4+PB poison damage with the spell.

name: Venom Coating Mechanism
desc:
This small venom activator has a tiny compartment that can be loaded with up to two coatings of venom transmitted through wounds. It takes a minute to load them up safely inside.

As part of an attack, you can activate this mechanism to coat the length of the blade with the poison and gain its benefits.

name: Chocolates of Poisoned Defense
desc:
Once ingested, your blood and skin become venomous and poisonous for one hour.

Harmful and deadly to anyone who is not immune or resistant to poison. A creature who lacks both takes 1d6 poison damage per minute per the duration.

Any creature that strikes you with a melee attack takes 1d4 poison damage. Similarly, any creature who grapples, restrains or swallows you with a body part takes 1d6 poison damage at the end of their turn if the contact persists.

name: The World's Tiniest Dulcimer
desc: This very very very very very tiny dulcimer can be played once a day to cast the reduce effect of the enlarge/reduce spell as if you had cast the spell.

name: Amulet of Ice and Fire
desc: Once a day,  you can use your bonus action to gain resistance to fire or cold until the end of the next round. This can be used as a reaction to damage of either kind.

name: Arrow of Charm
desc:
This arrow has a pink heart-shaped arrowhead. If you hit a humanoid with it, the person takes no damage and instead must make a Wisdom DC 15 save or be charmed by you for one hour or until you or your companions do anything harmful to it.

During this time, the charmed humanoid regards you as an adoring fan would their favorite star. Little hearts float around the person's head for the duration.

Once used, the arrow loses its magic until the start of a new dawn.

name: Stick of Destiny
desc:
This magical quarterstaff looks like any quarterstaff. If you point it at someone and mutter " We both knew it had to come to this", the quarterstaff sets on fire for one minute, gaining a 1d4 fire damage to the attacks.

You can use your casting ability for attack and damage rolls with this weapon.

name: Ice Skating Boots
desc: For winning the 1425 Avian Amateur National Ice-Skating Competition. While wearing these boots, ice blades appear whenever you touch frozen ice. Your speed doubles as long as you skate around on these.

name: Bracelet of the Snow Travelers
desc: For winning the 1425 Avian National Ice-Skating Combat Competition. As long as you wear this bracelet, you are unbothered by any difficult terrain caused by ice or snow.

name: Qlerk's Famous Candy
desc:
A character who eats Miss Qlerk's nearly inedible, and most certainly stale candy becomes slightly nauseous for 24 hours. While nauseous in this way the character suffers no drawbacks and is immune to later becoming Poisoned from any other source for the duration. 

The character is not immune to poison damage. There is only enough candy for one dose

name: Amulet of Elemental Control
desc:
A grimy, scratched up ruby cut into the shape of an eye is set in the middle of this tarnished golden amulet.

This amulet carries 3 charges and regains all its charges when exposed to a Prime Elemental Locus. As an action, you can hold up the amulet and expend one charge to speak its command word. When you do so, any elemental of CR 7 or lower within 15 ft. of you must make a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or become charmed by you for 1 minute, or until you or your companions do anything harmful to it. While charmed, the elementals are friendly to you and your companions. When the duration ends, the elementals know that they were charmed.

When you use this feature, you can choose to commit concentration to maintaining the effect. If you do so, the charm lasts until you lose concentration, or up to 24 hours.

name: Goldenvein's Companionate
desc:
This golden scarf is alive. It has a good alignment and is bonded with Pimeryn Goldenvein, and will only listen to his commands.

This scarf can magically extend up to 30 feet long and weighs 3 pounds. If you hold one end of the scarf and use an Action to speak its Command word, the other end darts forward to entangle a creature you can see within 20 feet of you. The target must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or become restrained.

You can release the creature by using a bonus action to speak a second Command word. A target restrained by the rope can use an action to make a DC 15 Strength or Dexterity check (target's choice). On a success, the creature is no longer restrained by the scarf.

Once on each of your turns as a bonus Action, you can mentally Command the scarf to move up to 15 feet and interact with an object. The scarf can perform simple tasks, such as fetching things, grabbing levers, serving food, cleaning surfaces, and pouring wine. Once you give the Command, the scarf performs the task to the best of its ability until it completes the task, then waits for your next Command.

The scarf has AC 20 and 20 Hit Points and a Strength of 2. It cannot attack. It has a flying speed of 30 feet if commanded to move. It regains 1 hit point every 5 minutes as long as it has at least 1 hit point. If the scarf drops to 0 Hit Points, it becomes inert and no properties can be used until it has had a short rest or *mending* has been cast on it to restore 5 hit points.

name: Map of the Worlds
desc:
This large stone astrolabe seems to be etched with runic symbols beyond your comprehension.

When you first attune to this artifact...

*No amount of divination seems to be able to reveal the purpose of this item.*

name: Ooze Blade
desc:
This polished golden sword hilt seems to be missing a blade entirely. When it’s grasped, though, semi-solid acid springs forth and hardens into a wicked edge.

You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, which may deal acid damage instead of its normal type.

If you are attuned to this blade, you can control whether the blade materializes or not when you hold it.

**Malleable Form (Attune).** You can alter some of the blade’s physical shape to better suit your needs. At the start of your first turn in combat, you can grant the blade one additional property of your choice: Finesse, Reach, Light, or Thrown (20/60). Alternatively, you can grant it a bonus 1d6 acid damage on a hit.

name: Revenant's Tidebreaker (Damaged)
desc:
This ancient hammer was found floating in the Astral Sea. It’s made of stone cool to the touch, and runic inscriptions along its faces reveal it once belonged to a legendary dwarven king from ages ago.

You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and it deals an additional 1d6 cold damage on a hit.

**Channeled Might.** While you’re attuned to this weapon, you take on the strength of ancient dwarves – your grow stronger and sturdier, and your beard, if you have one, grows fuller. You gain the following benefits:
- Your Strength score becomes 23.
- You gain resistance to poison damage, and you have advantage on saves against poison.
- Your maximum hit points increase by 1 point for every two levels you have, rounded down.

**Absolution.** As an action, you can summon forth a large spectral hammer at a location up to 60 ft. away that you can see, where it slams the ground in a burst of cold mist. Each creature in a 20-ft. radius sphere must make a DC 17 Strength saving throw, taking 4d6 bludgeoning and 4d6 cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. On a failure, a creature is also knocked prone.

Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

**Arcane Dampener.** It appears tampering from Pelu has damaged this object, blocking its true potential and risking irreparable damage. Perhaps there is a way to fix it…?

name: Noble Sacrifice (Dormant)
meta: *Wondrous item, Very Rare (requires attunement by a wizard or a sorcerer)*
desc:
This ruined grimoire once belonged to Otsdarva, before it gave itself up to save him within Limbo. The book is riddled with tears and scorch marks, and has been rendered otherwise completely unusable as a spellbook. If you put your ear up to its cover, however, you can hear a faint rhythmic ticking from somewhere within, almost like a weak heartbeat.

You can use this book as a spellcasting focus for your spells, and you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your wizard spells.

Additionally, you have the spells *Haste* and *Slow* known and prepared. These spells do not count against your known and prepared spells, and they are both wizard and sorcerer spells for you. Once per long rest, you can cast one of these spells from the grimoire without expending a spell slot.

This book has 12 charges and regains 1 charge every hour. The magic infused within allows you to manipulate the timestream when casting your spells, although such tampering comes with its own drawbacks. Whenever you activate one of these features, you can expend 2 charges to use it as written, or 3 charges to nullify the negative effects.

- **Hasten Spell.** When you cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action or 1 bonus action, you can quicken the spell by overlapping the casting with your future self. The casting time of the spell becomes 1 bonus action or 1 reaction respectively. On your next turn, you cannot cast any spells higher than 2nd level.

- **Delay Burden.** When you cast a spell that requires concentration, you can delay the onset of it upon your mind. Until the start of your next turn, you do not have to concentrate on the spell, and the spell persists as though you were. Once your concentration begins, you suffer a -3 penalty to checks made to maintain the spell, as the burden catches up to you.

- **Fast Forward.** When you cast a spell as a ritual, you can send yourself hurtling forward in time. The spell is cast with its normal casting time, rather than adding 10 minutes to it. Afterwards, you must succeed a DC 18 Constitution saving throw or suffer 1 level of exhaustion.

name: Acid Gauntlet
desc:
This tarnished gauntlet has a number of metal tubes running all down its length.

The gauntlet is a special type of simple melee weapon that does not occupy your hand, allowing you to hold other items. It deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage on a hit. 

Additionally, you can make unarmed strikes with your gloved fist, which are considered to be weapon attacks.

You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. It also carries 5 charges and regains 1 charge every hour. If you are attuned to the gauntlet, you can use the charges to fuel its features.

**Infuse Strike.** Whenever you hit an attack with the gauntlet, you can expend one charge to deal an additional 2d6 acid damage to your target.

**Procure Vial.** As a bonus action, you can expend one charge to create a single vial of acid in your gauntleted hand. This vial carries no monetary value.

**Corrosive Spray.** As an action, you can expend 3 charges to spray acidic fluid in a 15-ft. cone in front of you. Any creature caught in the spray must succeed a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take 4d6 acid damage and have its AC reduced by 2. Incorporeal creatures are immune to this effect.

name: Volcanic Soul (Damaged)
desc:
This fiery carnelian orb is almost painfully hot to the touch. You must attune to the orb by holding it in both hands, upon which the orb melts into a liquid that absorbs into you. If you unattune, it reforms into an orb and drops to the ground besides you.

**Gift of Magma.** While you are attuned to the orb, your body becomes quite warm to the touch. You gain the following benefits:
- You have resistance to fire damage.
- You are unaffected by damage caused by environmental fire.
- You can move through magma and lava as though you were swimming, and you take no damage from being submerged within.
- You are unaffected by the detrimental effects of hot and cold weather extremes.

**Volcanic Form.** As a bonus action, you can undergo an elemental transformation. Your skin becomes cracked with rock and veins of red-hot magma, and your eyes glow with an intense orange light. The transformation lasts for 10 minutes, or until you use a bonus action to end it early.

While transformed in this way, you gain the following additional benefits:
- Your melee weapon attacks and unarmed strikes deal an additional 1d8 fire damage on a hit.
- You gain immunity to fire damage.
- Any creature that starts its turn within 10 ft. of you or moves there for the first time on a turn takes 1d8 fire damage.

Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

**Arcane Dampener.** It appears tampering from Pelu has damaged this object, blocking its true potential and risking irreparable damage. Perhaps there is a way to fix it...?

name: Oozebinder Soul (Damaged)
desc:
This shiny flourite orb is cool to the touch, but it seems to almost burn your skin. You must attune to the orb by holding it in both hands, upon which the orb melts into a liquid that absorbs into you. If you unattune, it reforms into an orb and drops to the ground besides you.

**Conjure Fen.** As an action, you can target a point within 30 ft. of you that you can see and cause a bubbling mire to spill out of the ground in up to a 20-ft radius circle. This mire lasts for 1 hour and is difficult terrain. If a Large or smaller creature ends its turn fully within the mire, it sinks up to its waist and has its speed reduced to zero. A creature can use its action to extract itself from the mire.

You are unaffected by the mires. While you are standing in one, you can sink into the ooze and emerge from a different mire that you’ve created. The duration it takes to move depends on how far away the two mires are:
- Within 120 ft. –1 bonus action
- Within 600 ft. – 1 action
- Within 1 mile – 1 minute

You can have up to 3 mires established at one time. If you create a fourth, the oldest one dries up and disappears.

**Ooze-gift.** As an action, you can touch one creature and grant it a blessing which lasts until you use this feature again. A creature blessed in this way is unaffected by your mires, and it can travel between them in the same manner as yourself.

Once per long rest, you can use an action to grant this blessing to all creatures of your choice within 5 feet of you. When granted in this way, the blessing lasts for 1 minute.

**Arcane Dampener.** It appears tampering from Pelu has damaged this object, blocking its true potential and risking irreparable damage. Perhaps there is a way to fix it...?

name: Earthen Shard Spear
desc:
This is a sliver of razor-sharp rock that’s been magically affixed to an onyx shaft. The rock seems to crumble at the slightest touch, although it never really loses any mass.

This weapon is a martial version of the spear that loses its Versatile trait but deals 1d8 base damage. You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

Whenever you score a critical hit against a creature with this spear, you reduce its speed by 10 ft. until the end of its next turn.

**Shrapnel Strike (Attune).** When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can cause the outer edges of the blade to explode outwards in a hundred shards of stone. If the creature is capable of bleeding, it begins to do so. While bleeding, that creature has its speed reduced by 10 ft. and takes 1d8 necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns until it uses an action to end the bleeding or 1 minute passes.

Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you have taken a short or long rest.

name: Coin Purse Ring
desc:
Whenever you touch this ring signet to a gold coin, it vanishes inside it. As an action, you can speak a command word and make any amount of gold coins within it appear on your hand or a espace within ten feet of you.

The Ring can hold a maximum of ten thousand gold coins.

name: Sketchbook of Monstrosities
desc:
This sketchbook has several pages with sketchbooks of monstrosities. You can use an action to rip one of its pages, summoning such monstrosity. The monstrosity rules its own initiative during combat and follows your command.

After one hour, the creature vanishes. Once a page has been ripped, it is gone from the sketchbook.

The sketchbook has the following creatures sketched:  Griffon, Grick, Owlbear, Peryton, Banderhoob, Gorgo, Bulette, Chimera, Hydra,  Behir.

No more than one of the sketched creatures can materialize at the same time.

name: Good-Health Ale
desc: Drinking this ale gives you ten temporary hit points.

name: Scorched Earth
desc:
This vial has a dancing little flame inside. As an action, you can throw it anywhere within 90 feet of you.


The vial will break in use,  and a small bead of fire will float where it broke. At the start of your next turn, the bead will explode in flames.

Every creature within a forty feet radius must make a DC 20 dexterity save. On a failure, the creature takes 1d100 fire damage. On a success, the creature takes half the damage.

name: Little Guardian
desc:
This little white glowing orb of light is a construct that seeks attention, love, and care. It floats within ten feet of its owner, often rubbing against them to show appreciation.

While the orb floats around you, you can use a bonus action to release some of the love and attention that has been given:

- **Heal:** The orb can heal you or a creature five feet from you, using as many possible heal dice as you wish, given how many it has left.

- **Light:** The orb creates bright light in a 30 ft cone and dim light in an additional 30 ft.

- **Protect:** Once a day, the orb can reduce all instances of damage equal to the roll on one of its healing die, which it consumes, until the start of your next turn.

- **Restore:** The orb can cure you or a creature within five feet of you from one of the following: diseased, blinded, deafened, stunned, poisoned, paralyzed, charmed, or frightened. It costs three healing dice.

The Orb has a pool of eight 1d8 heal die. It does not require food, drink, or sleep and cannot communicate other than by short sounds.

name: Boots of  Double Jump
desc: While wearing these boots, you can jump a second time in the middle of the air after having jumped the first time. You gain an additional maximum of ten feet to the second jump if you go over your normal speed.

name: Raven Amulet
desc:
As an action, while wearing this amulet, you can turn into a raven. As a bonus action, you can reverse to your normal form.

As a raven, you keep your hit points and mental statistics, all other attributes are replaced with that of a raven. You cannot also activate a non-passive magic item during raven form, or benefit from a magic item that requires you to be a humanoid creature able to wear such, other than this amulet.

name: Ray Gun
desc:
This light weapon fires radiant ammunition that does 3d6 radiant damage.

It has the ammunition and loading property and a range of 40/80.

name: Aberrant Coin
desc: This coin always falls on the side you bet on.

name: Forged Forgery Coupon
desc: This forged coupon grants you entrance to forgery classes. After two weeks of attending it, you gain proficiency with the forgery kit.

name: Psychic Blade
desc:
This hilt can be used to call forth a psychic blade. It has the statistics of a scimitar but does psychic damage instead. A creature must have a minimum of 10 intelligence to be able to call it forth.

The blade has the finesse property and can be used with your intelligence modifier as well.

name: Orb of Adherance
desc:
This orb can be thrown at a target as a ranged attack, on hit, it will adhere to them and glow purple. 

On a hit, and at the start of each of the turns of the creature it adhered to, the orb gains more energy, gaining one charge.

After five charges, or whenever you use a command option during one of your turns, it releases its psychic energy and falls, causing 1d6 psychic damage to the target per charge.

Once used the orb cannot be used again until a new dawn.

name: The Tempest's Song
desc: This lute bares the symbol of Tempus crossed with a familiar set of horns. Once per dawn, you can use your action to strum the strings on the lute, letting out a cacophony of sound that blasts out around you. Make a Performance check. You deal 2d6 thunder damage per 10 you roll (Min 2d6) to all targets in a 10ft radius.

name: Flower Power
meta: Requires Attunement by a Spellcaster capable of casting Illusion Magic
desc: Once per dawn, you can use your action to twist the flowers of this bracelet, causing petals to emit from you in a swirl of color. For the next hour, when you cast an illusion spell of 1st level or higher, any target attempting to see through it must make the check at disadvantage. In addition, when casting an Illusion spell that would create a creature to fulfill its effect, you can choose one creature equal to half the spell slot used or lower (Min CR 1/4). That creature is summoned in place of the illusion, and is under your control until the illusion would disappear, wherein the creature disappears immediately. You can only control one creature in this manner at a time.

name: Raiment of the Heavens
meta: Requires attunement by a Druid of the Circle of Stars
desc:
When you activate your Starry form, you can apply one of the following effects, each twice per long rest:
Celestial Protection: A celestial array forms around you. Until the start of your next turn, any attack against you is made at disadvantage.
Star's Might: You can cause all attacks you do to do an additional 1d8 of radiant damage, until the end of your next turn.
Boon of the Heavens: Choose targets within 30ft up to your Wisdom modifier. They are healed for 2d6 + your wisdom modifier.

name: Cloak of the Rageful Zealot
desc: This cloak carries a magical protection fueled by the rage of one who utilizes it. This item has 4 Charges, and regains 1d4+1 charges at dawn, and an additional charge when you activate your rage. When you are attacked with advantage after using your Reckless Attack feature, you can use a charge to remove the advantage granted by Reckless Attack (This does not affect Advantage gained from other sources.)

name: Firehair Horn Rings
desc: When you land an attack with your Hammering Horns feature and successfully knock your target back, you can use your reaction to immediately cast one cantrip with an applicable range. You can use this feature thrice per dawn.

name: Wolven Bracers of the Shifter
desc: Once per Dawn, You can activate the Wolven marking on these bracers with a bonus action. For the next minute, you can use your reaction to store any damage you take until the start of your next turn. When stored, the damage does not apply immediately, and is instead dealt all at once at the end of your following turn. You can only store damage three times this way.

name: Corrupted Amulet of the Crimson Tide
meta: Requires Attunement by a Rune Knight
desc: This amulet glows with a crimson energy mixed with necrotic influence. When you activate your Giant's Might feature, you can choose to activate the "Crimson Tide" state instead. Your size remains the same, you have advantage on strength checks and saving throws, your Giant's Might damage becomes necrotic, and you gain a resistance to Necrotic damage. You can only activate this feature once per long rest.

name: Circlet of Fey Aegis
desc: This circlet contains fey writing on it in a glowing green text, intermixed with a brown, wood-like texture. Once per dawn, you can activate this circlet's magic to summon a Feywild Aegis within 30ft for a minute. It appears as a faintly glowing green wisp with a shield-like form. Any friendly creature standing within a 10ft radius of the Aegis gains half-cover and the wisp provides light as though a 10ft/10ft dim torch. On each of your turns, you can use your bonus action to command the Aegis to move up to 30ft.

name: Runed Stone of the Azure Depths
meta: Requires Attunement by a Dwarf capable of spellcasting
desc: This stone glows a deep azure blue on it's runic symbols, highlighting the dark black rock containing them. When you attune to this item, you can attach it to one other non-magical item. If you have the find familiar spell, you can instead bind it to your familiar. While you have this item bound to you, you can activate The Azure Depths for an hour once per dawn. While The Azure Depths is active, you have an aura with a radius of 10ft that grants you and your allies a resistance to cold damage and allows movement over ground-based difficult terrain without a penalty. If this item is bound to a familiar, you can use a bonus action each turn to choose whether the aura appears around you or your familiar.

name: Turbat's Baby Wing
desc:
Retrieved from the Turbat found to the north of Corvus. A baby turbat's wings, which come off the first time they truly take flight.

Whenever you gain a flying speed, add 10ft to that speed while carrying this wing.

name: Flower of the Field
desc: This flower was picked from the birthplace of the Turbat. When worn in hair, it changes color based on the wearer's current mood.

name: King Tutchin Grace's Rotsteel Gauntlets
desc:
**Attunement**
*Requires Attunement by a Blood Hunter*

While wearing these rotted steel plated gauntlets you gain the following two abilities:

***Rimeguard.*** You may cast Armor of Agathys at the level of your highest level spell slot without expending a spell slot. Once used, this property can't be used until after finishing a long rest.

***Crimson Bulwark.*** Upon amplifying a Blood Curse, you also add the number rolled on the Hemocraft Die to your AC until the start of your next turn.

name: Mallegro's Juggling Ball
desc:
As an action, a creature can make a dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check to handle the juggling ball. On a roll over 15, the creature can activate its magic, causing several balls sheathed in elemental magic to appear as long as the magical ball is being juggled. 

When activated, and each turn for the next minute, you can use your bonus action to throw one of the balls into a spot you can see within 90 ft of you. A creature within 5 ft of this spot must make a dexterity saving throw DC 16 or take 2d6 elemental damage of your choice.

Any time you take damage you must repeat the dexterity (Sleight of Hand) Check DC 15 to keep juggling the balls. You need at least one free hand at all times to keep the effect of the ball going.

On a roll below a 15, the ball erupts into random elemental damage. As part of that action, you can throw that ball into a spot you can see within 30 ft of you. Any creature within 10 ft of it must make a dexterity saving throw or take 4d6 elemental damage.


Once the magical ball has been activated and its effects end, it cannot be activated until the start of a new day.

name: Deck of Magical Trick Cards
desc: While in your possession, you can activate the deck's magic. While activated, you can magically alter any of the cards to look like any other in the deck.

name: Amelia's Ballon
desc:
As an action, you can activate the magical balloon, which depicts Amelia's face. Any person holding onto it can raise up to sixty feet per turn vertically into the air. Releasing the balloon drops you down safely towards the ground at a rate of 100 feet. 

The balloon magically returns to your hand after one hour has passed.

name: Vol's Or Vel's Dagger
desc: This dagger always strikes where you wish it to strike as long as you are aiming at an object not worn or carried.

name: Fire Eater Circlet
desc:
While wearing this circlet you reduce all fire damage you take by 1.

Once it has reduced ten points of fire damage. You can use an action to release a 15ft cone of fire in front of you. Each creature must make a dexterity DC 15 save or take 4d6 fire damage.

name: Garden Matches
desc: This box contains 15 green matches. When struck and thrown into a free spot, a plant or tree up to forty feet tall will appear. The tree or plant provides a safe passage to climb or move through them without trouble.

name: Mr. Patches' Knight Piece
desc:
This spotted knight chess piece can be used to call a steed named Mr. Patches. Mr. Patches is a spotted Warhorse that is friendly to you. 

Mr. Patches lasts for three days or until you use an action to turn him into a chess piece again. Once used, Mr. Patches needs two days of rest before being summoned again.

name: Mantle of Selflessness
desc:
While wearing this mantle, any magical healing you provide to another creature heals for an extra 2 hit points.

A particular creature cannot benefit from the extra hit points more than five times per short rest, or ten per long rest.

name: Bottles of Color
desc: These bottles contain an iridescent liquid inside. Throwing them onto an object changes its color to one of your choice for 1d50 days.

name: Bee Glove
desc:
While wearing this hive-shaped glove you can summon a swarm of bees from your hand.

As an action, you can summon a swarm to sting a creature within 60 ft of you. The creature must make a dexterity saving throw DC based on your spellcasting modifier. On a failure, the creature takes 1d10 magical piercing damage and then the bees dissipate. 


The bee’s damage increases by 1d10 when you reach 5th level (2d10), 11th level (3d10), and 17th level (4d10).

name: Now You See Me .....
desc:
This magical dagger has a +2  bonus to attack and damage rolls. 

While attacking with this dagger, whenever you roll a 20 on the attack roll, you become invisible for one minute. 

While attacking with this dagger, whenever you roll a 1 on the attack roll, your target becomes invisible for one minute.

As a bonus action, you can activate the Dagger's Magical Ability. For the next thirty seconds, while invisible, any attack roll made with this dagger below a 9 counts as a 9 instead. 

As an action, you can cast the spell *invisibility* at fourth level.

Once a creature has become invisible by the dagger by rolling a 20 or 1, it cannot become invisible again in such a way until it has completed a short rest.

name: Pop Sickle
desc:
This sickle is made from a strange material and exudes cold. Fog seems to cling to the blade and no amount of heat or fire warms the weapon. However,  when in sunlight, it drips a sweet sticky substance along its surface.

This magical weapon has a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls. The weapon also deals an additional 1d6 cold damage.

In addition, the weapon has 6 charges, you can expend one charge as a bonus action to imbue the blade with freezing cold. 

While the blade is imbued this way, a creature struck by it who is concentrating on a spell must make an additional DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a sharp pain erupts in their head, which breaks concentration, and the creature is unable to cast any magic that requires concentration until the end of their turn.

In addition, while imbued with cold, you can expend a charge as a reaction whenever you make a saving throw to evade fire damage, granting you advantage on the roll. On a success, you can spend an extra charge to take none of the damage.

The weapon regains 1d6+2 charges at dawn, as long as it has spent at least one hour away from direct sunlight.

name: Messenger Hat
desc:
This magical hat has a nest on top of it, in which a sparrow named Piper magically appears.

While wearing this hat, the sparrow that lives on top of it can be targeted to use the *animal messenger* spell at third level.

Once used, Piper needs a minimum of two days of rest before being sent to deliver another message.

name: Circlet of Portals
desc:
This circlet has five charges. As a bonus action, you can create a small portal in a spot within 120 ft of you. A secondary portal forms in front of you. As long as both portals are open, you can attack or cast a spell as if you were at the location of the first portal by attacking through the one that forms near you.

These portals close at the end of your turn.  The circle gains 1d2+2 charges each dawn.

name: Rusty Nail of Secrets
desc:
If you stab someone with this rusty nail and sink it in their skin, the creature must make a DC 20 Charisma saving throw or feel compelled to tell you a secret they are keeping from you.

This rusty nail acts as a finesse simple weapon for the purpose of stabbing someone.

Once a creature has given you a secret, it will not be affected by the nail until a new dawn.

name: Bottled Wish
desc:
This bottle contains a splinter of the greatest arcane achievements a mage can reach: A piece of a *wish*  spell.

Anyone who opens the following gains the benefits of a minor wish. A minor wish can recreate any spell of 8th level or lower and cast it at any spell level slot, including ninth. 

A minor wish will also revert, break, or heal any curse, disease, or magical effect that might be affecting a creature. 

A minor wish can also be used for a specific ask, which the wish might or might not be able to accomplish. Either way, the bottle becomes empty after any of these uses.

name: Shrunken Heads Bracelet
desc:
This bracelet has several shrunken heads dangling from it. As an action, you can press one of the heads to cast one of the following spells:

*dissonant whispers (3rd level) , command (2nd level),  thaumaturgy, shatter (3rd level), thunderwave(3rd level) , suggestion.*

Each head casts a specific spell and once cast it does not regain its magic until a new dawn. The spells use a spell save 16 DC.

name: A Weird Child
desc:
A weird child-looking thing. Very pale, dark medium hair that falls over the forehead, vacant unblinking blue eyes. It does not respond to stimuli in any way but permanently stands where moved.

It breaths, but any other sign of life is not present. Particularly light for its size (3 ft).  Immune to all damage and spells that affect creatures or objects.

name: Giant Cleaver
desc:
This magical weapon has a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

While holding this weapon, you can use a bonus action to become giant-sized (Huge). This lasts for one hour or until you stop holding the weapon.

While in giant form, your size is immutable by any skill or magic. You gain an extra ten feet of range and advantage on strength checks and saving throws.

Your weapon grows with you, granting you an extra 3d4 damage and a a 10x10 hit area.

name: Nightmare Bear
desc:
This horrifying bear effigy seems to breathe at all times. A creature who hugs it gains advantage against being frightened for that day.

If the effigy goes one day without being hugged, it will give nightmares to all creatures who have once hugged it until it is hugged again. A creature who receives these nightmares receives no benefit from a long rest and gains one point of exhaustion.

name: Black Sand Hourglass
desc:
You can use this item to cast the *haste* or *slow*  spell. 

Once a spell has been cast it cannot be cast again until a new dawn.

name: Dwarf Killer
desc:
This magical axe has a +1d6 lighting bonus to its damage rolls.

Once a day, while holding this weapon, you can send a blast of lighting as a bonus action. A creature you can see within 90 ft of you must make a dexterity saving throw DC 16 or take 6d8 lightning damage.

Whenever you strike a Dwarf with this Axe, you do an extra 2d10 thunder damage and magically destroy their beard.

name: Storm Piercer
desc:
This piece of ammunition has two uses:

- It can be shot at the sky during a storm, at which point any nonmagical  storm will stop in the following ten minutes. A magical storm if created by a spell of eight level or lower is dispelt.

- A creature struck with this bolt takes an additional 3d4 lighting damage. Additionally, every creature of your choice that is within a 30 ft radious also takes 3d4 lighting damage as the lighting travels through all of those near the striking point.

Once this piece of ammunition has used either of its uses, it need a day to recharge.

name: Perfect Strike
desc:
This magical piece of metal can turn into any type of blade with a bonus action. It has a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

Whenever you roll a 20 on an attack roll, you triple all your damage die for the attack instead of doubling them, as the strike becomes a perfect strike.

name: Frog Bombs
desc: As an action, you can throw this frog at a spot within 120 ft of you.  On impact the frog explodes in a cloud of poisonous stench. The cloud acts as if the spell *stinking cloud* with a 18 DC.

name: Dying Heart
desc:
This dying heart can be squeezed to release its ancient magic. A hostile creature within 5 ft of you must make a Constitution saving throw DC 18 or take 30 necrotic damage as the heart siphons its life force.

The heart then would gain strength and keep it inside it. As a bonus action, you can release that energy to fuel your own vitality, gaining 30 hit points.

After being used for healing, the heart would start dying again until siphons a hostile creature's lifeforce again.

name: Blinding Ash
desc: This satchel of dubious ash can be used to blind enemies. As a bonus action, you can throw ash at a creature who has eyes. The creature must make a constitution saving throw 18 or be blinded until the end of your turn.

name: Thorny  Eye
desc:
This eye is wrapped in thorns. As an action you can point the eye to a creture withing 60 fr of you and activate one of the following:

**Blindness:** A creature must succeed a DC 19 constitution save or be blinded as thorns grow from their eyes. 

**Deafness:** A creature must succeed a DC 19  Constitution save or be deafened as thorns grow from their ears. 

**Entangle:** A creature must succeed a DC 19 strenght save or be restrained as thorns grow from their skin and lock their muscles. 

**Suffocate:** A creature must succeed a DC 16 Constitution save or start to suffocate as thorns grow inside their lungs. 

**Thorn Grow:** A creature must succeed a DC 19 Constitution save or take 6d10 piercing damage as thorns burst from their veins.

Once once of this has been used, it cannot be used until the next dawn again. A creature can repeat the save at the end of their turn to stop its effects.

name: Dagger of Endless Stabbing
desc: Whenever you roll a natural 20 with this magical dagger, you will stab the creature twenty times in quick succession.

name: Bag of Souls
desc:
This bag of souls has six hundred and sixty six soul coins inside.

As an action, you can release the soul contained in the soul coin. The soul within will be freed and proceed to be judged and reach any resting place given its followed gods and actions.

As an action, you can consume one of the souls, destroying it in the process. A soul coin will grant you a luck point. You cannot have more than three luck points at any one time. 

Additionally, you can use the soul coins for any ritual or magic that requires a soul.

name: Sword of Endless Spirits
desc:
This sword is made of spirits, a green flame flickers from a broken hilt.

This magical sword has a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls and does an additional 1d8 necrotic damage per hit.

You can cast *speak with dead* once a day.

Additionally, you can use a bonus action to do one of the following:

- Gain 5 temporary hit points by retrieving life force from the sword's spirits
- Send flying spirits from your sword to a creature within 60ft of you, which can do one of the following: 
   -  Attempt to grapple a creature using your ability modifiers.
   -  Explode against the creature, causing 2d6 force damage.
   - Grant you advantage on your next attack as the spirits distract the creature.
- Gain advantage on any skill check to know about spirits and undead.

Once a day, you can use a bonus action to enter a spectral form. The spectral form lasts for 1 hour. You gain the following benefits:

- Your attacks do an additional 1d6 force damage.
- You gain a magical flying speed of 60 feats as you are clouded by spirits.
- You gain resistance to force, necrotic, cold, and poison damage.
- You can cast the *fear* spell, Save DC 18, as a bonus action once per activation.

name: Satchel of Lost Memories
desc: This Satchel has 36 memories left in it. As an action, you can absorb someone's memories, gaining a 1d6  bonus to any skill in the following hour as some of the memories sharpen your own skills, granting guidance or knowledge.

name: Wishbone of Desperation
desc:
This wishbone seems to be of a large mammal. When two creatures with a deep positive emotional bond hold each side, the runic work etched on the bone regains is magic

If the bone is then pulled apart, the magic inside releases.

The person with the bigger piece of the bone gains one cast of the *wish* spell.

The person with the smaller piece of the bone dies, their body turns to sand and their soul is destroyed.

name: Weakest Link
desc: Drinking this potion grants a permanent +2 to your lowest ability score.

name: Ruth's Parting Gift
desc:
This small inked mark appears on those who Ruth hated at the moment of death. As an action, you can polymorph into a tadpole for one hour.

Additionally, once a week, at a random time, you are turned into a tadpole for one hour unless you succeed a 23 DC wisdom-savng throw.

name: The Last Laugh
desc:
__*It is said a hag will always have the last laugh*__

A month after the hags died, all of those who ever made a deal with them start to hallucinate them at random times. The hallucinations last for a century. At some point after the month, one of the hags appears in their dreams to offer a message.

*"A final curse has been placed on all of those who ever dealt with us.  One which starts on our death. If a century from now we have not returned to life, all of you marked, along with your bloodlines, will perish "*

name: The Fisherman's Mighty Strike
desc:
Whenever you attack a creature that is underwater with this piece of ammunition, you have an advantage on the attack roll, ignore any underwater fighting restriction, and do an extra 2d4 bludgeoning damage.

If the creature has a swimming speed being utilized to travel waters, you gain an extra +2 bonus to the attack roll.

name: BloodBolt
desc:
This piece of ammunition is always covered in coagulated blood. The body of a creature who is killed with such explodes in a bloody mess, as long as they have any blood..

All creatures within fifteen feet of the creature are sprayed by it and either: heal 2d4+3 hit points or must succeed a dc 16 dexterity saving throw or take 2d6 piercing damage. You decide which effect affects each creature on its radious.

name: The Almighty
desc:
The Almighty is the spirit of an ancient warrior trapped without escape within a ring.

As an action, you can summon The Almighty. The Almighty acts after you in the initiative count and is immune to all damage and effects.

During its turns, it can grant one of the following  effects to one creature within 60 ft of it:

- The Almighty can grant a creature advantage to save against the frightened or charmed condition until the start of its new turn.
- The Almighty can grant advantage to the next attack a creature makes.
- The  Almighty can grant resistance to one creature to one damage of your choice type until the start of the Almighty's next turn. 
- The Almighty can grant advantage to one ability check the creature makes until the start of the Almighty's next turn.
- The Almighty can grant disadvantage to the next attack a creature makes.
- The Almighty can grant disadvantage to the next ability check a creature makes until the start of the Almighty's new turn.



The almighty can move up to forty feet per turn and it can hover. After one hour, The Almighty disappears and cannot be called forth until the new dawn.

name: The Third Eye
desc: A creature who drinks this dark potion of floating eyes gains a permanent advantage on perception and investigation checks.

name: Blessings of The Departed and The Freed
desc:
Those freed and departed reach to you and grant you a divine blessing, born from the strength of their combined gratitude.

A creature blessed gains the following benefits

- Your hit points increase by 20 points.
- You gain a +1 bonus to saving throws.
- You gain advantage on saves against necrotic damage.
- You gain resistance to necrotic damage

name: Coating of Vileness
desc:
A weapon coated in this substance gains the following properties:

Once per creature, you can release a corrosive explosion around them. The creature takes  4d4 acid damage. A creature wearing any kind of metal armor has its AC reduced by 1. A magical armor repairs itself after an hour, a non-magical armor remains broken permanently.

name: Mithril Tipped Luminous Arrow
desc:
A luminous arrow tipped with mithril has these magical properties:

* On a hit, this arrow deals an extra 2d6 radiant damage to the target. The damage increases by 1d6 if the target is an undead or a fiend. 

* An undead creature of CR 2 or lower hit by this arrow must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or immediately die and turn to ash. Once it hits a target, the ammunition is no longer magical.

* **Illuminate:** If you shoot the arrow straight upward, it creates a 60-foot-radius sphere of sunlight 30 feet above you for 1 minute. The sphere is bright light and sheds dim light for an additional 60 feet. The arrow is destroyed when used in this way.

Once this ammunition has been used, it loses its magic until a new dawn.

name: Weave Blocker Dart
desc:
This dart is charged with weave-disrupting energy capable of temporarily severing a target’s connection to the weave. The coiled tip of each dart is compressed until it’s embedded into a target, where it expands outwards to maximize damage. 

A creature hit with this dart must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it cannot use any ability that requires the creature to use magic for 1 minute. At the end of each of the creature’s turns, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success.

Once the dart has been used, it cannot be used again until a new dawn.

name: Sky Jump Blade
desc:
This magical rapier has a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

This blade has 7 charges and it regains 1d4+1 expended charges daily at dawn. As part of your attack, you can expend 1 charge to make a flying strike at an airborne creature with this sword. You can fly up to 60 feet and then make one melee weapon attack with this sword. This flight is in addition to your regular movement. If you hit a creature with this attack, you have a flying speed equal to your walking speed until the end of your turn and do not provoke opportunity attacks. If you fail to hit a creature with this attack, you immediately start falling. 

At the end of the turn, you fall to the ground if you are still in the air.

name: Armor of Shadows
desc:
This breastplate is always cold to the touch and surrounded by wisps of darkness.

Thrice per day, you can activate the armor's magic as a reaction to someone rolling a melee attack on you. Thick shadows explode forward and wrap the creature,  momentarily blinding them for that strike.

name: Potion of The Past
desc:
This potion is coveted by scholars and sought by those obsessed with the past.

When consumed you can name a time and place, or a person, place, and time. The time must be in the past and the person should be able to be targeted by divination. When you do so, you make a DC 12 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the potion is destroyed and you take 2d12 psychic damage.

**Past Sight:** On a success, your eyes cloud over and in your mind, you see the place or person you named. The scene will follow the person you named or stayed anchored to the place named. While observing the scene your vision narrows to a 10ft radius circle. The vision lasts for 10 minutes.

Once you view a specific time you can never do so again. 

The potion is thick and leaves a milky residue on the glass. It smells strongly of incense and drinking it causes your head to throb painfully.

name: Early Bird Necklace
desc: Once per day, you can decide to reroll your initiative roll. You must take the second roll.

name: Dark Bindings
desc: Once a day, when you kill a creature with a spell of 5th level or lower, the ring will use its magic to bind some of their fleeing life force and restore the spell slot used.

name: Amulet of Bestial Transformation
desc:
As a bonus action, you can invoke this amulet's magic to alter your physical form and gain bestial characteristics.
