When you use this amulet, you can spend one or more of its charges to gain some of the following effects.  This transformation lasts for 10 minutes, until you die, or until you end it as a bonus action.

**Amphibious:** You can spend one charge to gain gills. You can breathe air and water.

**Keen Senses** You can spend one charge so your eyes senses become more sensitive. You gain advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks.

**Blind Sense:** You can spend one charge to gain an intuitive instinct to detect prey. You gain blindsense within 10 feet of you.

**Climbing:**  You can spend one charge to grow tiny hooks or sticky skin. You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed, allowing you to traverse vertical surfaces and move along ceilings 

 **Swimming:** You can spend two charges to gain fins and webbing between your fingers and toes; you gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.

**Tough Hide:** You can spend two charges to change your skin becomes as tough as leather; you gain a +2 bonus to AC.

**Beastial Claws:** You can spend two charges to grow dangerous claws. These claws do 1d10+proflevel slashing damage.

**Bestial Agility:** You can spend three charges to gain bestial agility. You gain advantage on dexterity (acrobatic) checks, dexterity saving throws, and the ability to dash as a bonus action.

**Flight:**  You can spend five charges to gain wings that sprout from your back. You gain a flying speed equal to your walking speed.


This amulet has eight charges and gains 1d4+3 charges every dawn. You can only use an ability twice per short rest.

name: Fire Whetstone
desc:
As a bonus action, you can use this whetstone to add +2 fire damage to a weapon's damage for one minute.

Once used the whetstone needs a day to recharge its magic.

name: Ring of Swift Cantrip
desc: Twice per day, after you cast a cantrip, you can cast it again as a bonus action.

name: Force Tipped Mechanical Arrow
desc: The head of this ammunition twists on impact, releasing a small blast of force. A creature hit with it must succeed a DC 14 strength save or be pushed away 5 ft.

name: Ring of Minor Magical Protection
desc:
This ring protects from magical damage. Whenever you are dealt damage by a magical effect or spells (Not including magical weapons), the ring can absorb up to ten damage.

Once the ring has absorbed ten damage, it cannot absorb more until a new dawn.

name: Swift Bow
desc:
This magical weapon has a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

When you make an attack with this bow, you have advantage on the attack roll if you've made another attack with the bow this turn against the same target.

**Swift Shot:**  Immediately after you attack with this bow, you can make an attack with this bow as a bonus action. You can use this property a number of times equal to twice your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

**Flurry of Shots**: Once per day, as an action, you can fire a shot at up to five different creatures given that they are within the bow's range.

name: Potatoborne Scroll
meta: Long forgotten, often refuted, this scroll proves inarguably the existence of the  noble Potatoborne race.
desc:
Priceless. The parchment smells like potato peelings.

If destroyed IC or OOC, spawns potatoborne all over the continent.

name: Makeshift Crystal Piercer
desc:
This large crystal spike has been fashioned into an unusual weapon. While you hold this blade in your hand, you can cast the *Light* cantrip on it at will. Additionally, it is considered magical for the purposes of overcoming resistances and immunities.

**Fragile.** If you roll a 1 on an attack roll made with this weapon, part of it shatters off and it becomes a shortsword instead. If you roll a 1 thereafter, it shatters again and becomes a dagger. The dagger version of this weapon does not break.

name: Catacombs and Creatures, Illustrated (Volume Two)
desc:
This illustrated storybook tells tale of daring heroes and thrilling adventures in a far off, fictional land called Sarcelle. It appears to be the second installment of a series written by one B. Lorr.

When you read aloud from this book, illusory scenes come to life in front of you to help  narrate the story.

**Live Your Own Adventure.** There are enough empty pages at the end for you, the reader, to write one story of your own. Once complete, the book will create illusory scenes of your story as well.

Additionally, the book comes with a scroll of *Thunderwave* and two scrolls of *Shocking Grasp*. These special scrolls can be used even if you can't cast spells, or don't have them on your spell list.

name: Catacombs and Creatures, Illustrated (Volume Eight)
desc:
This illustrated storybook tells tale of daring heroes and thrilling adventures in a far off, fictional land called Loire. It appears to be the eighth installment of a series written by one B. Lorr.

When you read aloud from this book, illusory scenes come to life in front of you to help  narrate the story.

**Live Your Own Adventure.** There are enough empty pages at the end for you, the reader, to write one story of your own. Once complete, the book will create illusory scenes of your story as well.

Additionally, the book comes with a scroll of *Guiding Bolt* and two scrolls of *Toll the Dead*. These special scrolls can be used even if you can't cast spells, or don't have them on your spell list.

name: Desmond's Charm
desc:
A little ivory pendant that Desmond keeps for good luck. It's carved in the shape of twin crossed swords.

When you make an attack roll while wearing this pendant, you can add 1d4 to the roll. You can do so after the initial attack is made, but before you know the results. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until the next dawn.

name: Dwarven Skewer
desc: A magical skewer used by a specific clan of dwarves in Lithosphere. When roasted over a fire, this skewer makes every dish on it taste like meat.

name: Beaker of Ale
desc:
This beaker was gifted by a Dwarven friend. When filled with ale, the content's alcohol percentage doubles, giving disadvantage on drink checks.

If the beaker is filled with wine, the bottom immediately falls out.

name: Bag of Voting Rocks
desc:
A brightly colored bag that contains rocks of five colors. Once on your turn, before doing anything else, you can pull out a rock of a random color. The rock's color determines what you do:

**Red:** Immediately use your action to make a weapon attack against a creature of your choice.

**Yellow:** Immedaitely use your action to dash. You must use up all of your movement this turn.

**Blue:** Gain a +2 on your next ability check or saving throw within one minute.

**Green:** You immediately eat this rock as a bonus action. Gain temporary hit points equal to your level.

**Brown:** You can use an action or a bonus action on this turn, but not both.



You can only grab a rock from this bag once a day. After that, it loses all potency until the next dawn.

name: Weight of the Past - Bo
desc:
Your encounter with the Tree of Tales has left a heavy weight upon your soul. Your chest was ripped open, but you're still here.

If you are dying at the start of your turn, you can expend one of your Rages to gain 1 hit point and come to consciousness. When you do so, you enter Rage as normal.

Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

name: Tear of the Tree
desc:
This tear of crystallized sap was left behind after your excursion into the past.

You can crush the tear in your hand as an action, which disintegrates the crystal into a smoke. When you do so, you cast the spell *Legend Lore* instantly. The information you receive is given to you as a misty vision that lasts for 1 minute. During this time, your body is unconscious and unmoving.

Additionally, up to five other creatures that inhale this smoke can experience the vision, as though they were also affected by the spell.

name: Lotus Charm
desc:
This glass charm has been crafted in the shape of a pink, five-petaled lotus.

While the lotus is on your person, you have the spells *Charm Person* and *Suggestion* known and prepared. They are considered to be on all of your classes’ spell lists, and do not count against your normal limits.

If you are attuned to the lotus, you can cast each of these spells once per long rest without expending a spell slot.

Additionally, the lotus empowers certain spells - *Charm Monster*, *Charm Person*, *Dominate Person*, *Dominate Monster*, *Fast Friends*, *Incite Greed*, *Mass Suggestion*, *Modify Memory*, and *Suggestion*. Whenever you cast one of these spells while attuned to the lotus, that spell gains the following additional benefits:
- If the target succeeds on its saving throw against the spell, it has no knowledge that you tried to charm it or cast a spell on it.
- If you target a creature within 10 ft. of you, it has disadvantage on its saving throw.
- If a creature would learn that it was charmed by the spell (such as at the end of *Charm Person*), it must first make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it does not realize it was charmed and instead mentally fills in an explanation for its actions.

name: Bloomtender's Staff
desc:
This gnarled oaken staff is knotted and weathered with age. Little blossoms grow out from the head in all different sorts of colors.

You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic quarterstaff.

While the staff is on your person, you have the spells *Druidcraft* and *Plant Growth* known and prepared. They are considered to be on all of your classes’ spell lists, and do not count against your normal limits. Once per long rest, you can cast *Plant Growth* without expending a spell slot.

**Grasping Tendrils.** As an action, you can cause writhing vines to erupt from a plant within 60 ft. of you, or from the staff itself. A creature that you can see within 20 ft. of the vines must succeed a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be pulled up to 20 ft. towards the source of the vines and become restrained. The creature can use its action to make a DC 15 Athletics check, ending the restraint on a success.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all uses on a long rest.

name: Grounding Band
desc:
This dull metal bracelet is scuffed in way too many places and unnaturally heavy in your hand.

You have advantage on saving throws made to resist being charmed.

Additionally, you can use your action to end one effect on yourself that is causing you to be charmed. In the case of a domination effect, you must first succeed a contested Wisdom check against the dominator’s Charisma check in order to use this ability.

Once you successfully end a charm effect, you cannot use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

name: Vial of Reflection
desc:
This glass vial is filled with a clear liquid. When held up to the light, however, it becomes perfectly reflective.

You can spill this liquid out onto the floor, whereupon it rapidly expands to fill a 20-ft radius circle. When you first spill the liquid, you can create an illusory image as per the *Major Image* spell. The illusion cannot be moved, does not require concentration, and lasts for 10 minutes before the liquid dries away.

The DC for determining whether the image is an illusion is 14. However, creatures have disadvantage on this check unless they are within 5 ft. of it.

name: Crystal Blossom Butterfly
desc:
This delicate crystalline butterfly is crafted from sapphire and onyx. It weighs next to nothing in your hand.

As an action, you can speak the butterfly's command word to breathe life into it. You choose which effect to create:
- **Courier.** The butterfly acts as by the spell *Animal Messenger* for 24 hours.
- **Companion.** The butterfly acts as by the spell *Find Familiar* for 8 hours.

At the end of the duration, the butterfly becomes inert and cannot be used again for four days.

If you are attuned to the butterfly, you can instead resummon it after you finish a long rest. Additionally, the duration of both of the effects is doubled.

name: Poppy Wreath
desc:
This wreath of poppies exudes a calming aura. Three of the flowers are abnormally large, and colored golden instead of the other orange blossoms.

The wreath carries 3 charges. While you are wearing it, you can expend one charge to cast the spell *Calm Emotions* without expending a spell slot or requiring concentration. When cast this way, the spell's DC is 14. Afterwards, one of the large golden poppies shrivels away.

Additionally, while you are wearing the wreath, you can use an action to cause it to emit a golden dim light in a 20-ft. radius. You can disable the light as another action.

name: Preserved Blood Rose
desc:
This blood-red rose has been set in a clear, glass orb.

Whenever a non-undead, non-construct creature dies within 30 feet of this rose, it captures a fleeting sliver of its life essence and gains one charge. The rose can hold up to 3 charges at a time.

As an action, you can consume all of the rose's charges, gaining 1d4+1 hit points per charge expended.

name: Touch of Death
desc: You have created a close affinity with death. As a result, the following spells are spells known to you: *create undead, raise dead, resurrection, revivify.*

name: Tomewriter's Armband
desc:
This armband was "given" to you by the Tomewriter, a mysterious and ancient entity. The armband is made of rough sandstone and constricts around your arm. You are able to don or doff your armour or any clothing, but the armband cannot be removed.

Once every dawn, a DM can choose to inflict disadvantage on your next weapon or spell attack. When they do, the band constricts around your arm, throwing off your aim.

name: Sandnetic Glove
desc:
This glove was found in a tomb of Efriqo, filled with a hundred terracotta warriors.

When wearing these gloves, switching from one weapon to another can be done using your object interaction during a round.

name: Terracotta Soles
desc:
Found in the tomb of the Tomewriter, these soles originally belonged to a tribe of terracotta warriors.

This set of soles can be attached to the bottom of your shoes. When attuned, these shoes provide the following benefits:
- You gain resistance to piercing damage from nonmagical weapons
- You gain advantage on rolls that try to knock you prone
- When dropped in water, you will always sink feet-first
- Using an action, you can activate the latent magic of these soles. When you do, your weight doubles. This remains the case until you use another action to deactivate the magic.

name: Astral-Touched Ring of Jumping
desc:
While wearing this ring, you can cast the jump spell from it as a bonus action at will, but can target only yourself when you do so.

Additionally, when you make a jump, if you would be able to arrive on a surface in any direction different to the one you started from, once per turn you can invert your gravity and land on that surface as though it were the ground. You remain connected to that surface until a minute has passed or you use this feature again to jump to a different surface. Once you have spent 10 total minutes attached to surfaces in this manner within a single day, you must finish a long rest before you can benefit from this effect again. If you would be moved due to an outside effect, you immediately experience gravity as normal for the plane you are in.

name: Fae-Touched Glamerweave
desc:
Glamerweave is clothing imbued with harmless illusory magic.

This Glamerweave bears a familiar emblem of a fairy's wings. While wearing these clothes, you can use your bonus action to cause the wings to sprout from your back and give off a strangely alluring effect. Choose one of either Charisma (Performance) or Charisma (Persuasion) checks, for the next hour you gain proficiency in these checks and can make them at advantage once. You cannot gain this effect more than once per dawn.

When you make a Charisma (Performance) or Charisma (Persuasion) check while wearing this glamerweave, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the check. Once you use this property, it can’t be used again until the next dawn.

name: Hell-touched Periapt of Wound Closure
desc:
While you wear this pendant, you stabilize whenever you are dying at the start of your turn. In addition, whenever you roll a Hit Die to regain hit points, double the number of hit points it restores.

Once per dawn, you can touch an unconscious ally and activate this item to automatically stabilize them. For the next hour after you successfully use this effect, you gain resistance to poison damage.

name: Psi Blade
desc:
While attuned to this weapon, and not incapacitated, you can materialize it in your hand with your mind. 

This magical sword has a +1 bonus to attack rolls. Additionally, it does an extra 2d4 force damage on hit. 

This magical longsword has the finesse and thrown (60/30) property. After being thrown, the sword materializes in your hand again.

While holding this weapon, you can use a bonus action to change its state:

- Defensive State: 

     - While holding this weapon on this state, you have an additional 2 AC.
    -  While holding this weapon on this state, you have resistance to non-magical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage
  -  While holding this weapon on this state, the first time you would drop to 0 Hit Points as a result of taking Damage, you instead drop to 1 hit point.


- Offensive State:

     - While holding this weapon on this state, you do an additional 1d4  force damage.
    -  While holding this weapon on this state, you ignore enemies' resistance to this weapon's damage.
  -  While holding this weapon on this state, you can use your reaction after being hit with a melee attack to strike the creature back.

- Quickened State:

    - While holding this weapon on this state, you gain 15 ft to all your speeds
  -  While holding this weapon on this state, you can cast the haste spell on yourself once per long rest. The spell uses your normal concentration and ends if you are not holding the weapon.
  -  While holding this weapon on this state, you can use an action and choose up to six people within 60 ft of you. You can then roll a melee attack against each one and teleport to 5 ft from any of the targets.

* Explosive State:

    - While holding this weapon on this state, you can leave a piece of the weapon lodged inside a creature after an attack. As a bonus action you can cause it to explode, doing 1x(number of turns inside it)d12 force damage, to a maximum of 5d12 force damage. After explosion, it materializes in your hand whole again.
    -  While holding this weapon on this state, you can replace the weapons damage to Thunder damage.
  -  While holding this weapon in this state, you can cast the spell *thunderstep* at will.


Whenever you roll a natural one with this weapon, you take 5 force damage and lose concentration. Additionally, you lose a state's benefits if changed to another state.

name: Plush Manatee
desc:
This plush manatee is a sought-after souvenir from Wanaland. It is a plush version of the manatees found in one of their rides.

When squeezed, the manatee lets out an accurate yelp.

name: Troll Fork
desc: This fork was used by a flesh-eating troll guarding a bridge. When attuned to this item, you have a +2 bonus to saving throws against getting poisoned by food.

name: Rock of Annoyance
desc:
This rock is extremely annoying. You can use an action to throw this rock at a creature. When you do, make a ranged weapon attack with proficiency.

On a hit, the rock deals 1 damage and forces the creature's next weapon attack to be done with disadvantage. After that, the rock disintegrates.

name: King's Sigil
desc: The King of Lithosphere has named you and knighted you heroes of Lithosphere. You will always have an easy way in the land, places to stay, carriages and ships for travel, use of teleportation magic, information, spies, mages, academies, and churches all open, with services free of charge. Simply present the King's sigil, and it should open most legitimate doors in Lithosphere.

name: Chaos Residue
desc:
A residual amount of chaos energy has remained with you after the fight with Xavarathimus. As a result, you can learn one lvl 1 and one lvl 2 spell from the Sorcerer spell list and can cast it once per day without using a slot.

Once one of these spells are cast, you have 50%  of creating a Wild Magic Surge and activate one effect from the Wild Magic Table.

name: Bane Sword
desc:
This magical sword can transform its shape into other types of swords. It has a +2 bonus to roll and damage rolls. Additionally, it does an extra 1d4 necrotic damage on hit

A creature hit by this weapon is cursed and baned and must subtract 1d4 from any attack roll or saving throw until the end of its next turn. Only one creature can be cursed this way at a time.

name: Boon of Willfulness
desc: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened. In addition, you can use an action to end all effects on yourself that are causing you to be charmed or frightened.

name: Potion of Might
desc: Whenever you consume this potion, your strength becomes 23 and you grow one size.  While under the effects of this potion, all strenght based attacks do an extra +2 damage.

name: Chaos Boost
desc:
You gain proficiency in one skill of your choice, as chaos enters your mind and grants you unknown knowledge or strenght.

Additionally, before you roll for such skill,  you must roll a d2. On a 2, you treat the skill as if having expertise for that roll.

name: Sky Piercer
desc:
Once a day you can cast the spell *arcane gate* 

The two portals are created in the sky, are 60 ft wide, can last for one hour, and have an increased maximum range of 1000 ft.

name: Ring of Champions
desc: While wearing this ring you gain +1 to your attack and damage rolls. Additionally, whenever you receive more than 25 hit points of damage from a singular attack or ability, you regain 5 hit points.

name: Red Eternite Ore
desc:
Pure Eternite, unprocessed, is not good for much but make you mad, and perhaps, leveling towns.

Anyone who spends enough time with pure Red eternite close without procuring a silver bath after to cleanse its effects will rapidly fall ill to the "Red Madness"

Pure Red Eternite destabilizes spells due to its strength in the arcane. Any spell cast while it is present, that affects an area, can only be cast centered on it if for a radius of 90 ft around it.

Additionally, when shown to sunlight or touched by fire, red Eternite immediately explodes in a 60 ft radius. Each creature inside this radius must make a Dexterity 18 DC saving throw or take 10d10 fire damage.

name: Mind Flow Artifact
desc:
A device that keeps information inside, controlled by the mind, attuned to one person. A tool to combine, order, and categorize information in an accessible and portable way.

As an action, you can open your mind to the device. Everyone but its owner must make a DC 18 arcana check or take 2d10 psychic damage and be pushed out as waves of information enter their brain.

The owner can organize information by creating nodes visible only in their mind. Inscribed thoughts and images will remain inside, written, and able to be accessed by the mind.

name: Blasting Baton
desc:
The Blasting Baton is a finesse simple weapon.

The Blasting Baton has been infused with Eternite. On a strike, the baton does 1d8 bludgeoning damage. 

As a bonus action, you can cause the baton to open up and create a red electrical current between its forked streams. During this time, the baton does 2d8 piercing damage. In this form, you can also use the baton as a simple ranged weapon with a 60/90 range. On a hit, it does 2d6 lightning damage.

The baton can only be powered up for one minute, once every hour.

name: Kidd's Righful Lightning Hammer
desc:
Kidd The Mad, a prisoner of times past, forged this hammer out of stolen processed Red Eternite.


This magical hammer has a  +2 bonus to damage and bonus rolls. Additionally, it does an additional 1d6 lightning damage once per turn.

Red Eternite runs through it, and as such you can both imbue and release the power within. 

As a bonus action, you can use a spell slot of up to the fifth level into the hammer. The charge lasts for a minute, and for each level of the spell slot used, you do an extra 1d10 lightning damage on your next hit.

Additionally, twice a day, you can call from the power of the hammer and release a blast of red lightning around you. All creatures within 30 ft must make a DC 17 dexterity save. On a failure, they take 10d8 lighting damage or half on a success.

name: Red Eternite
desc: Red Eternite has a special affinity to magic. This mineral can replace any material component for any spell. If the spell requires consumable materials, an equal amount of worth from the mineral is consumed during its use.

name: Eternite Fueled Arrows
desc:
Once a creature is hit with this ammunition, it explodes, doing an extra 2d10 lightning damage.

Once the ammunition has ben used, it loses its charge and becomes mundane ammunition.

name: Twin Eternite Daggers
desc:
These daggers have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

As an action, you can roll two ranged or melee attacks with these daggers. On a hit, these daggers do an extra 1d4 lightning damage.

If the daggers both either hit the same target or two different targets within 90 ft of each other, a connection is made, and an arc of red lightning is traced between the targets.

A person under one of these arcs takes 3d10 lightning damage at the start of their turn. It takes an action to try to remove the dagger from their body. A creature must make a DC 16 constitution check to or fail at doing so.

name: The Red Tempest
desc:
The Red Tempest is a strange organic substance that can coat weapons or armor.

On armor, it provides the wearer with thunder resistance.

On weapons, it causes the weapon to deal an extra +2 thunder damage

Applying the coating to a piece takes ten minutes. Removing the coating from a piece takes one minute.

name: The Will of the Ashes
desc:
Deeply guarded in a lost but never forgotten home, the Ashes hid an artifact of great power. While attuned to the Circlet, you gain the following:

 - 2 minor beneficial properties

- You gain +1 AC, advantage to dexterity saves, and resistance to necrotic, radiant, and force damage.

-  You learn the shield spell and can cast it twice a day without using a spell slot.

- As a bonus action, you can heal 1d10+level hit points once per long rest.

- Once a day, it allows you to complete and benefit from a short rest in only ten minutes.

- Your ancestors grant you knowledge. You gain one proficiency in a skill, tool, weapon, or language or a fighting style.

name: Seed of Chaos
desc:
A pitch black metal band of unknown origins powered by the forces of darkness and death. The ring has been known to bring chaos wherever it goes and constantly hungers for more power and knowledge.

    2 minor beneficial property
    1 major beneficial property
    1 minor detrimental property
    1 major detrimental property

**False Appearance:**. The wearer of the ring can change the appearance of the ring to look like any other form of ring as a bonus action.

**Protection of Death:**  While worn, the ring grants a +1 bonus to AC, and any creature that hits the wearer with a melee attack takes 2d10 necrotic damage, bypassing resistances. In addition, the wearer is resistant to necrotic damage.

**Shadow Weaponry:**. The wearer may as a bonus action, create any sort of weapon, armor, or ammunition out of pure darkness. If the created item is armor, it grants a +1 bonus to AC, and if the item created is a weapon or ammunition, it has a +1 to attack rolls and deals an extra 1d10 necrotic damage. The wearer can summon items made of shadow up to an amount equal to their proficiency bonus and can dispel any or all of them as a bonus action. The weapons disappear into nothing if targeted by any spell or feature that dispels magic.

**Spellcasting.** The wearer can cast any necromancy or illusion spell of 1st - 9th level as an action, requiring no material (up to 10000 gp worth), somatic, or verbal components using your spell save DC and spellcasting ability. This does not apply to rituals. You can use this feature once and regain the ability to do so daily at dawn.

**Magical Ring:**  The ring is a magical ring that acts as a ring of invisibility.

**Dark Sentience:** The ring is a neutral evil ring that has an Intelligence score of 18, a Wisdom score of 20, and a Charisma score of 24. It hungers for dark power and knowledge and drives the wearer to find more of it. Failure to do so can lead to disputes in between the ring and the wearer. Should the argument escalate, The Ring may try to possess the wearer, who must succeed on a DC 20 Wisdom or be possessed by it until the next dawn.

name: Bolt of Hate
desc:
Random Properties. The Hate Bolt has the following random properties:

    2 minor beneficial property
    1 major beneficial property
    1 major detrimental property

The Hate Bolt is a magic sentient ammunition that grants a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. Once per turn, it deals an extra 2d10 psychic damage against non-good aligned creatures. When you make a ranged attack with this artifact it appears back in your crossbow.

**Strength from Hate:** While within the lands or lairs of an evil-aligned creature you have the following benefits:

  - You have a +3 to initiative rolls
 - Ranged weapon attacks that use the Hate Bolt don't suffer disadvantage
-  While the Hate Bolt is within 5 feet of you, you are immune to the charmed and frightened condition.

**Tyrant Slayer:** When in the presence of a creature that the bolt deems to be a tyrant you can make a ranged weapon attack against the creature with this artifact. When hit by the bolt in this manner, if the target has 100 hit points or fewer they instantly die. If the creature has more then 100 hit points they instead take 10d10 psychic damage 

**Sentience:** The Hate Bolt is a sentient chaotic good ammunition with an Intelligence of 15, a Wisdom of 13, and a Charisma of 16. The bolt communicates by transmitting emotion to the creature attuned to it. 


**Personality:** The bolt's purpose is to bring an end to tyrants. Killing those that support them, destroying their works, and foiling his machinations all help to fulfill this goal. The bolt's obsession with this eventually becomes a fixation for its user. While the bolt was created for a good cause, it is fueled by negative energy and is easily angered with hatred towards evil.

The bolt considers a tyrant to be a cruel and oppressive ruler, a person exercising power or control in a cruel, unreasonable, or arbitrary way, or a ruler who seized absolute power without legal right.

name: The Weave's Splitting Ring
desc:
This ring of processed eternity provides a mage with a great ability to manipulate the weave.

While attuned to the ring, you gain the following benefits.

- You can cast one level one spell from the sorcerer spell list once a day without expending a spell slot
- Twice a day, whenever you cast a spell, you can decide so it if was cast with one higher level spot than the one used.
- Twice a day you can expand the area of effect of a spell by up to 15 ft.
- Once a day you can cover yourself  on a protective Eternite barrier and be immune to the effects of one spell cast by yourself
- Twice a day, when casting a healing or damaging spell that rolls die, you can add two additional of its lowest die used to the result.
- Once a day you can maintain concentration on two spells as long as they are cast with second-level spell slots. Failing a concentration check drops both spells.
- Learning a spell from a spell scroll takes half its time.
- Once a day, when you cast a spell that originally targeted multiple people, you can target an additional person as long as that person is within the parameters of the spell.
- Once a day you can cast the *dispel magic* spell without using a spell slot.
- Once a day, you may cast a spell as a reaction despite having used a reaction that round already.

**Blast from the Weave:**  As an action you can release an explosive release of magic in front of you. All creatures within a 15 a ft cone of you must make an intelligence save DC 17 or take 8d8 damage and be stunned until the end of their turn.

Once used, you cannot use this ability again until a new dawn.

name: Everflowing Eternite Cloak
desc:
As a bonus action, you can activate this cloak's red eternite magic. For the next minute, you have a flying speed equal to your walking speed and do not provoke attack of opportunities.

Once used, the cloak needs to recharge until the new dawn.

name: Elixir
desc:
Can be consumed as a bonus action or injected as a reaction. (The cork transforms into a metal spike when left in the bottle and moved at a quick speed.)

*This elixir's potency fails when used outside of the vicinity of Yhorynlas.*

name: Phase Axe
desc:
This crystalline handaxe seems to be almost transparent when looking at the bladed head from different angles. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. This weapon has the thrown property with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. The first time on your turn when you hit a target with a ranged attack using the axe, you can force another creature within 10 feet of the target to take 1d8 force damage.

If you can see the axe and are within 60 feet of it, you can use an object interaction to return the axe back to your outstretched hand. When you do, the weapon phases through any obstacle in its path that is no more than 5 feet thick or made of lead.

name: Mistwalker Armor
desc:
*This dark leather armor releases thin wisps of fog from its seams*.

While wearing this armor you gain the following benefit:
- You are kept at at a constant, comfortable temperature in all weather conditions except extreme weather.

**Mistwalker:** You can cast Misty Step without consuming a spell slot. Once used, this feature recharges after a long rest.

**Boundless Vision:** As a bonus action fog doesn't obscure your vision for the next minute. If activated as an action the effect lasts for 30 minutes. Once used, this feature recharges after a long rest.

**Rimeveil:** You can cast Armor of Agathys at level 2. You can choose to activate this as an action normally or **after** taking damage as a reaction. Once used, this feature recharges after a long rest.

**Pondbender:** As an action, you can target a point of natural ground within 30 ft. of you that you can see and cause a pond to spill out of the ground, filling a 5 ft. square. This pond lasts for 1 hour and is difficult terrain. If a Medium or smaller creature ends its turn fully within the pond, it sinks up to its waist. Once used, this feature recharges after a long rest.

name: Ring of Eldritch Fire
meta: Ring, rare (requires attunement by a warlock)
desc:
This ring has 3 charges and regains all expended charges each day at dawn. While wearing this ring, you can expend 1 charge as a bonus action to mark an unmarked creature that you can see within 60 feet of you for 1 hour. The mark is lost if you fall unconscious or end it early using a bonus action. A marked creature appears to be shrouded in a strange, sharpening mist that magically helps you focus on it with spell attacks. The formula for your spell attack rolls becomes **proficiency bonus + charisma modifier + 2** and you gain a +2 bonus to spell damage for those attacks when you attack a marked creature. In addition, can choose to change the spell's damage type to fire or necrotic.

 
You know a marked creature's general distance and direction from you at all times while it's on the same plane of existence as you. In addition, you perceive a marked creature within 60 feet of you as though you had truesight, and can see through any illusion that stands between you and the marked creature.

name: Mask of the Unseen
desc: *While wearing this Mask, your body becomes invisible but only to the limit of your skin or equivalent bodily shell. Anything you are wearing or carrying remain visible, including the Mask of the Unseen. If you cast a spell or attack while under the effects of this item, the magical invisibility is removed until a minute passes without casting, wherein the invisibility resumes.*

name: The Scroll of Truth
desc:
*A magical scroll with hints of faded text.*

While attuned to this scroll you can use your reaction when a creature with a soul within 45 feet of you dies to seize a small portion of the departing soul to feed the scroll, adding a charge. With each portion seized, icy blue text becomes more and more visible. The scroll can hold up to 20 charges.

The charges can be spent the following ways:

• While holding this scroll you can use your action to expend 5 charges to cast the **Zone of Truth** spell.

• While holding this scroll you can use your action to expend 10 charges to ask one question. A one word answer appears on the face of the scroll, written in magical ink. The question cannot have a yes or no answer. 

• While holding this scroll you can use your action to expend 20 charges to ask one question. A one sentence answer appears on the face of the scroll, written in magical ink.

The answers revealed on the scroll will always be truthful but their clarity is not guaranteed. An hour after it has been read the text vanishes.

The question must hold some relation to the souls sampled. For example you cannot kill bandits in Avia to recieve information about the Feywilds.

name: Harp of Healing
desc:
Whenever you play this harp, you can use your action to heal up to six people of your choice that you can see within sixty feet of you for 2d8 hitpoints.

Once used its magic disappears until a new dawn.

name: Shield of Sacrifice
desc:
This magical shield is said to have been a gift from the Couatls. It has a +1 bonus to AC.

Additionally, once per day, you can use your reaction when you see a creature within 60 ft of you take damage and take that damage instead. Any effect from the source of the damage is instead passed onto you.

name: Amulet of Sorcerous Healing
desc: Whenever you use sorcerer points, you can heal an amount equal to the number used.

name: Waters of Wisdom
desc: When you drink this potion, you have advantage on Wisdom checks for the next hour.

name: Talisman of Divinity
desc:
While wearing this talisman, when you use a spell slot through divine magic you can call divine energy to your aid. The spell slot used will not be spent. 

The spell slot must be equal to or lower than half your proficiency score (rounded up). 

Once used, this amulet loses its magic until a new dawn.

name: Uroboro's Strenght
desc: While wearing this Uroboros amulet, your maximum hit points increases by 3 hit points.

name: The Watchful Eye Of The Couatls
desc: This emblem designed to resemble a Couatl's eye is said to allow Jazirian's Couatls to watch over you in times of need.

name: Gravity Splinter
desc:
As an action, you can throw this dark wooden-like splinter into an empty spot within ninety feet of you and create a small black gravitational pull. The pull brings all creatures within 60 ft of it who fail a strength saving throw DC 17 to the closest empty space of its epicenter. 

Once used, the splinter is destroyed.

name: Ring of Psionics
desc:
While wearing this ring, you can cast a spell of the third level or lower that you know or have prepared with psionics. The spell is cast at its lowest level and must not have a consumable costly material.

Once used, you cannot use the psionic energy within the ring until a new dawn.

name: Boots of Swift  Transposition
desc: Once per day you can use your bonus action to swap places with another willing creature within 60 ft of you. At the end of your turn, you swap places with that creature once again.

name: Gem of Vanishment
desc:
As a bonus action, while you hold this gem, you can use a charge to turn invisible until the end of your turn. The invisibility ends if you attack or cast a spell.

This gem has a number of charges equal to your proficiency bonus. It regains its charges used at dawn.

name: Bahamut's Mighty Will
meta:
*Artifact*

*Attunement*
*requires attunement by a favored of The Dragon King, Bahamut*
desc:
This magical sword is granted to the warriors of The Lord of the North Wind, The One Platinum Dragon, Bahamut, to fulfill his will. Only a favored one, one who follow the tenets of Bahamut, can wield the weapon.

A mark of the wielder's dedication to the disruption of evil, its shape transforms through the vanquishing of grown malevolent dragons, destroying Tiamat's plans. 

It is said that the more a warrior proves themselves, the more pronounced are the Draconic aspects of Bahamut's influence

This magical sword has a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls. 

Once the wielder has vanquished: 

An Evil Dragon:

- As a bonus action, you can transform the sword into any other kind of bladed weapon.

- You gain an additional 1d4 cold damage to damage rolls with this weapon

- Good dragons will hear you and show more respect compared to other humanoids and non-dragons.

* You can learn two cleric cantrips of your choice.

A Trio of Evil Dragons

- You learn the daylight and light spells and can cast them at will.

 - As an action you can heal a number of hit points equal to your level.

- Good dragons will be more likely to allow you to ride them and grand you passage into their homes

- Once per day, as a bonus action, you can call for Bahamut's light to protect you. You gain a +2 to AC and Saving Throws for one minute.

Half a Dozen Evil Dragons:

- As an Action, you can plane shift (self) to the gates of Bahamut's Palace once per day. 

- You gain telepathy (120ft) as long as you are attuned to this weapon.

- You gain an additional 1d4 radiant damage against undead dragons.

- Two minor beneficial properties. 

A Dozen Evil Dragons: 

* You gain an additional +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

* Good dragons will join and aid your cause as long as it is a just and good cause, shall you ask them to do so.

* You learn the sunbeam spell and can cast a number equal to your proficiency bonus.

* Once per day, as an action, you can imbue yourself with the power of Bahamut and release a damaging breath weapon equal to that of an adult metallic dragon.

Two Dozen Evil Dragons:

* You gain an additional 1d4 force damage to your damage rolls.

* Once per day, you can commune with Bahamut as if you had cast the *commune* spell. Bahamut will allow five questions and respond as clearly as possible, with as much information as needed.

* Three times per day, as an action, you can create a 30 ft aura around you for one minute. Every creature within this area has advantage to ability checks and saving throws.

- Once per day, you can use *frightening presence* along with your attack or multi-attack action, as if you were an adult gold dragon.

Fifty Evil Dragons:

-  A place will be granted along the top generals of Bahamut's army, a dragonrider of the strongest dragons who exist in Bahamut's court.

- You gain cleric spellcasting ability and  progression as if you were a level 9 cleric.

- Once per day, as an action, you can imbue yourself with the grace of Bahamut and release a secondary breath weapon equal to that of an adult metallic dragon.

- 1 major Beneficial property

One Hundred Evil Dragons:

* You become part of Bahamut's inner council, your voice and ideas being weighted along with the greatest of dragons and the Seven Gold Wyrms

- You are allowed request assembling with Bahamut themselve.

- You gain resistance to all damage.

- You gain invulnerability to cold and force damage.

- As an Action, you can disintegrate any creature with less than 100 hit points you can see within 30 ft. The target must make a DC 21 constitution save or be disintegrated

**~** **Honor and Correction to the Enemies of Justice and Good.** **~**

name: Pouch of Feywoad Ashes
desc:
The powdered remains of a feywoad fomorian. Despite having burned to nothingness, these ashes still almost seem alive.

This pouch can hold 120 pinches of ash. You can use the ash in the following ways:
- As an action, you can grab a handful of ash and toss it into the air. The ash lingers as a cloud of smoke centered on your location, as if by the *Fog Cloud* spell, without requiring concentration. The radius is equal to the number of pinches you consume: 10, 15, or 20.
- You can spread ash over an area on the ground. Each 5-foot square requires 5 pinches of ash and takes an action to cover. The ash remains for 24 hours or until blown away by a strong wind. If the area comes into contact with flame, it instantly ignites into a field of fire. A creature that starts its turn in the field takes 1d4+5 fire damage.

If this pouch is subject to fire damage while not being worn or carried, it will explode in a violent conflagration. Creatures within 15 feet of the pouch must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking fire damage on a failure or half as much on a success. The fire damage is equal to 1d4 per 10 pinches in the satchel. Flammable objects not worn or carried within this radius are set on fire.

name: Rat of Sending
desc:
*One of Adren Nightfall's rattos.*

You write a message on a sheet of parchment and roll it up, giving it to the Rat of Sending. The rat will then take the message to another rat, passing it along. Eventaully Gharmid's network of rats will deliver the message to the recipient, whom can then send a message back in the same manner.

name: The Duke's Complementary Cinnamon Sticks
desc:
*They're free!*

This cigarette box contains 5 cinnamon sticks. Chewing on one for ten minutes removes a level of a exhaustion and tastes swell to boot.

name: Bottled Wisplight
desc:
This jar contains a little globule of warm yellow light. It can be held like a lantern in one hand, and you can use an action to hide or expose its light.

While uncovered, the jar emanates bright light in a 20-ft radius and dim light for another 20 feet past that. If this bright light comes into contact with magical darkness, the overlapping area becomes dim light instead.

Additionally, the bright light reveals invisible creatures and objects, as the light clings to it and suppresses the invisibility effect. This suppression lasts for 1 minute even after the creature leaves radius of the jar's bright or dim light.

name: Couch (Trick Furniture - Futon)
desc:
As a bonus action you can transform this couch into the Bed.

When it is time to go to sleep this Couch transforms into the Bed as a free action.

name: Bed (Trick Furniture - Futon)
desc:
As a bonus action you can transform this bed into the Couch.

After completing a long rest in this Bed it transforms into the Couch as a free action.

name: Phase Axe, Standard Issue
desc:
This crystalline handaxe seems to be almost transparent when looking at the bladed head from different angles. This weapon has the thrown property with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet.

If you can see the axe and are within 60 feet of it, you can use an object interaction to return the axe back to your outstretched hand. When you do, the weapon phases through any obstacle in its path that is no more than 5 feet thick or made of lead.

name: Ruenar's Full Moon (Trick Weapon - Harvester)
meta:
*Weapon (greataxe), very rare*

**Attunement**
Requires Attunement by a creature with proficiency with greataxes. Attunement to this greataxe counts as attunement to the Fallen Crescent as well.
desc:
As a bonus action you can change this into **Fallen Crescent**.

You gain a +1 to attack rolls made with this greataxe. This greataxe deals an additional 1d6 cold damage, emblematic of the chill of the Yhorynlas.
 
**Full Moon**: When you land a killing blow on an enemy you can choose make one attack against an enemy adjacent to the target as a free action. Making this attack forces it to transform into the **Fallen Crescent** as a free action. This feature recharges after a long rest.

name: Fallen Crescent (Trick Weapon - Harvester)
meta:
*Weapon (greatscythe), very rare*

**Attunement**
Requires attunement by a creature with proficiency with greataxes. Attunement to this greatscythe counts as attunement to the Ruenar's Full Moon as well.

*Weapon: Martial, melee*
15 lbs. --- 2d6 slashing - heavy, reach, two-handed
desc:
As a bonus action you can transform this weapon into **Ruenar's Full Moon**.

You suffer a -3 debuff to attack rolls and gain 1 damage made with this unique scythe.

**Crescent Sweep**: Once per turn when you attack with this weapon you can also choose to make a single attack against every other enemy within your reach. You attack the original target as normal but the other attacks are made using only the weapon's damage dice and modifiers. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. After expending all uses of this feature you can transform into **Ruenar's Full Moon** as a free action. All uses recharge after a long rest.

name: Hunter's Phase Axe (Trick Weapon - Handaxe)
meta:
*Weapon (handaxe), very rare*

**Attunement**
Requires attunement. Attunement to this handaxe also counts as attunement to the Axebow of the Hunter as well.
desc:
As a bonus action you can transform it into the **Axebow of the Hunter**.

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

When you use your Gathered Swarm feature to deal damage to the target of an attack roll with this handaxe, it instead deals 2d4 force damage.

If you see this axe and are within sixty feet of it, you can use your object interaction to have your swarm return the axe to your outstretched hand. It travels in a straight line, passing through any creature and object less than 10 feet thick on the way. Any creatures the axe passes through take your enhanced Gathered Swarm damage. You may cause this damage a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. All uses recharge after a long rest.

You may forgo an attack to transform the **Hunter's Phase Axe** into the **Hunter's Axebow**, or use a reaction to transform it upon targeting a creature with a ranger feature that marks (such as Hunter's Mark or Favored Foe).

name: Axebow of the Hunter (Trick Weapon - Handaxe)
meta:
*Weapon (axe crossbow), very rare*

**Attunement**
Requires attunement. Attunement to this axebow counts as attunement to the Hunter's Phase Axe as well.

*Weapon: Martial, Ranged*
5 lbs. --- 1d6 slashing - ammunition, range (100/360), two-handed, finesse
desc:
This magical weapon has a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls. This crossbow fires crystaline, magic handaxes that disappear upon striking the target, reappearing knocked in the axebow.

As a bonus action you can transform it into **Hunter's Phase Axe**. If you make an attack against a target within 30 feet of you it will transform at the end of your turn.

When you use your Gathered Swarm feature to deal damage to the target of an attack roll with this handaxe, it instead deals 2d4 force damage.

When you hit a creature with an attack roll using this shortbow, you may force another creature within 10 feet of the original target to take 1d6 force damage.

As an action, you can lead a volley with this axebow. Your swarm joins in with smaller, illusory copies of this weapon, launching a volley of spectral handaxes that cover a 20-ft-radius sphere on the ground. A creature in this space must make a Dexterity saving throw against your Ranger Spell DC or take 4d6 non-magical slashing damage. On a success, the creatures take half the damage. Using this volley automatically transforms **Hunter's Axebow** back into **Hunter's Phase Axe**.

You may forgo an attack to transform the **Hunter's Axebow** into the **Hunter's Phase Axe**, or use a reaction to transform it upon a hostile creature ending their turn within 5 feet of you.

name: Satchel of Glowspores
desc:
This pouch contains powdery spores that glow with a teal bioluminescence.

This pouch can hold 120 pinches of spore. You can use the spore in the following ways:
- As an action, you can throw out one or more pinches of spore to create a tiny cloud of floating, glowing light centered on your location. The cloud emits dim light in a 10-foot radius, and lasts for 1 minute for every pinch consumed.
- As an action, you can throw out 20 pinches of spore in a 15-foot cone. Creatures within that cone must make a DC 14 Dexterity save or become affected as if by the *Faerie Fire* spell. This effect does not require concentration, but an affected creature can use its action to wipe its spores away.

If you plant 10 pinches of spore in dirt and tend to it for a week, it will grow into a small, glowing mushroom.

name: Scholar's Cane (Trick Weapon - Caneon)
meta:
*Weapon (Quarterstaff), Rare*

**Attunement**
Requires Attunement by a Wizard. Attunement to this cane counts as attunement to the **Arm of Proselytization** as well.
desc:
You can use this cane as a spellcasting focus.

As a bonus action you can transform this cane into the **Arm of Proselytization**.

This cane can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff that grants a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. 

While holding this cane you gain the following benefits:
- Your movement speed is increased by 5 feet.
- This cane can be used as a set of Mason's Tools that you are proficient with for the purpose of breaking down walls.

**Deep Fried Foes**: When you roll damage for a leveled spell you can roll one additional damage die. You can use this feature proficiency bonus times per long rest.

**Moradin's Ward**:  Once per long rest you can 'parry' and 'riposte' a melee attack with a spell. If a melee attack misses you for any reason you can use your reaction to cast a spell at the creature. The spell must have a casting time of 1 action and must target only that creature. This feature can be used once per long rest.

**Load the Guns**: Damaging an enemy with a leveled spell while holding the cane charges the cane. When at 5 charges it can change to the **Arm of Proselytization** as a free action. The cane cane hold up to 5 charges.

name: Arm of Proselytization (Trick Weapon - Caneon)
meta:
**Attunement**
Requires attunement by a Wizard. Attunement to this cannon counts as attunement to the Scholar's Cane as well.

*Weapon: Special, Ranged*
50 lbs. --- 2d6 force - range (80/320)
desc:
*A ghostly weapon crafted from the remains of one of the fishmen's cannons by The Duke. A strange metal tube that fires whispy balls of souls long departed.*

This cannon is a spellcasting focus.

As a bonus action you can transform this into the **Scholar's Cane.**

You gain a +1 to attacks and damage rolls made with this cannon and attacks made with this cannon use your Intelligence modifier.


While holding this cannon it decreases the holder's walking speed by 10 ft.

**Chain Shot**: When using the attack action, as a bonus action you can make a second attack against the same target or an adjacent target.

**Greybeard's Barrage**: Gazat's signature hissy snicker echoes magically amplified around the area. Pick five creatures within a 30-foot-radius that then must make a Dexterity saving throw. A target takes 1d4 + (*1d4 per charge in the Caneon*) cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one, using your spellcasting DC.  Using this feature changes back into the **Scholar's Cane** as a free action and consumes all charges.

name: Scholarguard's Vigil (Trick Weapon - Bulwark)
meta:
*Armor (shield), very rare*

**Attunement**
Requires attunement by a Fighter. Attunement to this shield counts as attunement to the Scholarguard's Judgment.
desc:
While holding this shield, you have a +1 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.

There is a slot built into the front where a Lanter of Revealing can be temporarily installed.

As a bonus action you can transform it into the **Scholarguard's Judgment**. The lantern is extinguished.

**Town Watch**: As a bonus action you can ignite the lantern, shedding bright blue light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Invisible creatures and objects are visible as long as they are in the lantern's bright light, and it temporarily burns away magical darkness. You can use an action with a free hand to lower the hood, reducing the light to bright light in a 5-foot radius.

**Hold the Line**: As an action you raise this shield into the air causing the dolorous town bells of Yhorynlas to fill the air. Pick any number of targets other than yourself in a 60-foot-radius that can hear this bell. They gain a +2 bonus to their AC for the duration as spirits of departed Scholarguard flit around them offering protection. While this is active your attacks have disadvantage. At any time you can choose to end this effect which immediately transforms this shield into **Scholarguard's Judgment** and gives your next three attacks made before the end of your next turn advantage. Once this feature has been used you cannot use it again until finishing a long rest.

name: Scholarguard's Judgment (Trick Weapon - Bulwark)
meta:
*Wondrous item, very rare*

**Attunement**
Requires attunement. Attunement to this counts as attunement to the Scholarguard's Vigil.
desc:
As a bonus action you can transform it into the **Scholarguard's Vigil**, separating from your weapon and returning to your other arm.

This weapon attaches to your wielded weapon's head/blade, losing its AC bonus and granting the following benefits to your weapon:
 
**Counterweight**: The weapon's damage die goes up a size (from d4 to d6, d6 to d8, d8 to d10, d10 to d12) and it gains the two-handed property if it did not already have it.

**Town Bell**: It deals additional 1d6 thunder damage on a hit to the primary target and creatures of your choice within 5 feet of your target. 

If you make an attack with **Scholarguard's Judgment**, at the start of your next turn it transforms back into **Scholarguard's Vigil**, separating from your weapon and returning to your other arm.

name: Explorer's Jacket
meta:
*Wondrous item, rare*

**Attunement**
It does not require Attunement to use but to wear and benefit from it you must own a camel
desc:
You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed providing the surface is not completely smooth.

**Camel Whisperer**: You have advantage on Animal Handling checks when interacting with creatures from hot climates.

**Camel Rider**: You can cast the Beast Bond spell on a camel without concentrating on it and without vocal and material components once per long rest.

**Harsh Environments**: You can cast Absorb Elements at level 1 once per long rest.

**Grave Robber**: You can cast the Knock spell once per long rest.

name: Voodoo Galleon
desc:
*A galleon with black sails floats in small waves inside this magical die.*

**Ghost Ship**: You can spend 10 minutes channeling the power of this die while aboard a sea vessel. After this time a bond forms between this die and the vessel. The following benefits are active for one week:

 • The sails fold over backwards, magically becoming black and now bearing the skull and cross bones of a pirate. 
• You can sail the ship without a crew with the die by rotating it and using the waves and wind current present within the die. You have disadvantage on checks made to sail the boat in this manner without proficiency in Vehicles (Water).
• Anything that happens to the ship in the die happens to the bound sea vessel.

This bond can be broken early by the user or if the item is destroyed or taken away from the vessel for more than 24 hours.

name: Mireborne Circlet
meta:
*Wondrous item, uncommon* 

**Attunement**
Requires Attunement by a Bard.
desc:
*The metal bands of Valeri's Focus Circlet have gained an odd indigo sheen after being submerged in that deluge of mysterious fluids.*

While wearing this silver circlet, you gain the following benefits:
- You gain a bonus to Constitution saving throws that you make to maintain your concentration on a spell you cast equal to the spell's level, up to a maximum of +3.
- You gain a swimming speed of 60 feet.

**Mindmeld:** Once per turn when you cast a leveled spell that targets an ally or use a feature that grants temporary hit points, that target can use their reaction immediately to make one weapon attack against a creature in their range. This feature has 5 uses that recharge after a long rest.

name: Siren's Call (Trick Weapon - Bandore)
meta:
*Weapon (crossbow, hand), very rare*
 
**Attunement**
Requires Attunement by a Bard. Attunement to the Siren's Call also counts as attunement to the Heartseeker.

*Weapon: Martial, Ranged*
3 lbs. --- 1d6 psychic - ammunition, range (30/120), light, loading
desc:
*Spectral tendrils protrude roughly perpendicularly from the lower bout of your instrument. Pulling back over the fingerboard releases a spectral bolt from these tendrils, functioning like a hand crossbow.*

This magical weapon has a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls. 

As a bonus action you can tranform it into the **Heartseeker**.

This instrument shares the same features as the Fochlucan Bandore.

**Lock and Note**: After casting a spell with this weapon as the focus, you can make a ranged weapon attack as a reaction.

**Disarming Melody** After casting a spell while holding this weapon you can choose to transform it into the **Heartseeker** as a free action. You have advantage on your next melee attack made with the weapon against a target of this spell that succeeded on its saving throw.

name: Heartseeker - (Trick Weapon - Bandore)
meta:
*Weapon (rapier), very rare*

**Attunement**
Requires Attunement by a Bard. Attunement to the Heartseeker counts for attunement to the Siren's Call as well.
desc:
*The tendrils of the instrument extends forward fully, glimmering slightly as they meld into the metal blade of a rapier. The rest of the instrument narrows slightly, allowing it to be swung effectively.*

As a bonus action you can transform it into the **Siren's Call**.

This magical weapon has a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

**Bioluminescence**: In darkness, the unsheathed blade of this trick weapon sheds teal light, creating bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet.

**Guiding Aequorea**: After hitting an enemy with this rapier you can choose to transform it into the **Siren's Call** as a free action. The target has disadvantage on the next saving throw made against a spell cast by you before the end of your next turn. This feature recharges after a long rest.

name: Rimeborne Ring [Deprecated]
desc:
While wearing this ring you gain the following benefits:
- While attuned to this Trick Weapon you know the Armor of Agathys spell.

**Ocean's Chill**: This ring has 3 charges and regains all expended charges after a long rest. While wearing this ring, you can expend 1 charge as a bonus action to mark an unmarked creature that you can see within 60 feet of you for 1 hour. The mark is lost if you fall unconscious or end it early using a bonus action. The formula for your spell attack rolls becomes **proficiency bonus + charisma modifier + 2** and you gain a +2 bonus to spell damage for those attacks when you attack a marked creature. In addition, you can choose to change the spell's damage type to cold or necrotic.

You perceive a marked creature within 60 feet of you as though you had truesight, and can see through any illusion that stands between you and the marked creature.

name: Night Diamond Splinter
desc:
While attuned to this amulet, you gain the following benefits:

- Your ability scores become a minimum of 11

- A spellcaster gains an extra first-level slot, second-level slot,  and third-level slot.

- You gain resistance to cold damage.

-  You learn the spells *corrupting touch* and *heartbreak*

-  Once a day as a bonus action you can use the diamond's magic to raise your strength ability score to 19 for one minute.

**Promise:** The wearer of this amulet is unable to physically or magically relinquish or separate from this artifact unless the promises' are met.

name: Cerunnos' Mark
desc:
Alfyn and the rays absorbed some of Cerunnos' green flames during The Hunt:

- Whenever you use your Gathered Swarm to cause damage, you do an additional damage die. This damage is fire.

- Whenever you use your Gathered Swarm to cause move an enemy, you can choose to set the path they are moved back on fire until the start of your next turn. A creature takes 1d6 fire damage whenever it enters the affected area.

- Whenever you use your Withing Tide feature, you are enveloped by an aura of Green Flames. A creature of your choice that ends its turn within 10 ft of you takes fire damage equal to your proficiency bonus.

name: Hag Sticks
desc:
There are four remaining Hag Sticks. You can use one of the Hag sticks in the following ways:

-  You can use an action to use the stick's magic against a creature within 60 ft of you. Roll a ranged spell attack against the target, on hit, they take 4d10+8 piercing damage as thorns erupt from the target's body. If the target is on the ground, it is also restrained until the end of your next turn.

- You can plant it on the ground as an action, after which an blighted *Awakened Tree* will be summoned. The awakened tree lasts for eight hours or  until its hp reaches 0. The awakened tree has a number of attacks equal to half your proficiency bonus, rounded up.

- You can crush a stick on your hand and be covered wrapped in branches and twigs. You gain an additional +2 AC and resistance to piercing and bludgeoning damage for 12 hours.

Once a Hag Stick has been used, it loses its magic.

name: Protective Flower
desc:
A red flower given by an Elven friend.

The first time you would drop to 0 hit points as a result of taking damage, you instead drop to 1 hit point.

Once the flower has protected you three times, it withers and dies, losing its magic.

name: Raven's Lord Cloak
desc:
While wearing this cloak you gain the following  benefits:

- You have a +1 bonus to AC and Saving Throws.

- As a reaction to taking damage, you can transform into ravens and move up to twenty feet away without provoking an opportunity attack. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus per day.

- Once per day you can cast speak with animals to target ravens. They are friendly to you and will offer any information asked.

- Once per day, as a bonus action, you gain a flying speed of 60 ft for half an hour as your cloak transforms into raven wings.

name: Raven's Lord Feather
desc:
If you whisper the words "Birds of a feather" in Sylvan and blow onto this feather, you will summon The Raven's Lord for a short audience.

Once used, this feather vanishes, and its magic is lost.

name: Hunter's Blessing
desc:
While wearing this amulet, twice a day, you can use a free action to activate its magic for one minute. Whenever you attack with a natural weapon, you do an additional die of the damage type.

Additionally, while active, your natural weapon bypasses resistance to nonmagical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

name: Giant's Gift
desc:
When you drink this potion, you become Huge for 1 hour if you are Medium or smaller, otherwise, the potion does nothing. For that duration, your Strength becomes 23, if it isn't already higher, and your hit point maximum and hit points is raised by 30.  In addition, the reach of your melee attacks increases by 5 feet.

Everything you are carrying and wearing also increase in size for the duration. When rolling damage for weapons enlarged in this manner, roll two times the normal number of base damage dice.

name: Silver and Gold Acorns
desc:
This set of two acorns has magical powers. As a bonus action, you can throw one acorn at someone's head within 20 feet of you.

- The Silver acorn casts the *sanctuary* spell on the target

- The Gold acorn casts the *healing word* spell on the target.

Once an acorn has been used, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

name: Bottled Boggle Tears
desc: This vial of black slippery substance replenishes at each down. As an action, you can cast the *grease* spell centered on yourself.

name: Sisters' Cure
desc:
The draught Sister Aliz brewed for the Institute virus was unpleasant, but seemed to leave no lasting detriments. Having undergone the entire procedure, you now have advantage on saving throws made against poison and disease.

The properties your blood has taken on are quite unique. if you are afflicted with a poison or disease, you can spend 1 hour studying a sample of your own blood. Upon doing so, you learn all the properties and effects of the poison or disease. At the DM's discretion, certain afflictions may be too obscure or complicated for you to fully decipher.

name: Creeping Vine
desc:
This was found growing along one of the windowsills of the Institute's main corridor. It's a short length of vine with a single flowerbud at its end.

The vine appears to be one half of a sensory device. As an action, you can open your senses to the paired vine, seeing and hearing as though you were in its place. During this time, you are deaf and blind with regard to your own senses. If the paired vine is on another plane of existence, you cannot make the connection.

name: Regenerative Draught
desc:
This potion is a viscous and deep red liquid, looking very much like a vial of blood.

A creature that consumes this potion regains 2d4+2 hit points immediately. For one minute, that creature also regains 1d4 hit points at the end of each of its turns.

name: Antidote Draught
desc:
This potion is a muddy brown in color, but surprisingly liquid. It's filled with all sorts of specks and impurities.

A creature that consumes this potion regains 2d4+2 hit points immediately and becomes cured of any poison affecting it. Additionally, that creature becomes resistant to poison damage for 1 hour. During that period, it also has advantage on any saving throws made to resist poison.

name: Draught of Desperation
desc:
This potion looks exactly like water, but it has the consistency of honey. As a result, it takes a full action to consume regardless of special features.

A creature that consumes this potion gains 8d4+8 temporary hit points immediately, which last for 1 hour. During the same hour, if you were to fall to zero hit points, you can make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a success, you fall to one hit point instead.

The DC of this save increases by 5 for every success you make.

name: Arm of the Forgotten
desc:
This magical weapon is a skeletal arm that moves with a mind of its own.

The arm is a simple melee weapon that deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage on hit.

Additionally, while holding the weapon, you can use your object interaction to grab an object within five feet of you that is not being worn or carried and is no heavier than 5 pounds.

When you use the arm to grab an object, roll a d20. On a roll of 5 or lower, the arm drops the object.

name: Detective's Magnifying Glass
desc:
While holding this small magnifying glass in hand, you are able to focus on small details when investigating. 

This magnifying glass has a total of 5 charges and regains 1d4 + 1 charges at dawn. When you make an Investigation check to investigate a crime scene, you can expend any number of charges to add a bonus to the roll equal to the number of charges spent.

name: Illusory Quill
desc: This quill has 3 unreplenishable charges. While holding the quill, you can use an action to expend 1 of its charges to cast the *illusory script* spell. After the last charge is expended, this quill loses all magical ability and becomes completely mundane.

name: Ooze Hammer
desc:
This magical hammer's head is always covered in Ooze. You become proficient with the weapon when attuned to it.

You can use a bonus action to transform the weapon between a war-hammer and a light hammer.  Additionally, you gain an additional 1d6 acid damage on hit. 

Once per long rest, you can throw the hammer at a creature you can see within 60 ft of you. On impact, the hammer's head explodes, launching ooze everywhere within 15ft of it. 

A creature on this range must make a dexterity saving throw DC 15 or take 5d8 acid damage. A creature takes half damage on a successful save.

name: Sticky Boots
desc:
While wearing these boots, as a bonus action, you can activate ooze on their soles. For one minute, you can adhere to vertical surfaces, including ceilings and roofs.  Additionally, you have advantage on checks to avoid being pushed or made prone.

Once these boots have been used twice in a day, they cannot be used again until a new dawn.

name: Ooze In A Vial
desc:
It is ooze in a Vial. It does not do much. 

Even if the vial comes in the shape of a flask and has a twisting cap, probably not safe for consumption.

name: Ochre Cloak
desc: This sticky, sticky cloak protects you from attacks. While being worn, you reduce all bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage by 1.

name: Ooze Ring
desc:
This ring can consume and store magic within its petrified ooze ring gem. You can cast a first level spell into it and store it. 

As an action, you can release the spell from within and cast it. The spell released is cast as if it were cast with a first level slot despite the level slot used to store the spell in it.

name: Ooze Warrior Pendant
desc: While wearing this pendant, you gain a +1 acid damage bonus to  the damage rolls you make with unarmed strikes and natural weapons.

name: Bracelet of Splendors
desc:
This bracelet has three charges. While wearing it you can use a charge to gain advantage on any charisma check you make.  This can be done after rolling but before knowing the result.

It regains 1d2+1 charges at dawn.

name: Potion of Weather Resistance (Heat)
desc: When you drink this potion, you become immune to the effects of hot weather for 1 hour.

name: Forgetender's Hammer
desc:
This sturdy looking blacksmith's hammer is covered in dwarven runes and doubles as a magic implement.

You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic hammer. Additionally, whenever you make a thrown attack with it, it magically flies back to your open hand.

If you are attuned to this hammer, it deals an additional 1d6 thunder damage on a hit.

**Quick Fix.** You can cast *Mending* without any components by tapping the target object with this hammer. When you do so, the spell's casting time is reduced to 1 action, although the object still mends over the course of a minute.

**Artisan Magic (Attune).** You learn the *Fabricate* and *Summon Construct* spells, and have them always known and prepared. They count as class spells for each of your spellcasting classes, and you can cast each spell once per long rest without expending a spell slot.

name: Cyrenne's Journal
desc: The pages of this journal are linked to each other. Whenever someone writes something on a piece of paper or page that was ripped from the journal, the written words appear on the nearest empty space  on a page that is still part of the journal.

name: Book of Common Written Language
desc:
This magical journal translates anything written on its left pages to common on the right side.

Similarly, any common phrase written in the right side is written in several languages on the left side.

The book translates Infernal, Abyssal, Draconic, Primordial, and Celestial.

name: Hound Seeker
desc: While wearing these glasses, you are able to perceive invisible  Hell Hounds

name: Hellish Brand
desc:
This bran can be applied to one weapon of your choice in the morning, covering it in smoky embers.

A weapon with this branding does an additional one fire damage per hit. Additionally, you regain hit points equal to the extra damage done by the brand.

The brand disappears at the end of the day and needs to be reapplied over a minute every morning.

name: Ring of Fiendish Punishment
desc: Once a day when you deal radiant damage to a fiend, you can maximize any damage die rolled.

name: Pendant of Inspiration
desc: Twice a day, when you use your bardic inspiration feature on someone, the target can decide to roll one hit die as a free action.

name: Fiendish Ash
desc:
As an action you can throw Fiendish Ash at a creature within five feet of you or at an empty spot. If thrown at a creature, they must make a dexterity saving throw DC 16 or take 1d8 poison damage. Additionally, on a failed save, they become covered on the Fiendish Ash.

Fiendish Ash is flammable. A creature or area covered in Fiendish Ash that is hit with fire damage is set on fire. A creature that starts or ends their turn on this area takes 1d6+2 fire damage. Similarly, a creature covered in it takes that damage at the start of their turn.

A creature can use an action to rid itself of any Fiendish Ash on them. Once it has been set on fire, they must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution Save to be able to clean it through the flames.

name: Crown of Minor Devil Command
desc:
While wearing this crown you know the infernal language. Additionally, you have advantage in any charisma check to deal with devils.

As an action, you can force a minor devil under your command. The creature must make a Charisma Saving Throw DC 17 or be under your command for the next hour. On a successful save, the devil is immune to the crown's powers and benefits for a day.

The creature obeys any order you give it to the best of its ability. The crown can only influence Minor Devils, any devil of CR 6 or above is immune to the crown's charms.

You can only command two devils at a time with this ability. Once an hour has passed, the devil ceases to be under your command, and becomes immune to its power for a week.

name: Infernal Conch
desc: When you raise this conch to your ear, you can hear the screams of a thousand tortured souls

name: Journal of Finding
desc:
As an action, you can ask the book to find something you have written within it. The command can be from something vague to something specific such as words, people, or sentences. Additionally, you can also use information on when or how you wrote the information such as time period or writing style. These can be combined together as see fit.

Some examples are:

"Find me information about cows"

"Find me all I wrote this day, last year, about cows"

"Find me all underlined information I wrote at night during the Twelft Moon of the Years 1426 and 1428 with red ink that are about Mary's Cows"

The book's pages will only show information that fit the criteria said  once opened.

name: Amulet of Radiant Poisoned Defense
desc:
Once a day, a creature resistant or immune to poison can   use an enemy's attempt to hurt them against them. As a reaction to being attacked with poison damage, you can transform the poison resisted or negated into radiant flames against the attacker. 

If the creature taking poison damage is immune to poison, the attacker takes damage equal to the poison damage dealt. If the creature taking poison damage is resistant to poison, only half is dealt instead.

name: Ring of Hellish Magic
desc: Whenever you do damage with a spell, a creature takes an additional one fire damage.

name: Blood Siphon
desc:
This magical longsword has a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls and the finesse property.
