This weapon becomes more powerful against specific targets over time. Whenever you strike a creature in a turn, the weapon siphons the creature's blood and uses it against them.

If the creature is struck with this weapon again in the next turn, it does an additional 1d4 damage, and so on in future turns, to a maximum of 2d4 extra damage. 

As a bonus action, you can release the siphoned blood in a violent attack, dealing 1d4 slashing damage per continuous turn they have been struck with the sword, resetting all sword counters for that specific creature to 0 when released.

The weapon's counter resets against a creature after any turn that the creature is not struck with the blade.

name: Lightningrod Weapon
desc:
The steel of this weapon seems to be oddly tarnished, with a dull rainbow staining its very top.

You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

While you hold this weapon, it draws in lightning in a 30 ft. radius around you. Any effect that creates lightning in an area (such as *Lightning Bolt* or a blue dragon's Lightning Breath) has no effect in this radius, as it is drawn to you instead. You are forced to take the effect's damage as though you had failed its saving throw, which cannot be reduced by any feature (such as *Evasion*).

After you are hit by lightning in this way, the weapon becomes **charged** for 1 hour. During this time, it deals an additional 1d6 lightning damage on a hit.

**Grounding (Attune).** You have resistance to lightning damage.

**Overcharge (Attune).** You can use an action to expend this weapon's charge and unleash lightning out of it. When you do so, you duplicate the ability that initially charged the weapon. If the ability was a spell, you are not considered to be casting a spell when you use this feature (and thus cannot be countered).

name: Oozebinder Soul
desc:
This shiny flourite orb is cool to the touch, but it seems to almost burn your skin. You must attune to the orb by holding it in both hands, upon which the orb melts into a liquid that absorbs into you. If you unattune, it reforms into an orb and drops to the ground besides you.

While attuned to this artifact, you have resistance to acid damage.

**Conjure Fen.** As an action, you can target a point within 30 ft. of you that you can see and cause a bubbling mire to spill out of the ground in up to a 20-ft radius circle. This mire lasts for 1 hour and is difficult terrain. If a Large or smaller creature ends its turn fully within the mire, it sinks up to its waist and has its speed reduced to zero. A creature can use its action to extract itself from the mire.

You are unaffected by the mires. While you are standing in one, you can sink into the ooze and emerge from a different mire that you’ve created. The duration it takes to move depends on how far away the two mires are:
- Within 600 ft. –1 bonus action
- Within 1 mile – 1 action
- Within 10 miles – 1 minute

You can have up to 3 mires established at one time. If you create a fourth, the oldest one dries up and disappears.

**Ooze-gift.** As an action, you can touch one creature and grant it a blessing which lasts until you use this feature again. A creature blessed in this way is unaffected by your mires, and it can travel between them in the same manner as yourself.

Once per long rest, you can use an action to grant this blessing to all creatures of your choice within 15 feet of you. When granted in this way, the blessing lasts for 10 minutes.

**Jury-rigging.** Sejun has modified this artifact to prevent its own violent self-destruction, but Forgemaster Hilina must re-power Thunderhead Keep before Pelu's tampering can be fully fixed. In the meantime, this artifact seems to have regained some of its former strength.

name: Revenant's Tidebreaker
desc:
This ancient hammer was found floating in the Astral Sea. It’s made of stone cool to the touch, and runic inscriptions along its faces reveal it once belonged to a legendary dwarven king from ages ago.

You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon, and it deals an additional 1d6 cold damage on a hit.

**Channeled Might.** While you’re attuned to this weapon, you take on the strength of ancient dwarves – your grow stronger and sturdier, and your beard, if you have one, grows fuller. You gain the following benefits:
- Your Strength score becomes 23.
- You gain resistance to poison damage, and you have advantage on saves against poison.
- Your maximum hit points increases by 1 point for every two levels you have, rounded down.

**Absolution.** As an action, you can summon forth a large spectral hammer at a location up to 60 ft. away that you can see, where it slams the ground in a burst of cold mist. Each creature in a 20-ft. radius sphere must make a DC 19 Strength saving throw, taking 4d6 bludgeoning and 4d6 cold damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. On a failure, a creature is also knocked prone.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all uses on a long rest.

**Jury-rigging.** Sejun has modified this artifact to prevent its own violent self-destruction, but Forgemaster Hilina must re-power Thunderhead Keep before Pelu's tampering can be fully fixed. In the meantime, this artifact seems to have regained some of its former strength.

name: Volcanic Soul (Infused)
desc:
This fiery carnelian orb is almost painfully hot to the touch. You must attune to the orb by holding it in both hands, upon which the orb melts into a liquid that absorbs into you. If you unattune, it reforms into an orb and drops to the ground besides you.

**Gift of Magma.** While you are attuned to the orb, your body becomes quite warm to the touch. You gain the following benefits:
- You have resistance to fire damage.
- You are unaffected by damage caused by environmental fire.
- You can move through magma and lava as though you were swimming, and you take no damage from being submerged within.
- You are unaffected by the detrimental effects of hot and cold weather extremes.

**Volcanic Form.** As a bonus action, you can undergo an elemental transformation. Your skin becomes cracked with rock and veins of red-hot magma, and your eyes glow with an intense orange light. The transformation lasts for 10 minutes, or until you use a bonus action to end it early.

While transformed in this way, you gain the following additional benefits:
- Your melee weapon attacks and unarmed strikes deal an additional 1d8 fire damage on a hit.
- You gain immunity to fire damage.
- Any creature that starts its turn within 10 ft. of you or moves there for the first time on a turn takes 1d8 fire damage.
- You may choose to become Large size.

You can use this feature twice per long rest.

**Thermal Power.** Whenever you would take fire damage while transformed, you can use your reaction to convert some of that energy for your own use. You regain hit points equal to the fire damage you would have taken, and you regain 1 ki point. Additionally, your speed increases by 15 ft. until the end of your next turn.

Twice per transformation, you can use this reaction upon taking any type of damage except cold. Instead of regaining hit points this way, you reduce the damage taken by 2d6 + half of your level, rounded down (to a minimum of 0).

**Jury-rigging.** Sejun has modified this artifact to prevent its own violent self-destruction, but Forgemaster Hilina must re-power Thunderhead Keep before Pelu's tampering can be fully fixed. In the meantime, this artifact seems to have regained some of its former strength.

name: Watchful Wind
desc:
A small gift of insurance from the forge-tenders of Thunderhead Keep.

If you would fall, you can use your reaction to conjure a small gust of wind that keeps you on your feet. Instead of falling, you can instead fly up to 30 ft. to an unoccupied location without provoking opportunity attacks. You cannot end this movement in the air unless you have another means of keeping yourself aloft.

Once you use this reaction, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

name: Thunderhead Keep Brooch
desc:
This silver brooch is crafted with a signature Djinni design, and bears the symbol of Thunderhead Keep. A sapphire gem sits in its center.

The gem bears the goodwill of Djinnkind. By wearing the brooch, you have advantage on Charisma checks made against other Djinn.

name: Madman's Knowledge
desc:
*A fragment of the soul of a man turned mad by the influence of the Aboleths.*

A fragment of the Headmaster's soul was trapped within the strange dome of iron and glass and filled with Aboleth mucus that once housed his brain. For a limited time it remains bound to this plane, forced to answer questions.

Information can be retrieved from the Headmaster based on Arcana, History, and Religion checks.

*"Making contact with eldritch wisdom is a blessing, for even if it drives one mad, it allows one to serve a grander purpose, for posterity."*

name: Flux Capacitor
desc:
*This strange device filled and powered with the mucus of an Aboleth allows one to warp time and space.*

This device is worn on your back like a backpack.

Immediately after rolling initiative you can swap positions in initiative with a willing creature. This property recharges after a long rest.

name: Abyssal Breastplate
desc:
While wearing this armor you gain the following benefits:
- Damage that you take is reduced by 2. This damage cannot be reduced below 0, is applied before other reductions and resistances, and does not stack with any other features that subtract damage from attacks in this manner.
- On your turn as a free action you can choose to sink up to 500 feet straight down through a body of water as a free action.
- You feel comfortably dry despite any amount of water on your skin and soaked into your clothes.

**Coral Carapace:** When you take damage you can use your reaction to gain resistance to the damage type. This resistance lasts until the start of your next turn. Once used, this feature recharges after a long rest.

**Depth's Embrace:** When a creature within 20 feet of you that you can see takes damage from an attack you can use your reaction to wrap a tentacle from your back around them, transferring the damage to you instead. This feature doesn't transfer any other effects that might accompany the damage, and this damage can't be reduced in any way. Once used, this feature recharges on a long rest.

**Abyssal Grasp:** As an action you can channel the blessing of the Aboleths through this eldritch breastplate. Two tentacles sprout from your back for 1 minute, or until you lose concentration as if you were concentrating on a spell. As part of the action to activate this and as a bonus action on your subsequent turns, you can use *'Clenched Fist', 'Forceful Hand', 'Grasping Hand',* or *'Interposing Hand'*. The tentacles are identical to Bigby's Hand with the following differences:
- They are sized Medium, occupy your space, and move with you.
- The reach of *‘Clenched Fist’, ‘Forceful Hand’,* and *‘Grasping Hand’* is increased to 15 feet.
- Your spellcasting ability modifier for the purpose of the spell is your highest ability modifier.
- *‘Interposing Hand’* can only be used on yourself, granting yourself half cover from one target. 
- Up to two creatures can be actively grappled by *'Grasping Hand'*, one in each of the tentacles.
- At least one tentacle must not be grappling a creature to use *'Interposing Hand', 'Forceful Hand',* and *'Clenched Fist'*.

Once used, this feature recharges after a long rest.

name: Cloak of the Imposter
desc:
**Corvid Wings**: Jet black avian wings manifest between your shoulder blades when you reach the apex of a jump, flapping once powerfully before fading away. Allows the wearer to jump twice as high or twice as far.

**Feather Fall**: As a reaction jet black avian wings manifest between your shoulder blades, granting the wearer the effect of the spell Feather Fall. This feature recharges after a long rest.

name: Ruby's Enchanted Spellbook
desc:
When found, the spellbook contains the following spells: 
- 1st level: Feather Fall
- 2nd level: Ruby's Mind Whip
- 3rd level: Enemies Abound, Major Image, Sleet Storm
- 4th level: Confusion, Hallucinatory Terrain
- 5th level: Animate Objects, Geas, Modify Memory

While you are attuned to this spellbook, roll a 1d2 when you take damage that would reduce you to 0 hit points. On a 1 you are reduced to 0 hit points with 1 successful death save. On a 2 you are instead reduced to 1 hit point. This feature recharges after a long rest.

In addition, whenever you roll a Hit Die to regain hit points, double the number of hit points it restores.

While attuned to this spellbook you can reroll one attack roll, ability check, or saving throw. Once you have used this item in this way, you cannot do so again until finishing a long rest.

name: Flying Colors
desc: Upon landing a killing blow Yasht can choose to have a ghostly image of her crew's colors billow in the breeze above her head for several seconds.

name: Key of Knowledge
desc:
This magical key is one of several made by an order of historians and archivists, the Scholars. While holding the key, you can use an action to twist the key, as if you were unlocking a door. When you do, a magical, spectral door slowly appears in front of you over the course of 1 minute. You and any creature you designate when you cast the spell can enter the extradimensional dwelling as long as the portal remains open. You can open or close the portal if you are within 30 feet of it. While closed, the portal is invisible.


Beyond the portal is a magnificient library that magically includes copies of books, scrolls, maps, and more. Its contents are strangely organized and seemingly change each time the key is used. A creature can visit the library and attempt to uncover a piece of knowledge, such as ancient poetry, the history of a forgotten kingdom, or the family lineage of a tyrannical king. The GM determines the checks and DCs needed to locate a given piece of information. For example, a book of foreign nursery rhymes may be easy to find, but a copy of necromantic research documents would be extremely difficult, if not impossible.

A staff of 10 near-transparent servants attends all who enter. You decide the visual appearance of these servants and their attire. They are completely obedient to your orders. The servants can fetch things, clean, mend, fold clothes, light fires, serve food, pour wine, and so on.

After ten minutes or an attempt to retrieve a piece of information is made and resolved the spell ends, ejecting the occupants to the nearest available squares.
 

When the spell ends, the key can't be used this way again for 24 hours.

name: Age of Aboleths
desc:
*You have been touched by something ancient. Can you ever go back, or is there only forward?*

- You understand and can speak Deep Speech.
- You still share a deep hatred for the Gods, but can now control your actions. The mere mention (by anyone) or thought of a God's name is enough to make your blood boil.
- You can see through illusory disguises of the Aboleth's minions, and you can see them on the Ethereal Plane while you are on the Material Plane.
- You can breathe underwater, for you have subtle gills and excrete non-transformative Aboleth mucus now.
- You have a swimspeed equal to your walking speed because of hidden fins on your forearms and ankles.
- When you take psychic damage you take 50% more damage.
- **Ancient Knowledge**: Once per long rest you may roll an additional 1d4 when making an arcana, history, nature, or religion check.
- You are far on your path to ~~enslavement~~ enlightenment.

name: Far Traveler's Mantle of Inspiration
desc:
Your Mantle of Inspiration feature instead grants 13 temporary hit points. At 15th level this increases to 16 temporary hit points.

When you use Mantle of Inspiration feature you can choose to replace its normal effect with one of the following:
- Restore 3 of each creature's hit points. At 15th level this increases to 10 hit points.
- Grant each creature 5 temporary hit points. When a creature gains these temporary hit points, it can immediately use its reaction to move up to its half speed. As part of this reaction the creature can make one attack or cast a ranged spell attack cantrip.
This replacement feature has 2 uses and both recharge after a long rest.

This cloak has 10 charges. While wearing it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of the cloak's charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell save DC: banishment (4 charges), blink (3 charges), misty step (2 charges), passwall (5 charges), or teleport (7 charges).

The cloak regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the cloak vanishes forever.

If brought to The Duke this item can be dismanteled, turning into a Voyager's Staff. This cannot be undone.

name: Rimescale Ring
desc:
While wearing this ring you gain the following benefits:
- The Armor of Agathys spell is added to your list of known spells.
- When casting Chaos Bolt against a target marked by **Ocean's Chill** you have advantage on the attack roll. If you have advantage on the roll from your **Tides of Chaos** feature, you may roll three dice instead of two.
- When casting Chaos Bolt you can choose to reroll one of the damage dice. You must keep the new result.
- When casting Chaos Bolt, if the first two d8s are 2 (cold), the first original target takes another 4d8 cold damage.

**Ocean's Chill**: This ring has 3 charges and regains all expended charges after a long rest. You can expend 1 charge as a bonus action to mark a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you for 1 hour. The formula for your spell attack rolls becomes **proficiency bonus + charisma modifier + 2** and you gain a +2 bonus to spell damage for those attacks when you attack a marked creature. In addition, you can choose to change the spell's damage type to cold or necrotic.

You perceive a marked creature within 60 feet of you as though you had truesight, and can see through any illusion that stands between you and the marked creature.

name: Flasklock Repeater
meta:
*Weapon, Very Rare*

**Attunement**
Requires attunement by a Warlock. 

 *Weapon: Special, Ranged* 
 3 lbs. --- 1d10 force - range (120/240)
desc:
This weapon has the following benefits:
- You can cast Eldritch Blast and Chaos bolt through this weapon as it serves as a spell focus.
- Any features or items that affect Eldritch Blast affect this weapon's attacks. 
- Once per turn when you cast Eldritch Blast through this weapon you can fire this weapon as a free action.
- Casting Chaos Bolt through this weapon adds another 1d6 damage of the type rolled.

**Flasklock and Load:** After finishing a long rest you can craft 3 individual magiflasks that serve as special ammunition for this weapon, each containing a spell cast at the 3rd level. Crafting this ammunition does not consume spell slots. The following spells can be cast once through the repeater as a bonus action spell following the normal casting rules:
- Chaos Bolt
- Armor of Agathys
- Aganazzar's Scorcher.

name: Tendril of the Deep
desc:
While wielding this whip you gain the following benefits:
- You can perform object interactions within reason at a range of 15 feet.
- If you fall and there is something within 15 feet that the whip could reasonably latch onto and support your weight as a reaction you can attempt a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw to save yourself from falling. On a success you instead hang off the object. On a failure you fall as normal.

name: Rusted Coin of Luck
desc:
Whenever you make an attack roll you can use this coin to roll an additional d20. You choose which of the d20s is used for the attack roll.

This coin crumbles to dust after being used once.

name: Ruby Tipped Spear
meta:
*Weapon: Simple, Melee*
3 lbs. --- 1d6 piercing - thrown (20/60), versatile (1d8)
desc: This **non-magical** spear is tipped with a small sharpened and hardened ruby. Your attacks made with this weapon do a bonus 1 fire damage.

name: Needle of Mending
desc:
As an action, you can touch the needle to any object made of cloth, leather, or similar material, and repair any damage to it. This includes holes, tears, snags, and frayed edges. The object is restored to its original condition as if it had never been damaged.

The needle cannot repair objects made of metal or other non-cloth materials. It also cannot repair magical items.

name: Crystal of Truth
desc:
While holding the crystal, you can use an action to ask it to reveal whether a creature within 60 feet of you is telling the truth. The crystal glows with a bright light if the creature is being truthful, and a dim light if it is not.

The crystal does not reveal the truth itself, or the content of any hidden information. It only allows the user to detect when someone is being deceptive.

Once used, it cannot be used again until a new dawn.

name: Force Rune
desc: Once applied to a weapon, this rune grants the weapon the ability to produce a blast of concussive force during an attack when the command word is spoken, dealing an extra 1d6 force damage to the target and any creatures within 5 feet of it, excluding the wielder. This ability can be used once per day.

name: Runic Spell Ring
desc: While wearing this runic ring, you gain an additional 1 damage per turn when using a spell that does not use a weapon as part of its attack.

name: Grimm's Gilded Gift
desc: While living as a Dhamphir, Kosha retains the bonus damage ability of Orcish Fury.

name: Evergarlic Mask
desc:
*This curious mask is fashioned of skull like that which Lapetus appears to wear.*

This mask prevents the wearer from smelling anything but garlic.

Creatures within 5 feet of you can also smell the garlic.

Donning and doffing this mask takes a full action.

name: Cull
meta:
*Sickle, Uncommon*

 *Weapon: Simple, Melee* 
 2 lbs. --- 1d4 necrotic - finesse, light
desc:
*This sickle appears to be crafted with a bone handle and a sharpened Aarakocra skull as the blade.*

You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magical weapon.

name: Winds of War
desc:
*A small set of windchimes fashioned out of bones, painted black. When an attacker takes you in their sight, an unseen wind catches the chimes, alerting you...*

While hung around your neck you can choose to make an initiative check with advantage. You are also immune to the surprise condition when this is activated. This feature recharges after a long rest.

name: Druid Arts and Crafts
desc:
*A small wooden box that can be easily affixed to one's belt containing several paint brushes, an assortment of natural paint hues, and various seeds.*

While this item is on your person the instantaneous, harmless sensory effects created by the spell Druidcraft can be extended to last up to ten seconds and fit within a 10-foot cube.

In addition, even large campfires can be instantly lit or snuffed out.

After the next dawn the exhausted supply of paints and seeds will have been found to have refilled itself upon opening the box.

name: Seed a Barren Corpse
desc:
This neclace has 5 seed-like beads hanging from it. You can use an Action to detach a bead and throw it up to 20 feet away at a dead creature. 

Upon landing the creature will become under the effects of the Gentle Repose spell, following all associated rules.

The beads are inert if the necklace has not been worn for at least ten minutes.

name: Weapon Charm - Firefly Lantern
desc: This tiny lantern can be attached to the grip or pommel of a weapon. On command, it casts Druidcraft.

name: Grimm Co. Auto-Miner
desc:
*An enchanted mining pick used by the Grimm Co. Mining Company employers. There is a vial full of a crimson liquid in the handle. A Pint of humanoid blood.*

Activating this tool as an action causes it to float from your hand to a surface of your choice made of wood, plaster, or stone and dig through it over 1 minute. You choose the opening's dimensions: up to 5 feet wide, 8 feet tall, and 10 feet deep. The passage creates no instability in a structure surrounding it.

Using the tool in this manner consumes a fourth of the vial of liquid. When the vial is empty it must be filled in order for the mining pick to be used again.

name: Grimm Co. Multitool
meta:
*Warhammer, Uncommon*

*Weapon: Martial, Melee*
2 lbs. --- 1d8 slashing - versatile (1d10), finesse, thrown (20/40)
desc:
You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magical weapon.

As an action you can replicate the effects of the **Mold Earth** cantrip with this shovel limited to a range of 5 feet. You also cannot create colors with it.

As an action you can twist off the shaft, revealing a **+ 1 Dagger** inside the shaf, attached to the handle. As an action you can re-assemble the shovel.

As an action you can twist off the shaft's handle, revealing a **whistle** inside. As an action you can re-assemble the shovel.

name: Excagator
desc:
*A magical construct that bears a striking resemblence to an alligator.*

The Excagator can dig tunnels large enough for medium or smaller creatures to crawl through. It digs at a rate of 5 feet per minute. If the Excagator is more than 100 feet away from you it stops moving. After 24 hours has passed the tunnels collapse harmlessly.

If you are attuned to this item this construct can be activated for combat. The Excagator takes its turn immediately after yours. It uses the Giant Crocodile's stat block with the following changes:
- It has 45 hit points.
- Its size is medium.
- It does not have the multiattack feature and its attacks suffer a -10 penalty to damage rolls, dealing a minimum of 1 damage.
- It cannot do anything but move on its turn unless you use your bonus action to command it.

The Excagator cannot be healed by magic or other means. It regenerates 20 hit points per long rest.

If the Excagator is reduced to 0 hit points it deactivates. After being reduced to 0 hit points the Excagator cannot be used in combat again until it has regenerated 45 hit points.

name: Banner of Blood
meta:
*Wondrous Item, Very Rare*

**Attunement**
Requires attunement by a Samurai
desc:
This small patch of fabric is embroidered with a glistening crimson fleur de lis.

You can take an action to press the patch against a two-handed weapon you are holding, fusing the two together. Thereafter the patch becomes a war banner, hanging from your weapon. Spectral blood drips from it hungrily.

Alternatively, you can take an action to press the patch against your armor, fusing the two together. Thereafter the patch becomes a sashimono, displayed and hanging from a pole on your back.

As an action you can change between the two methods of display.

While attuned and equipped to a **weapon**, you gain the following benefits:
- When you make an attack, you can choose to use your **Daunting Roar** feature as a free action, targetting any enemy that can see you and your war banner as it strikes fear into their hearts.
- All of your attacks made against the target of your **Challenge** have advantage, and if you use your **Fighting Spirit** feature and attack them you can instead roll 3 d20s and take the highest. 
- Any successful attack made against the target of your **Challenge** heal you for 2 hit points.

While attuned and equipped to your armor, you gain the following benefits:
- You and your allies within 60 feet who can see your sashimono gain a bonus 5 feet to their walking speed.
- While moving towards the target of your **Challenge** your walking speed is doubled.
- Allies within 15 feet of you that can see the sashimono can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to a saving throw once per turn. This bonus increases to 2d4 for saving throws against being frightened.
- When you use your **Fighting Spirit** feature, you can choose to grant allies within 15 feet of you 10 temporary hit points as well. If they already have 10 or more temporary hit points or are dying, they are instead healed for an amount equal to the temporary hit points you gained. This feature recharges after a short rest.

**Challenge**: As a bonus action you can challenge a foe to combat. Your attacks made against the target of this challenge deal bonus damage equal to half your proficiency bonus rounded up. While the challenge is active, you have a -2 penalty to your AC, except against attacks made by the target of this challenge. You can end the challenge at any time or when one of you die. When the target of your challenge dies you regain one use of your **Fighting Spirit** feature. This feature recharges after a long rest.

name: Necklace of Rubies
desc:
*Eight dazzling rubies hang from a golden chain. Lightning dances across their structure just beneath the surface.*

This necklace has 8 rubies hanging from it. You can use an Action to detach a ruby and throw it up to 60 feet away. When it reaches the end of its trajectory, the bead detonates as a 3rd-level Shatter spell (save DC 15).

name: Winds of War (Damaged)
desc:
*A small set of windchimes fashioned out of bones, painted black. When an attacker takes you in their sight, an unseen wind catches the chimes, alerting you...*

While hung around your neck you can choose to make an initiative check with advantage. You are also immune to the surprise condition when this is activated. This feature recharges after a short rest.

name: Potion of Blood
desc:
*Curiously, this vial seems to fit the **Grimm Co. Auto-Miner** perfectly...*

As a bonus action this potion can be drank, healing the drinker for 2d4 + 4 hit points. As an action it can be administered to a willing creature instead. It is already filled.

This potion can be refilled as an action by pressing it against the flesh of someone willing or incapacitated. It reduces the hit point maximum of the target by 2d4 + 4 as it quickly draws a pint of blood until the next long rest.

Once the potion has been consumed, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

name: Bag Stone
desc:
A stone. You can connect it to other sending stones for a fee.

If you are attuned to it while holding the stone you can activate it with a command word and send a verbal message that plays aloud from a connected stone on the same plane you specify.

While attuned to the stone you can also speak the command word to activate it hand it to a willing person who can then speak into the stone, sending a verbal message that plays aloud to a stone you specify.

name: Chicken Pot
desc:
As an action you can reach your hand into this small iron cauldron and withdraw a single live chicken, expending a charge in the process.

The pot has 1000 charges, after the last charge is spent, it loses its magic.

name: Keep Sack
desc:
This rucksack has the size and appearance of a normal backpack except for a large arcane symbol pressed
into the leather. Perishable foods, remains, or objects placed inside are preserved and preventing from spoiling, rotting, or molding. It has no effect on living creatures.

name: Babel Amulet
desc:
This small gold amulet appears to be a small inverted tower, hanging from its bottom by a gold chain. 

 As an action you can speak expend a charge and touch the amulet to a willing creature. An affected creature gains the ability to speak and understand one language of your choice for 1 hour.

Once used, it cannot be used again until a new dawn.

name: Babble Amulet
desc:
As an action you can speak the amulet’s command word to expend a charge and direct a blast of psychic energy towards a creature you can see within 60 feet. The target must succeed a DC15 Charisma saving throw or feel their personal inhibitions completely sheared away.

For one minute the target cannot help but speak continuously, boisterously narrating every thought that comes to mind. The affected creature cannot benefit from being hidden, it automatically fails any deception checks, and cannot perform the verbal components of spells.

Once used, this amulet cannot be used again until a new dawn.

name: As Long As I can't See You ...
desc:
While wearing this ring, you can use an action to turn invisible. The invisibility is held as long as the wearer keeps their eyes closed to a maximum of two hours.

Once the invisibility has been broken, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.

name: Narcissus' Mirror
desc:
Once per day when you look in the mirror your reflection will inspire you by praising your looks and qualities, giving you advantage on your next skill check.

After the fifth time of using the mirror, and every time thereafter, you must succeed a DC 10 Charisma saving throw or spend a minute enthralled listening to your reflection.

name: Deck of  Creatures
desc:
This deck of cards has several depictions of animals. As an action you can summon the creature it depicts. The creature is friendly to you and follows your commands. After four hours, the creature disappears.

There are the following creatures within it: Riding Horse, Cave Bear, Direwolf, Giant Bat, Cockatrice, Boar, Giant Boar, Gargoyle, Animated Armor, Griffon, Ogre, Pegasus, Tiger, Lion, Imp, Hippogriff, Harpy,  Shark, Giant Vulture, Giant Spider, Giant Eagle, Crocodile, Tressym, Basilisk, Killer Whale, Hell Hound, Owlbear.

Once a creature has been summoned, the page loses its magic. Only one creature can be summoned at one time.

name: Peephole Ring
desc:
This small ring has the image of an eye engraved on it. When pressed against a surface that is less than five feet thick, it allows the wearer to see at the other side of the surface as if there were a peephole.

Once this ring has used twice in a day, it cannot be used until a new dawn.

name: Gloom and Bloom
desc:
Gloom - Whenever you strike a creature with this piece of ammunition, it takes an additional 2d4 necrotic damage and its tip embeds inside the target.

Bloom - Whenever you strike a creature with this piece of ammunition, the weapons base damage instead heals the target. Additionally, they heal another 2d4 hit points, and the tip embeds inside the target.

While Gloom and Bloom are inside a target each, the target which has Gloom takes an 2d4 necrotic damage  at the start of their turn. Additionally, the target that has Bloom within them heals that same amount. 

A creature can use its action to cut or take the tip of the arrow from its body.

The two pieces of ammunition reform on your quiver the next dawn after used.

name: Staff of Chronal Defense
desc: Staff creates pockets of slower time

name: Sword of Middle Measures
desc:
This magical longsword has a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls and the finesse property.

On your turn, you can decide to give up an attack and instead take a defensive posture. Until the start of your next turn, you gain +2 AC as long as no attack of opportunity is taken.

Any critical failure (1) or critical hit (20) rolled with this weapon are instead re rolled, the new result must be used instead.

name: Undying Tome
desc: Reading the inscriptions in this tome allows you to remove dye from objects. Each color has its own specific page and inscription.

name: Starglass
desc:
While gazing through this spyglass you can see the stars regardless of the time or weather conditions.

Additionally, you gain a  +5 bonus to perception checks made to perceive something more than a mile away while looking through this spyglass.

name: Kinetic Cloak
desc: Whenever you take damage, you gain an additional ten feet of movement speed during your next turn.

name: Lightning Bottle
desc: This bottle holds a lighting bolt inside. As an action, you can destroy the bottle on the ground, creating the same effect as if you had cast the *lightning bolt* spell.

name: Artificer's Gloves
desc:
These mismatched gloves are often used to cool or melt important pieces in the construction process.

As an action, you can set a flammable surface the size of your hand on fire.

As an action, you can freeze a liquid or part of a liquid you touch up to the surface size of your hand.


Once a day, you can clap your hands and create a 20ft radius cloud of fog and smoke. The cloud lasts for one minute and the area inside it is considered to be heavily obscured.

Additionally, your unarmed attacks made with your fists gain an additional 1 cold or fire damage depending on which glove you are using for your attack.

name: Bell of Silence
desc: Once a day, when you ring this silent bell, you are able to cast the *silence* spell. Additionally, you can center the spell on yourself, which would move with you for the duration.

name: Chain Sword
desc:
Its hilt is an ornate piece made of different twisting silver chains. This magical sword has a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

Additionally, once per day, you can activate its chains whenever you hit with an attack. The target must succeed a DC 15 dexterity check or be restrained by the chains for one minute. The restrained target can contest the restraint at the end of each of their turns, ending the effect on a success.

name: Ring of Glass Cutting
desc:
While wearing this ring, you can use an action to use its tip and cut through glass. Additionally, as a bonus action, you can use its tip to shatter any glass window.

Once per day, you can use its magic to turn the glass being shattered into the sand instead.

The ring cannot cut or shatter glass that is more than 3 ft thick.

name: Spiked Boots
desc:
These boots have a small enclosed trap on their heel. As a bonus action, you can activate its trap as you walk, leaving behind 5 feet  area of caltrops behind you. You can use this twice, once for each foot, before you need to reset the caltrops into the boots.

It takes an action to set up the heel traps on your boots once again.

name: Honingbirds
desc:
As an action, you can command up to three of these mechanical hummingbirds to hone onto up to three targets you can see within 120 ft of you.

The mechanical hummingbirds fly to their target before exploding near them. Every creature within 10 ft of the explosion must make a Dexterity saving throw 14. On a failure, the creature takes 2d4 force damage and 2d4 fire damage. On a success the creature takes half the damage.

The honingbid is destroyed during the explosion.

name: Boots of Gravitational Concentration
desc: As long as you are concentrating on a spell, these boots allow you to walk on vertical and upside-down surfaces.

name: Glass Cannon
desc:
This magical crossbow is a defective prototype of a more refined magical self-loading, repeating crossbow. The magical weapon has a  +1 bonus to damage rolls.

Once per day, you can use an action to fire three bolts from it. Afterward, the magic on the weapon falters and it loses its self-loading and repeating properties until a new dawn has passed.

name: Big Boi Sippy Cup of Milk
desc: Maximized Greater Healing Potion

name: Wondrous Cheese
desc: Potion of Giant Strenght with maximized duration.

name: Special Butter
desc: When eating this butter your hit point maximum and current hit points increase by 5 for a period of 24 hours.

name: An Actual Bomb
desc: This small, unstable circular contraption of gears is activated when struck by a spell. On impact, it explodes in a 20ft radius. Every creature must make a DC 16 Dexterity check. On a failure, a creature caught on the blast takes 4d4 lightning, fire, and force damage and falls prone from the blast. On success, the creature takes half damage and does not fall prone.

name: Marvelous Pleasant Yogurt
desc: Eating this Yogurt grants you resistance to acid damage for four hours.

name: Sippy Cup of Milk
desc: Maximized healing Potion

name: Sending Stone
desc:
This stone has no notable properties by itself. For a small fee, you can magically pair it to additional stones, adding them to its list of connections. You may disconnect a stone from the list for free.

While holding one stone, you can activate it by speaking its command word and specifying another stone from its list of connections. When you do so, you can speak a short verbal message into the stone, which plays aloud from the destination stone as long as it is in a creature's possession and both stones are on the same plane of existence. Attunement is not required to hear the message, although it is required in order to respond.

When you activate the stone, you can choose to designate another creature and give them the stone. That creature may give the verbal message in your stead.

name: Amulet of Whispers
desc:
This amulet has a hold on you psychically. If you don’t have it on your person, you begin to feel sick, anxious. Worried that someone will take it while you’re away from it.

You spend **1d4 hours** each night looking at the amulet, polishing it, making sure it stays as pretty as the day you found it. During this time, you believe you hear a voice coming from the amulet, asking you to listen to it. Most of the words are incomprehensible, too quiet to hear properly. When you take the time to listen, the voice is silent. No one but you can hear this voice.

If attuned, you gain a +2 to intimidation checks, and can cast the *Cause Fear* spell. The spell can only be cast once per long rest.

name: Ice Whetstone
desc:
As a bonus action, you can use this whetstone to add +2 cold damage to a weapon's damage for one minute.

Once used the whetstone needs a day to recharge its magic.

name: Glove of Healing Touch
desc: Once per day, while wearing this glove, when you cast a healing spell with the touch range, you can reroll the dice. You must use the new roll.

name: Pants of the Liar
desc:
While wearing these pants when you make a Deception check you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the check. Once you use this property it can't be used again until after a long rest. 

If you roll a natural 1 on the Deception check while wearing these pants they burst into flames, dealing 1d3 fire damage to you every 6 seconds to you until you use an action to put them out. The pants are unharmed by this fire.

name: Ring of Rings
desc:
Only a humanoid can wear this collar due to its shape.

When your hit points are reduced to 0 while wearing this collar, illusory golden rings spill out of your clothing and armor. Every creature within 5 feet of you that can see the rings must make a DC 10 Wisdom saving throw or be under the effect of the **Incite Greed** spell. 

In addition to the normal conditions for ending the effect of the spell, the effect ends on all targets if you regain at least 1 hit point. If you regain at least 1 hit point or the effect ends on all creatures, the rings vanish.

This feature cannot be used again until after finishing a long rest.

name: Penny Pincher
desc:
When you take damage from an enemy attack, spell, or feature that reduces your hit points to 0, any excess damage is turned into gold coins inside this pouch at a rate of 1 gold coin per hit point.

This does not work in spars.

name: Conspirator's Cloak
desc:
This magic longcoat is adorned with crimson owl feathers and carved jet buttons. While wearing this coat, you have a +2 bonus to Charisma (Deception) checks. In addition, the coat has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dusk. While wearing the coat, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 of its charges to disappear, releasing up to three owls from your location under your control that scatter into the air. Each owl has AC 8, 1 hit point, and can fly up to 40 feet as part of this bonus action without provoking opportunity attacks. At the start of your next turn, the owls vanish, and you reappear in the space of one of the owls (your choice).

If all the owls are reduced to 0 hit points, you reappear early in the same space as the last one that died. When you do, any excess damage dealt to the owls carries over to your normal form.

name: Spider Laser
desc:
*The head of one of the spiderlike magical constructs (identified by the inventory as 'SP1-D3R's) jerry-rigged to the back of a heavy leather glove.*

You cannot use a weapon, focus, reagant, or anything else with the hand in this glove, but you can manipulate and grasp objects and the environment with it.

This item allows you to cast the **Shocking Grasp** cantrip as normal but with a range of 30 feet.

name: Staff of Frostbite
desc:
*A once powerful staff, now broken it offers little more than to serve as an arcane focus.*

When casting a leveled spell with this staff as a focus you may choose to spend a charge to roll one additional dice of damage. Doing so causes you to take 1d3 cold damage per spell level. If the spell deals damage in an area of effect the number of dice for the damage you take is doubled. This damage taken cannot be reduced in any way.

This staff has 3 charges and all charges recharge after a long rest.

When a charge is used to enhance a spell higher than 3rd level, roll a d100. If you roll a 5 or lower, the staff is destroyed after the successful cast of the spell. You then take an additional 6d3 cold damage.

name: Pertunt's Enhanced Arm: A Slice of Ginger Original
desc:
This new mechanical arm offers some new enhancements, courtesy of the Artificer Slice Of Ginger.

This mechanical, lightweight arm can separate through the middle as a free action, revealing a strong metallic rope-like mechanism that joins the two parts. The metallic rope inside the arm joining the two parts can extend up to 40 ft. As a bonus action, you can recall the arm back together. 

As an action, you can choose a ledge or protruding spot within 40 ft of you and aim your hand at it. The end of your arm then can shoot out as a projectile that latches onto the spot. The hand can support up to three hundred pounds and the rope can be used to facilitate climbing or transport objects or creatures. 

Additionally, once per short rest, by tugging with the rest of your body, a reeling mechanism will pull you or your hand to where the other half is located, joining both parts. 

As a reaction to falling, you can shoot out your hand to a ledge or protruding spot within 30 ft of you. As long as the distance to the destination is smaller than the distance to the ground, the hand will prevent you from falling and holster you to the ledge. Once this mechanism has been used, the filaments used for this fast maneuver need to rest until the new dawn.

As an action, you can fire your hand at a creature within 40 ft of you. An unwilling creature must make a Dexterity Save DC 15 to avoid being latched by the fired hand. 

   - If the creature is small or smaller, you can attempt to pull it closer to you. An unwilling creature must make a DC 15 Strength (Athletics) check or be pulled toward you. 

- If the creature is medium or larger, you can pull yourself closer to the creature.

Once this property has been used, it cannot be used again until you have finished a short rest.

If you attune to this magical arm, you can use it as a spellcasting focus. 

For the first year of use, the mechanical arm suffers from rarely-occurring minor issues that diminish as time goes on, including: Delay of sensory information, minor shocks, involuntary movement of the fingers, itchiness, overheating, and small episodes of tingling.

name: Gloves of the Ambi-Hextrous
desc: While wearing these gloves when you cast a cantrip with a range longer than 10 feet which targets a creature you can spend a charge to use a bonus action to cast it a second time on a different creature within range. These gloves have 3 charges and all charges recharge after a long rest.

name: Ruby Spyglass
desc:
As an action, objects viewed through a spyglass can be enlarged to twice their size as if affected by the **Enlarge/Reduce** spell. This does not use your concentration but the effect still lasts for 1 minute.

Enlarging an object uses 1 charge and this spyglass has 5 charges and all charges recharge after a long rest.

name: Solareth's Cord
meta: *Wondrous Item, rare (requires attunement by a cleric of Selûne or a cleric with the Twilight domain)*
desc:
This moonstone amulet hums faintly with a harmonic resonance only Silence can perceive.

***Moonlit Refrain***. As a bonus action, you can activate the amulet by pressing it to your chest and intoning Solareth's Chord. The amulet emits a soft, harmonic lunar resonance for **1 minute**, ending early if you become incapacitated or die.

**You can use this property a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.**

While the amulet is active, **dim light within 10 feet of you becomes soft moonlight**. This light is still considered dim light for the purposes of vision and abilities, but it counts as magical moonlight.

You and your allies who are within the light and can hear the amulet’s resonance gain the following benefits:
- A +1 bonus to all saving throws
- Resistance to psychic damage
A creature that is deafened, inside a silence effect, or otherwise unable to hear the amulet does not gain these benefits, even if it is within the light.

***Echo of the Home Chord***. While Moonlit Refrain is active, when a creature you can see within 30 feet of you that can hear the amulet fails a saving throw against being charmed or frightened, or against an illusion or enchantment spell or other magical effect, you can use your reaction to invoke the amulet’s echo.

That creature immediately rerolls the saving throw and must use the new result.

A creature can benefit from this feature only once per activation of Moonlit Refrain.

*You must let your DM know about this effect ahead of your session so that it can apply behind the DM screen!*

***Stonecunning***. Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to the origin of stonework, you are considered proficient in the History skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check, instead of your normal proficiency bonus.

name: Ring of Sanctified Resonance
meta:
*A ring that has weathered the storm of a village besieged by pain and death.*

**Wonderous Item, Rare (requires attunement by a Sorcerer or Paladin)**
desc:
A worn gold band with hand-carved inscriptions of love and care, once worn by the mother of Vincent Leftfoot. Given to her by her spouse on their wedding day, it would lay on the finger of the woman who owned it until the day of her death. It then would lay in the soot and dirt surrounding Briar Helm for decades, unmoved and unbroken by the anguish that rested in the restless.

**Properties:**
**Divine Arcanic Synergy**
When you expend sorcery points or a spell slot to deal radiant damage to an undead creature, you may choose one benefit:
Gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1)
or
Add +1 to the attack roll or save DC of that effect.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus and regain all uses when finishing a long rest.

**See Through the Veil**
You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) and Wisdom (Insight) checks made to detect deception, disguises or false appearances of undead creatures.

name: "Would You Love Me If I Was A Worm" Ring
meta:
Ring, Uncommon

This ring can only be used by a creature that has undergone the bonding effects of a Ceremony Spell. If the wearer has broken these bonds, the ring does nothing
desc:
This simple band of carefully sculpted silver is shaped like a segmented earthworm wrapping around your finger. It exudes a slight warmth and is mildly squishy.


**Properties**
As an action, you can transform into a Worm. This transformation follows the rules of the Polymorph spell, but you do not need to maintain concentration. You can remain in this form for up to 1 hour, or until you use a bonus action to return to your normal form. You revert to your normal form early if you drop to 0 hit points.

While you are in worm form, your partner (the individual with whom you fulfill the attunement requirement) always knows your exact location as long as you are on the same plane of existence.

If all parties of the relationship are wearing a "Would you love me if i was a worm" Ring, you can communicate telepathically

*"He said yes. Now he has to carry me in a damp matchbox through the dungeon until we get to the boss room."*

**Worm**
*Tiny Beast, Unaligned*
**Armor Class:** 10
**Hit Points:** 1 
**Speed:** 5 ft., burrow 5 ft.

STR 1 DEX 11 CON 7 INT 1 WIS 8 CHA 3

**Senses:** Blindsight 10ft, Tremorsense 10ft, Passive Perception 9
**Languages:** --
**Challenge:** 0

name: Maadrem Spores
desc:
You were infected by Maadrem spores in the distant domain of Yonder, and carry them with you still.
You receive the following mechanical changes.
* You gain a +1 bonus to Charisma (Performance) checks, as the loping dances of the Maadrem feel as familiar as breathing.
* Whenever you succeed on a saving throw or ability to check to perceive truth behind an illusion, you instead perceive something else strange and fantastical for 1 minute.
* Upon completing a long rest, you may find yourself with barklike patches of flaky skin or varicose veins tinted green. These effects fade over the course of the day.

The spores remain indefinitely unless removed with *Remove Curse*, *Greater Restoration*, or *Wish*.

name: Undertaker's Planchette
meta: Wondrous Item, Artifact (requires attunement by a suitable wielder)
desc: This Heirloom of providence is said to allow its wielder to summon and speak to a person from a specific point in history. Alive or dead. Wielders are rare and gifted individuals and must spend a great deal of time getting in tune with the heirloom to use it.

name: Growing Croc Scale
desc: This scale from a giant crocodile can be consumed by a humanoid creature to change their size to large and increase their reach by 5 ft for 1 minute. If the scale is consumed by a crocodile that creature permanently transforms into a giant crocodile.

name: Burrow Worm Egg
desc: The egg of a burrow worm. If kept warm will hatch into a baby burrow worm after 28 days. Baby burrow worms imprint on the first creature they touch and will be friendly to that creature. As long as it is fed, it will not be aggressive to other creatures either. Its fur smells fragrantly of apples and cinnamon and tends to fill any room they’re in and stick to the clothes of people who care for them.

name: Charm of Mastery
desc: When this charm is attached to a weapon over the length of at least a short rest, it allows the weapon’s wielder to use the mastery property of that weapon. The charm takes an action to remove from a weapon.

name: Dreadwood Legion Associate Mercenary Badge
desc: Allows the Deadwood to recognise the bearer and allow them swifter access to Dreadwood Legion outposts.
